Title: Great staff room and bathroom was mediocre
ReviewText:
The staff here were very friendly, professional, and courteous, which made for a welcoming check-in. The room itself was an adequate size and the beds were somewhat comfortable for the stay.

However, the bathroom really needs the most attention. There is no vanity storage space, there was visible mold in the caulk line, and the tub stopper was completely broken. Worst of all, the shower pressure was absolutely abysmal it felt like a weak drizzle of rain rather than an actual shower. Great service from the team, but the bathroom maintenance needs a major upgrade.

Title: Room was Freezing
ReviewText: The fact that customers could not control room temperature made our stay a little uncomfortable. We had to sleep in layers. When we asked if our room temperature was malfunctioning we were told they controlled it and could only be changed if they received enough complaints.

Title: There are roaches in this hotel
ReviewText: See the title there were roaches, the staff was helpful and kind. But there are roaches.

Title: Birthday
ReviewText: I spend one night at this hotel for my birthday.  I would like to go here again

Title: Amazing Hotel
ReviewText:
Courtyard by Marriott is an amazing hotel. Pleasant staff, beautiful clean rooms and great service is the recipe for a wonderful stay.  I love staying at the Marriott. Join the team. Book your next stay at Courtyard by Marriott. 

Maureen

Title: Friendly staff and small hotel feel
ReviewText: This hotel is located in a safe area of Mid-town and walking distance to many NYC restaurants and sites.  The staff is friendly and the hotel has a small, intimate feeling when you stay.  We have stayed here twice and will book again in the future.  Housekeeping and concierge staff are fantastic!  Special Thank you to Evelyn, Nancy and Pedro!

Title: Courtyard Marriott New York Manhattan/Fifth Avenue is the best!
ReviewText: I highly recommend this hotel for your next stay in New York City.  The hotel is conveniently located to many city attractions, as well as excellent restaurants and shopping.  The part is located across the street from Bryant Park and the New York City Public Library.  It is not very far from Central Park and many other attractions.  The area is very clean and we felt very comfortable there.  The hotel is clean and the staff is very helpful and friendly.  My family and I feel that it provided us great value for the price that we paid.

Title: Listing states queen beds but we got double beds
ReviewText: Everything with the stay went fine, except we were thinking we'd get a room with queen beds as advertised.

Title: Will stay again!
ReviewText: So clean, so friendly! Loved the 24 hour coffee and water. And it was so quiet

Title: Extremely Outdated
ReviewText: If you're looking for small overpriced rooms that scream 1987 this is your place. Carpet in room made me keep a walk around pair of socks next to my bed just to use restroom or walk around. The decor looked like it was hand-me-downs. Bathroom and toilet were even more outdated than the rest of the room. The toilet had that 80's yellowish color to it. Bed sheets look like they came balled up out of a plastic bag. Wall paper on walls looked like it was hanging on for dear life.

Title: Pleasantly Quiet & Easy for a Great Location
ReviewText: I was nervous about driving into the City for this stay, but getting to the hotel, and even parking around the corner, was relatively easy. It's also an easy walk from Grand Central. We loved being close to the library and Bryant Park. The hotel was pleasantly quiet considering how close it is to everything. Our room was simple and clean. Two fast elevators and an efficient front desk kept the lobby from being crowded. Hotel staff were incredibly helpful - very kind and patient. It was amazing to be able to leave our bags there securely until later in the day after we checked out.

Title: Badly managed Venue not to choose for business individuals
ReviewText: I took a red eye from California and requested for eqlry checkin so i could be at my business meetings ready and in time. Hotel faculty didn't honor the request and didnt bother to ask about how they can help solve for the needs. They said they will call as soon as a room would be available but never called. I ahd yo show up to ask for a room again. Disrupted my entire day and made it invain to lodge in downtown closer to my meeting venue.

Title: Awful hotel, look elsewhere.
ReviewText: This hotel is a huge step below any Marriott property I’ve stayed in across the US, Europe, Canada, Mexico, etc. Courtyard has always been my safe go-to for business travel, but there is no way I could ever stay here again. Super rude and unhelpful staff - like going out of their way to be rude to my employees who were asking normal hotel/travel related questions. Overall a baffling experience. Why do you work in hospitality if you hate people?

Title: QUIET & CLEAN!
ReviewText: Very quiet, very clean, staff was very attentive when I had a need!!

Title: Ok
ReviewText: Staff could have been friendlier

Title: Not a Good hotel
ReviewText:
The room was not very clean, and it was extremely hot. I reported the issue, but was told that since it’s April, the hotel is still running the heater. There was no real attempt to fix the problem, even though I was traveling with my kids.
Very disappointing experience. I would not stay there again and would not recommend this hotel. It felt like they didn’t care about their guests.

Title: A fine hotel.
ReviewText:
My 15 year old son and I spent a few days in Manhattan for spring break. This hotel worked out well for us: we each had our own bed, we had a nice view of the city skyline, we were close to food and Bryant Park, and it was pretty clean. I like that the TV had a menu where you could sign into Netflix or HBO, etc. 
There was a lot of construction noise on the street below, which is not the hotel's fault. We slept fine, despite the noise. The beds were firm.

Title: March 2025
ReviewText: Overall, location was fine and close to meetings I needed to attend in the area. Room was dated and carpet was really dirty and just made the space feel dirty.

Title: Masquerade
ReviewText: Spent the night in NYC to attend Masquerade and get in a few touristy things….  Was able to arrange car service to the airport, in a GREAT location for tourist attractions, and genuinely welcomed by everyone at the Marriott New Yort Manhattan/Fifth Avenue!

Title: Despite friendly staff, hotel needs major remodeling
ReviewText: Very outdated hotel, small rooms and requires major remodeling. The gym can only fit 3 people! Unreal

Title: Great spot close to NY public library and Bryant park
ReviewText: Great location with easy access to Bryant park, the NY public library and broadway. Friendly staff. Clean rooms and speedy elevators. Coffee situation is ok but there is a great cafe next door.

Title: Solid choice in midtown
ReviewText:
Location was great. Neighborhood had everything and felt safe. 

Double queen room worked well for family. But bathroom was tiny and no lobby space.

Title: ideal location
ReviewText: close to Grand Central - convenient and safe after a late night.

Title: Convenient location and Friendly Staff
ReviewText: Great location! Within walking distance of everything that was important to us and was a decent price for NYC pricing. This location has no in-house breakfast options, but there's a cafe literally right next door so we ended up going there in the morning instead. The coffee in the rooms were dark roast only but they did offer both caffeinated and decaf. Beds/pillows are hit or miss depending on your personal preference. For me, the beds were a bit firmer so after a couple of nights I was starting to get sore. The pillows for me were also too flat so I didn't have much support there either. Doubling up on the pillows was a must to get a decent night's sleep. But for a weekend it was okay. Made my bed at home feel that much more softer when I slept in in again lol.

Title: Nice, not thrilling, but great location!
ReviewText: For a weekend business trip where I didn't spend much time in my room at this hotel, it was fine and served me well with the location.  The queen bed room I had on the 8th Floor (804) had some trouble with the door locking which concerned me after having a couple times swiping the key card.  The room looked a bit more fancy in the advertised photos.  The very worn and chipped furniture which made the space a bit cramped and uncomfortable needs to be replaced along with the weird bed lamps with faces on them and the word "Think"...(?) :P  The water pressure was low when I took a shower and the toiletries not so great.  Expected a bit more upscale comfort.  I did sleep okay and had an easy check-out.  The TV and channels available were adequate but the remote was a pain to use.  If location is important to you, this is a good hotel option walking distance to Grand Central and midtown sites like Times Square. Wish there had been more bells & whistles for what I paid.  Nice staff though!

Title: Decent price for amenities/location, issue with support from staff
ReviewText: Location was great value, close to major landmarks and made trip walkable. Room was okay, the AC did not work and had to sleep and get ready with windows open. Had several issues with staff in checking in - we called to ensure hotel had valet parking and upon arrival was told it was “not available at this time”. Those handling our bags did not give us a ticket to pick up and gave us issues when trying to leave (even when I showed them the room receipt and described all 7 bags we were picking up) and delayed us another 30 minutes from leaving.

Title: Great location/ clean and modern
ReviewText: Great hotel. The location is perfect, it is clean and modern, and the staff were great. However, the bed was rock-hard, the pillows were of poor quality, and the room was so loud that falling asleep was almost impossible. I heard every little sound outside, even with earplugs on.

Title: Do NOT stay here.
ReviewText: Terrible accommodations. Rude staff. Definitely not family friendly. Hotel has nothing to offer.

Title: Family stay
ReviewText: Good for families and close to all main attractions. Will recommend it. No fridge on the room.

Title: Average hotel.
ReviewText: Not worth the price. No fridge. rooms are small and old utilities.

Title: Overall good experience
ReviewText: It was an overall pleasant stay, with some perks that need to be attended.

Title: Good service
ReviewText: Great location , love the coffee station front desk very attentive  good service and clean

Title: Very Poor Experience
ReviewText: Hotel was extremely dated, not even an offer of bottled water for a Titanium member, was told to go tot he gym and get a paper cup and fill it up. No lotion in bathroom, mattress hard as a board, HVAC noisy and would not warm room, fitness center a complete joke and nasty… would fit maybe 3 people. Awful experience and well below the Marriott standard.

Title: Práctico hotel en excelente ubicación
ReviewText: El hotel cumple con el objetivo de ser un refugio cómodo y sobrio en la mejor ubicación de Midtown Manhattan.

Title: Outdated and in need of updating
ReviewText: My room had duct tape on the windows to stop drafts, and the heating unit couldn’t keep the room habitable. While it was very cold outside the heater unit was lukewarm at best. The hallway was significantly warmer. I was not able to stay overnight and had to find other accommodations. I was not offered another room or options. It was not a good Marriott experience

Title: EXCELLENT SERVICE!
ReviewText: We love staying at Courtyard by Marriott New York Manhattan/Fifth Avenue.. every staff were very accomodating, greet you kindly from frontdesk, bellman, ,housekeeping. It was our first time in NY and the bellman ( forgot his name, very helpful from giving directions and handed us a NY Map he lend us his umbrella which very kind) and the front desk prolly he was the manager as we were given an early CI at 12nn( we got in the hotel at 7am from California) they have unlimited cofffeeee and teas which was perfect.. over all give this stay and property excellent service.. thank you all from california

Title: Would stay again
ReviewText: We enjoyed our stay. The rooms were a bit small, but being in NYC, it’s understandable. The staff were all very kind and helpful.

Title: Great Place, Convenient to Everything
ReviewText: With two queen beds, we had enough room to fit all four of us for our girls trip home base during our stay in NYC. Safe, clean, helpful staff. Exactly what we needed!

Title: 10 dias en Nueva York
ReviewText: El hotel cumplio la promesa por servicio, comodida, ubicacion. Muy recomendable

Title: such a great experience for a low cost
ReviewText: i needed a place to stay at the last minute and it was just the right location and very comfortable. The people were nice, it was simple and easy, and the coffee machine was free 24-7. thanks a lot, i will recommend this to others and possibly stay just for fun sometime.

Title: Great location and service, but lacks amenities
ReviewText: The hotel is in a great location, right off 5th Avenue and walking distance to both Times Square and Grand Central Station.   I was surprised, however, that there were no on-site snacks/drinks to purchase, places to work in the lobby, or breakfast options.   There was, however, a very nice coffee machine off the lobby and the staff was very helpful.   Ask for a refrigerator if you need one in your room and it will be delivered if available.

Title: Great stay!
ReviewText: The rooms were clean the coffee area was the best! I would stay there again.

Title: Birthday trip to NYC
ReviewText: I came to NYC for an early birthday trip with my mom. I chose this hotel based on its convenient location to multiple attractions I planned to visit. It is within a reasonable walking distance to grand central station, the New York public library, Bryant park, Rockefeller center, shopping on 5th avenue, and time square. There is a good restaurant right next door and the staff recommended the pizza place across the street which we ended up eating at! All the staff I spoke with were friendly & helpful. Whenever I come back to NYC, I would definitely stay here again!

Title: Doesn’t Measure Up
ReviewText: Zero amenity, run-down Marriott Courtyard in a sea of better Courtyards & countless other hotel choices. Keep looking…

Title: Not a good Courtyard compared to others.
ReviewText: I was disappointed with my experience. This hotel is not what I expect from a Courtyard. They did not even have bottled water nevermind offer it to me as a curiousy. I have never experienced that at a Marriott and I am a Marriott loyalist.

Title: Ideal location for a trip to New York
ReviewText: The location was ideal, walking distance to all the attractions.  I wish we had stayed longer, we didn't get to see everything we wanted.  Staff at the Marriott was very helpful.  Would definitely come back.

Title: Will never stay here again
ReviewText: Room was way too small for a family of 2 adults and 2 teens. Room was dirty and dusty. Water pressure was not good. The entered my room when there was a do not disturb sign and no one was in the lift. request for towels and pillows were unmet as they did not have enough. Had to request for things multiple times before they were delivered.

Title: Family vacation
ReviewText: Everything was good,  great location. Stuff was very helpful

Title: Great hotel in terrific location
ReviewText:
Our priority for our trip was a clean comfortable hotel in a good location accessible to many sites during the Christmas season. This place delivered on both. Easily walk up to Central
Park, Rockefeller Center, Times Square, right near to the Bryant park winter garden. Can get to both the green and red lines to move throughout the city. 
Clean room and friendly staff and the free fancy coffee espresso machine in the lobby was a nice surprise to start our mornings.

Title: Will not return
ReviewText: Extremely small lobby. Too few elevators. Rooms ready well after check in time. Front desk staff and some bellmen rude.

Title: NYC Holiday Trip
ReviewText: The hotel and staff were great. This was a first stay in NYC for grandson and myself. Staff made recommendations for restaurants and provided good directions for traveling the subway system for our sightseeing. Great location for walking to Times Square, Central Park and more.

Title: Great hotel
ReviewText: Hotel location was great and the staff was very nice. Very clean hotel. Coffee is good.

Title: Superb hotel..!
ReviewText: This hotel does not disappoint. It’s in a great location which is close to Time Square and Rockefeller  Plaza.  The rooms are clean and comfortable.

Title: Don't stay here
ReviewText: Upon arrival, the staff greeted me rudely never indicated as a gold Marriott Bonvoy totally indifferent attitude like I was bothering them. Got to my room and it smelled like mold was dirty toilet had pee around it. Smoke detector was hanging from the wire. Second room was a little bit better no mold, but still dirty And just old and tired for the amount of points I had to use I would expected the Taj Mahal. Coffee machine downstairs was slow as could be and there's line of 30 people in the morning because it was the only one they had. There was no food. It was not your typical courtyard by Marriott. Stay somewhere else if you're coming to town.

Title: Problems in Mahatton.
ReviewText: Not up to the standards for Marriott. I am a Premier member and have not been happy with Marriott in my last 2 stays. Requested rooms were not available and had to settle for inferior rooms. Tired of continued sales attempted when I was lied to several years ago by sales rep. Then told Marriott rep that I would not go on another sales presentation and she ignored me.

Title: Disappointed
ReviewText: It’s very run down, outdated, and just felt dirty. Not a pleasant stay. Staff was great though; the hotel just needs a lot of improving.

Title: Meh...
ReviewText:
I slept well, that was the one up side to this Marriott property.  Arrived at 5:30 PM, room was not ready until 6:15 PM.  Only compensation they offered me were some points.  Waited in the tiny cramped lobby, on the single sofa there, next to the front doors on a cold winter night.  Every time the lobby doors opened or closed, the sofa got hit with a blast of ice cold air, which was such a welcoming experience... 

I had friends also staying here, they arrived at 6:15 PM and their room was not ready either.  Check in time is at 4:00 PM.  If the hotel cannot meet their end of the bargain, then they should either change the check in time, or offer you a discount on the room.  

The room was clean, tidy, and I slept well.  That was the only upside.  Being told they are having staffing issues is not my problem, I am the guest, who paid for a room.

Title: Good location, poor rooms in need of a deep refresh
ReviewText: Considering the quality of other Marriott hotels in the area, even courtyards a few blocks away, this Courtyard hotel is in pretty poor shape.  The rooms are clean, and the bathroom is large, but the interiors are worn out, with parts hanging off and stains that won't come out.  One bright spot:  they do have daily housekeeping!

Title: Marriott courtyard
ReviewText: Excellent location to the theater district and shopping.  The staff was helpful

Title: Great location and outstanding staff
ReviewText:
If you are looking for a prime/safe location, outstanding as well as kind staff, and an exceptionally clean but very minimalistic room then this is your hotel. 

It was perfect for us because we were out enjoying the city from morning until night and only came back to  sleep.

Title: Roach motel
ReviewText: Filthy roach infested hotel with zero amenities and a staff that has zero concern for their guests. DO NOT STAY HERE ITS TANTAMOUNT TO A SHELTER

Title: last minuite plans to stay in N Y
ReviewText: the hotel is a safe and practical place to stay. not that great. Clean but not outstanding. the only food service I found was a coffee machine. no place to set ( one bench in lobby ) no lounge or lobby area. location is good. the rooms are fine, I dont get all excited about a lavish hotel room, thats not why I come to N Y so if my favorite hotels are full I would go back.

Title: Zero Rating
ReviewText: One of the queen beds in my room had sheets that were left with an unknown bodily substance on the sheet and duvet cover. Would not stay here again.

Title: Great location, excellent service
ReviewText: Highly recommend this location, clean room and spaces, excellent and courteous service!

Title: November 2025 Stay
ReviewText: The hotel is old and outdated. There is no onsite parking. The staff was friendly and it was in a good location to downtown New York.

Title: Okay/Fair
ReviewText:
Room was not updated, the bathroom floor was not clean/stained. The shower was not clean either, brown rust in bathroom. No free breaksfast but free hot beverages which was nice. 
Elevator broke down - only one working for many floors.

Title: Heating issues
ReviewText: Heating was not working and getting help was difficult

Title: New York adventure
ReviewText: Good location and helpful staff. However the decor is tired looking although clean. The room air con is very noisy and upgrade room for a view was only of a back street office.

Title: Great Location near Bryant Park
ReviewText: Hotel was in a great location near Bryant Park and walking distance to a Subway Station. It took approximately one hour to reach by train to/from JFK Airport. Hotel was clean and the room was a similar size to other hotels in Manhattan (on the smaller size compared to other cities). The lobby offered complimentary coffee and water at the small mezzanine area near Reception. The room was slightly dark due to limited lighting and the automatic blind malfunctioned after lowering and was unable to be raised. Staff were efficient at check-in/check-out

Title: Gym and Elevator needs attention
ReviewText:
This hotel has small gym like 10’X15’ room. 
One of the two elevators is down here for months.

Title: Inexcusable
ReviewText: Inexcusable to have one operating elevator

Title: Extremely Disappointing
ReviewText: My experience at this property was extremely disappointing.  I stay at Marriott properties exclusively, and have never had such a poor experience.  One of the two elevators was out of service, and the wait for an elevator consistently exceeded 20-30 minutes.  I took the stairs to and from my 8th-floor room the entire duration of my weekend stay.

Title: Elevator Nightmare
ReviewText:
The hotel is in a great location, next to Bryant Park. The room was fine, no issues. However, the fact that the hotel only had 1 working elevator for a 30 floor hotel is just unacceptable. We were there for 3 days and it was broken before we arrived and still broken when we left and the staff seemed to have no clue when it would be fixed. When you asked them, they would just shrug their shoulders and say it wasn’t their responsibility. It became a total nightmare trying to get back to your room in the afternoon or evening and it was almost impossible trying to go down to leave at any point. We ended up walking down the stairs each time as it was quicker than waiting for an elevator.

Before you make a reservation here, check to see if the elevators are ALL working. If it’s still just 1 elevator in service for 30 floors, find another hotel.

Title: There Are Better Hotels in the Area
ReviewText: There is nothing special about this hotel, really. I am a Titanium Elite member and was not acknowledged at any time during my stay. There is no F&B like every Courtyard is supposed to have. The rooms are dated and have awful (and I mean awful) lighting, even by hotel standards. 1 elevator was broken so the lines to use the elevator were obnoxiously long. Generally, that is ok and I understand that things happen, but the staff had no guidance or training to call the elevator to the lobby on it's downtime so guests would not have to wait. That was just 1 thing on top of all the other things that made the hotel completely forgettable. The housekeeping and maintenance staff were good and very friendly, always making sure they said hello and being as helpful as they could. At checkout, whoever your older gentlemen is working your valet, I would fire him at my hotel immediately. We called an uber to take us to the airport and he berated my wife on why we did that rather than pay the hotel to take us (which the hotel was more.) He wouldn't shut up about it, he brought it up 3 times while we were in the lobby and it was extremely uncomfortable and unprofessional. Overall, for the price point and location, there is absolutely zero reason to stay at this hotel. You can stay at just about any comparable brand hotel in just a block or two radius and get more value for your money, like included breakfast or literally anything for your membership level.

Title: Poorly maintained
ReviewText: Poorly maintained, waited 20 minutes for an elevator (one way) to get to my room.  The room stunk and the shower was dirty and had not been restocked. The windows were dogged over so we couldn't even see outside. Requested a late checkout and this aso didn't happen. Very poorly maintained and run.

Title: Great location, friendly staff
ReviewText: Cannot beat the location right off of 5th Ave, the staff was very friendly and there is free water and a coffee/hot chocolate machine (with lattes, cappuccino etc) on the 2nd floor. For a NYC 2 queen room, which are usually tiny in this price point, it had a decent amount of space for our family of 4. The AC worked well. Only complaint is there was only 1 working elevator during our 4 night stay, but Marriott did comp points for the inconvenience which was unsolicited by us, so that was a nice recovery. Definitely recommend.

Title: Location. Location. Location. Not much else.
ReviewText: I have stayed at the hotel nearly half a dozen times this summer and fall, staying three to four nights each stay. The hotel is located near Bryant Park, and that’s the best reason to stay there. Location, location, location. The staff are helpful. The facility is another story, however. Thankfully, I generally don’t spend very much time in my room. The vent fan in every bathroom is always on. It roars so loudly you have to close the door to muffle the noise to get some sleep. There are two elevators for the 30 story building with a basement. Only one of the two elevators has operated for over a month. There seems to be no sense of urgency to get it fixed. The desk staff have gotten so accustomed to it they don’t bother to apologize to guests for the inconvenience. The rooms are clean, but the furnishings are dated with damaged and chipped drawers and desks throughout. On a scale of A to F, overall the hotel is a weak C.

Title: Location great - but needs updates
ReviewText:
Pros: 
1. Location location location
2. housekeeping staff was very nice - front desk staff was acceptable
3. room was passable and a good size

Cons (for $$$$/night I expect a lot more - even in NYC): 
1. broken elevator, leaving only one for 30 floors of guests as well as the staff - I waited 30 mins to get to the lobby (from the 5th floor) to check out, was charged a wait fee by my Uber, and nearly late for my flight
2. no food - not even snacks or bottled water
3. front desk staff/concierge should be better informed about food choices located nearby
4. room was very dated - and had no robe

Title: DUMP! DO NOT STAY HERE 0 OUT OF 10
ReviewText: Old and dated.  30 floors and only one working elevator.  Dirty -no amenities.  The room was a shoebox. The bedding was thin and didn't keep you warm.  Pillows were worn and squishy.  Overall - just absolutely gross.  I get that it is New York but it was awful.  I was on the 12th floor and the walls are paper thin.  I heard every horn, siren, scream.  Felt like I was sleeping on the street.  Do not stay here 0 out of 10 - DO NOT STAY HERE.  Plus it is way over priced for what a dump it is!!

Title: Perfectly located
ReviewText: Excellent service and staff! Only one functioning elevator and odd odor from bathroom in our room.

Title: Good hotel for Manhattan, but broken elevator caused challenges
ReviewText: 30 floors with just one elevator for guests and staff is a challenge.  Other than that... it was what was expected for a mid-town location

Title: New York
ReviewText: So close to Time Square and all the beauty of New York City. Staff were amazing

Title: Aging Hotel and Elevator Issues Affected the Stay
ReviewText: This was my 4th stay at this property over the past 5+ years. The location remains excellent, but the building is clearly starting to show its age. During my stay, only one of the two elevators was working, and for a 20+ floor building, that made getting in and out extremely slow and inconvenient.

Title: Stay away from This Marriott Courtyard!
ReviewText: This hotel is a poor excuse and doesn’t live up to the Marriott image and history. Sad.

Title: Perfect location for family vacay in NYC
ReviewText:
The hotel was in the best location for touring NYC on foot over a period of 3 days/2 nights. Walkable to almost all the sightseeing spots. Very clean, great staff, especially concierge (so funny) who was our goto for best directions. Our kids missed having access to cereal in the morning but there was a cafe adjacent to the hotel which had everything ($$). Missed having a fridge in the room to store drinks and leftovers but room/hotel 
 was clean with super comfy beds. Only downside, for our entire stay there was only one elevator operational. Being on 19 floor meant long waits and too full elevators - we heard that this was the situation for over a week before we arrived. Didn’t seem like it was a priority to repair.

Title: Great stay in NYC
ReviewText: The Courtyard is perfectly situated next to Bryant Park and the NYC Public Library. We enjoyed the beds and view, but hope that the elevator issues are resolved soon. One worked and one did not, so there were some unhappy people. Those I encountered who worked at the hotel were top-notch, kind, and responsive. I highly recommend the hotel.

Title: One elevator to none and lack of upkeep
ReviewText:
Throughout the duration of my stay, only one elevator was operational in a hotel with over 30 floors, causing long wait times and significant inconvenience for guests. On the morning of my checkout, the only working elevator also stopped functioning. As a result, my guest and I were forced to carry our belongings down 20 flights of stairs to reach the lobby.

What made this experience even more disappointing was that no staff member offered assistance, alternative arrangements, or even a simple gesture such as arranging a taxi to compensate for the inconvenience. This lack of support was not only physically exhausting but also inconsistent with the level of hospitality I expect from a Marriott property.

Title: Nonworking Elevators
ReviewText: This location has only two elevators. During our stay, both elevators did not work, and we had to climb 29 flights of stairs to get to our room.

Title: Hotel is ok at best
ReviewText: Small rooms, outdated, bad elevators.

Title: Unacceptable
ReviewText: We paid high price for a hotel that doesn’t have the basics (eg had to go to CVS to buy water, the hotel literally didn’t have water bottles). There are 2 elevators and one is broken. At one point, both were broken. The beds were uncomfortable. Relied on the marriott brand and was utterly disappointed. I know this is low-class category of marriott, but still, the experience at this hotel is unacceptable.

Title: Great location
ReviewText: Nice staff clean and quite

Title: My Courtyard Experience
ReviewText: Courtyard by Mariottt is an amazing hotel. I had a wonderful night stay there. The staff is very friendly and helpful. The room was very clean and quiet. It’s a great place to stay. If you are looking for for a hotel stay, Courtyard by Marriott is the place to be.

Title: Don't expect good customer service
ReviewText:
Although the room was clean, it was significantly smaller than all other Courtyards in Manhattan that I have stayed at. 

Even worse than the room, though, was if you have a concern or problem, you're ignored. Oh, you'll get the usual "I'm sorry" but it's more "I'm sorry you're having problems but that's your problem, not mine" rather than "I'm sorry, how can I help make this right." Apparently, the staff is used to having complaints and so there response is simply insincere. I guess they, too, become immune to the many complaints they get. And, if you ask for the manager to discuss the issue, don't expect a call. Basically, no one cares about your stay. 

Move onto another hotel.

Title: Excellent Location and Stay
ReviewText: This Courtyard is perfect if you want to be near NYC attractions but not in the midst of the crowds.  The room was very comfortable and quiet.  The staff was friendly, professional, and helpful.  I would recommend this location.

Title: No Air conditioning in room
ReviewText: No air conditioning in room. No maintenance service.

Title: Labor Day Trip
ReviewText: Single Queen Room is tiny. Would get the double queen beds for larger room. Have to ask for fridge. Only two elevators, sometimes have to wait a minute. No restaurant or hangout space in lobby. Rooms are clean and well maintained. Location is a big plus. Staff is attentive and courteous. Price on the high side for motel-like atmosphere.

Title: Great Experience and Service
ReviewText: We stay here when we attend the U.S. Open Tennis Championships for the last 8 or so years. We love it!

Title: Location is Everything!
ReviewText: What they lack in amenities,  and a "fancy" look and good lighting in the bathrrom, they make up for in Location, Great staff and a comfortable bed. They will give you a mini fridge but you must request it. The hotel is located Near Bryant Park, 5th Ave., a few block from Time Square. I would stay again

Title: Pleasant weekend stay
ReviewText: The hotel staff were nice; the location is perfect for a city getaway.

Title: Traveling on business to Manhattan
ReviewText: Great location, comfortable rooms, friendly and helpful staff.

Title: Excellent Staff. Excellent Location. Very Good Hotel
ReviewText: My second stay at the hotel in the last 2 months. The hotel is in a great location, just steps away from Bryant Park and near Grand Central Station. It’s also an easy walk from Moynihan Station. The staff is outstanding. Very accommodating. Very friendly. The rooms are clean and comfortable.

Title: Hotel review
ReviewText: Overall quality is poor and hotel needs renovation. This does not adhere to the standards of a Marriott property.

Title: Great location, lovely staff, but several shortcomings.
ReviewText: Location is excellent - midtown Manhattan just off 5th Avenue.  Staff was friendly and accommodating.  Room left much to be desired - no minifridge, no bottled water, no water glasses, poor lighting in bathroom.  Only window covering was blackout shade, so no privacy unless you blocked out daylight.

Title: Great Stay
ReviewText: Excellent location. Rooms are good size and clean. Staff is friendly and responsive. Would definitely stay here again.

Title: Belle localisation , mais hôtel vieillissant offrant peu de service
ReviewText:
La localisation est parfaite , mais le personnel n’est pas très accueillant et le prix payé était loin d’être celui annoncé ( +50$ / jour sans explication valable ) 
Les chambres offrent peu de confort

Title: Great hotel!
ReviewText:
Great hotel! The room was clean. The staff was very friendly. Excellent location in Midtown Manhattan around the corner from Fifth Avenue 
and Bryant Park. Very short walk to Grand Central Station.

Title: Cramped with no amenities
ReviewText: The rooms are small and cramped and did not have coffee maker, mini fridge, or microwave.  The only coffee is in the lobby area and the morning line is long.

Title: No volveria a considerarlo como una opcion
ReviewText: La tarifa fue alta, la habitacion con mucho ruido externo, sin frigobar, olor a humedad. La ubicacion es perfecta.

Title: Great value
ReviewText: Great value and location. Staff was excellent. Great find

Title: Great Manhattan hotel
ReviewText: Excellent location for exploring Manhattan.

Title: Horrible room!!!
ReviewText: Very dated, horrible, smelly carpet and old, stained tile in the bathroom. Avoid this at all cost!!

Title: Overpriced
ReviewText: Hotel was towards the lower end of 3-star service but priced like a 4-star hotel

Title: 私達は快適な滞在が出来ました
ReviewText: とても感謝してます。立地条件が最高でした。治安も良く安心して過ごせました。セントラルステーションまで近いのが最高に良かったです^_−☆

Title: Be Careful of your room
ReviewText: Before you get comfortable check to make sure your bed is clean and the bathroom.

Title: 3 night stay - not up to Marriott standards
ReviewText: Dark, noisy room. Poor lighting.

Title: Great experience
ReviewText: It was a great experience staying in Courtyard New York Manhattan/ fifth avenue. The room was clean, the staff were friendly and accommodating. It was really a relaxing place to stay for leisure.

Title: Excellent Location + Priced Right Room Options
ReviewText:
This Midtown location is amazing (Bryant Park, Grand Central Station are just a few blocks away and it's a very quick walk to the Theatre District). 
The rooms are clean and priced right for NYC. The staff, especially the housekeeping crew, are friendly, helpful, and attentive to your requests.  Safe, clean, and affordable option for families visiting NYC!

Title: Marriott stays not recognized no water provided. No water in the room
ReviewText: It’s a ok hotel for a quick one/two day stay. Do not expect good service.

Title: Room for the buck
ReviewText:
I came to NY for work meetings. I stayed at the hotel with my husband and child. We got a room looking a next door office building, but we didn't have the sound of traffic from 5th Ave. The room was clean. We had two beds, which were a bit small for two people, but it's fun to share a bed with your kid sometimes ;)
We didn't have a fridge at first, but when we requested it, it was delivered within 20 minutes.
The only negative thing I will say, is upon arrival with my child, who was tired and falling asleep, I asked the front desk to explain the bill to me (it was significantly higher) and I was met with a slight annoyance that he had to explain it to me twice. Minor issue, but never the less it's the first impression.

Title: Basic, clean and good
ReviewText: Rooms are basic, but were clean. We never had any issues with the elevators. We chose to stay on a lower floor just to make sure there were no issues and ended up on the fifth floor. The location is perfect. You have great access to the subways and to all the attractions. The only thing I’d unlike other Marriotts they don’t give you any bottled water and they don’t have any to buy onsite.

Title: I’ve been to better Marriott's
ReviewText: I’ve been to better Marriott

Title: Not good
ReviewText: Only good thing about this hotel was that it was in a reasonable location. Dirty and dingy

Title: Great place to stay!
ReviewText: Rooms were quiet and pretty.  Was a little tight with a wheelchair, but typical for New York.

Title: “New York” Queen beds
ReviewText:
The staff was extremely rude when we asked about our bed size being a double and not a queen. They stayed “it’s a New York queen size bed” and giggled. 

The coffee area had dirty cups and garbage overflowing most of the time too.

Paid for queen beds and got doubles with 4 people in our room.

Title: Great central location
ReviewText:
Excellent, friendly staff. We stayed at three Marriott locations in NYC on this trip. This Courtyard had the friendliest and most helpful staff.
Great central location. Easy access to everything we wanted to see. 
We had luggage that was 2 days last and when the airlines delivered it the bellhop immediately called my personal cellphone to let me know. My luggage was stored in a secure, locked area until I was able to pick it up.

Title: girls trip to New York
ReviewText: Hotel has perfect, midtown location, excellent coffee machine in the lobby all staff on site, courteous, and extremely helpful

Title: Poor service but great location!
ReviewText:
Hotel was very clean and nice.  The rooms were a decent size and nice.   The location was so convenient to everything we were doing and made it really easy!  The coffee bar and water station was really nice too!   We had some trouble with a very rude staff member at check in and part of party not getting the room
They booked.   We also didn’t get the request to have rooms near each other which is frustrating when you call in advance and are told this is happening and planning a trip for a family.

Title: I felt more refreshed after I left than I did before I arrived!
ReviewText: There was nothing stressful about any part of the stay.  Every staff member I encountered was welcoming and accommodating.  Staying where the staff are happy help make the visit restful.  In addition to that, the blackout curtains and the bed made each nights rest rejuvenating.

Title: Horrendous outside noise meant sleepless nights
ReviewText: No recognition for a gold member by staff. Changed rooms to improver noise, no change. hardly got any sleep.

Title: Feedback Regarding Recent Stay
ReviewText:
During my stay, I accidently dropped a pizza slice in the lobby. The gentleman and female receptionist at the front desk responded rather rudely by loudly instructing me to “throw it outside,” instead of allowing me to use a proper trash receptacle inside the lobby. Their tone and handling of the situation felt unprofessional and disrespectful.

Additionally, when I checked in, the older Asian gentleman at the front desk was not welcoming. He did not offer a greeting, nor did he provide any information about the hotel amenities or explain how to operate the elevators—something I’ve come to expect at other Marriott locations.

Speaking of the elevators, I found them to be quite confusing and frustrating. I had to press my floor button multiple times before the elevator finally responded, and there seemed to be no clear pattern or instructions on how the system works.

Lastly, I had indicated during booking that there would be three guests in the room, implying that we would be using the pull-out couch. However, there were no additional pillows or a comforter provided in the room, and I had to request them separately.

I hope you find this feedback helpful in identifying areas for improvement. I appreciate your time and attention to these matters.

Title: Great location and reasonably priced
ReviewText: Central location, Marriott quality, and doesn't break the bank.

Title: Stay in another hotel. Not worth the price.
ReviewText: The services are poor. Don’t have basic amenities.

Title: Not worth the money
ReviewText: Very small gym, very expensive hotel. No service

Title: Gem in Lower Mahattan
ReviewText: Really enjoyed this hotel. Convenient location to attractions in Lower Manhattan. Rooms have been stylishly updated and good size for a NY hotel. Would definitely choose again on a return trip.

Title: Nice Hotel in the Heart of everything
ReviewText: Nicely located comfortable hotel with complementary coffee and water.

Title: Este hotel no cumple con las expectativas
ReviewText: Solo está bien situado, de resto recepción pequeña, cuartos mínimos, el aseo deja mucho

Title: There are better places
ReviewText: There are better places to stay than here. Poor bathroom upkeep, dimly lit. Felt like I was constantly being watched everywhere I went in a public area. I won’t stay there again.

Title: Staff is incredible
ReviewText: From the moment U checked in until I checked out the entire staff has a Happy face on and were extremely welcoming.

Title: Comfortable stay
ReviewText: It was a comfortable stay start to finish! Nice room and wonderful people!

Title: Lage sehr gut, Schlafsofa sehr unbequem
ReviewText:
Wir haben ein Zimmer mit Kingsize-Bett und Schlafsofa gebucht. Das Bett war bequem, das Schlafsofa hingegen absolut nicht. Man spürte jede Metallverstrebung und die Matratze hing durch. Trotz mehreren Decken auf der Matratze hat unser Kind sehr schlecht geschlafen. 
Das Zimmer war angenehm gross. Im Bad fehlte eine Ablagefläche.
Wir hatten ein eher ruhiges Zimmer zum Innenhof. Leider hörten wir auch die Klimaanlagen der anderen Gebäude.
Die Lage neben dem Briant-Park ist sehr angenehm und praktisch, kann ich sehr empfehen.

Title: Bon hôtel
ReviewText: Je recommande cet hôtel pour son emplacement et sa propreté mais les chambres méritent d’être modernisées, la Clim est vraiment bruyante et la moquette au sol ce n’est plus possible.

Title: Wrong Room
ReviewText: We booked a room through Hotels.com, and when we arrived were told the room we booked wasn’t available due to an “inventory error”.  After a terrible travel day, this was especially bad news.

Title: Room Services
ReviewText: Bed sheet and blankets were made us itchy skin most of the night and could not sleep well.

Title: Nice hotel, horrible booking process
ReviewText:
We loved the hotel - it’s clean, modern, nice sized rooms and well situated. 
Booking it was a disaster - the Marriott website literally doubled the price of the rooms while we asked our son if he preferred the Marriott with the gym (Courtyard) or breakfast.  When I called the Marriott main number in disbelief, we were told that this happens and there’s nothing they can do to assist, despite our holding a Marriott credit card for twenty some years. We ended up using points and plan to cancel our credit card when the annual fee is next due

Title: Friendly staff but no perks
ReviewText: Nice average hotel in a good location. Very friendly staff but no marriott loyalty perks. Would help to have just a water in the room.

Title: Exactly what I expect from a Marriott.
ReviewText:
Excellent location in Midtown Manhattan (5th Ave & 40th St).
--Clean, quiet room 20 floors up.
--Efficient, friendly front-desk staff.
--24 hr free/coffee & tea on mezz level next to fitness ctr and biz ctr.
--No 'resort fee' that you sometimes find at many locations
--Excellent price for a Sunday night

Title: A very comfortable stay in Midtown
ReviewText: This hotel is conveniently located right beside Bryant Park and just a short walk from Grand Central Station and Times Square. Rooms are comfortable and service is very efficient. Communal areas are quite small but I love the free coffee machine...very nice touch. Nothing to complain about here, a very well run establishment. Only minor nit pick is the elevators can get busy leaving you waiting for a few minutes butt  overall not a big deal.

Title: Family Getaway
ReviewText: Great location, great staff

Title: Excellent Stay at Courtyard New York Manhattan/Fifth Avenue – 10/10
ReviewText:
**Excellent Stay at Courtyard New York Manhattan/Fifth Avenue – 10/10**  

Courtyard New York Manhattan/Fifth Avenue delivers on its promise of inviting, flexible spaces with classic style. The hotel's location at 3 E 40th Street is fantastic—just a block east of Bryant Park and within walking distance of Grand Central Terminal, making it an ideal base for exploring the city. Several metro stations are nearby, providing easy access uptown and downtown.  

The rooms are comfortable, with our two-queen setup providing a great night’s sleep. While the HVAC runs constantly, the lowest setting is 66°F but feels closer to 70°F. The exercise facility, located on the Mezzanine, is compact but well-equipped with Life Fitness machines (a stepper, treadmill, and recumbent bike), two benches, a weight rack, and space for stretching. A convenient coffee machine offering hot water, hot chocolate, and warm milk is also available there.  

Dining is effortless, thanks to **Crema Gourmet**, located right next door. We had breakfast there, and while the food was good, the real advantage was the convenience—perfect for a quick and easy start to the day.  

Service was excellent—Richard, our bellman, arranged a reasonably priced ride to LaGuardia for us. Overall, this hotel is in a prime location, offering a balance of comfort, convenience, and accessibility. I’d definitely stay here again!

Title: Small but adequate
ReviewText: Although the room was small, we were only in it to sleep!  Room and bathroom were very clean.  The staff we interacted with were pleasant and helpful!  Overall, we had a great stay!

Title: Perfect
ReviewText: Perfect location close proximity to everything . Comfy beds, great staff. Definitely will be staying again.

Title: Great location
ReviewText: Would book again! Good location and accessible by walking or driving.

Title: NYC Spring Break
ReviewText: My granddaughter and I thoroughly enjoyed staying at the courtyard. The proximity to so many locations was easy to walk to and getting a taxi or Uber to travel to sites further away was as fast as one or two minutes, wait time. The staff at the front desk were so helpful and welcoming, and it was obvious each time we talked to them that they were more than glad to help us. We never felt like we were wasting their time. I would definitely stay at this property again.

Title: New York Family Vacation
ReviewText: The team here are very quick to respond and kept the hotel organized, clean and friendly. House keepers were great and remembered my preferences for each day. The front desk was quick to get me items I requested and even planned ahead to help as they took the time to know what my needs would be through friendly conversation. Great experience!

Title: St Patrick’s day
ReviewText: Fantastic location! Wonderful staff! Amazing coffee machine on the main floor! We would definitely recommend this hotel for anyone coming to Manhattan. Only draw back was there wasn’t a small refrigerator in the room so we had to throw out our leftover Italian pastries and the NY bagels and cream cheese.

Title: Room Type Issue
ReviewText: The hotel could not honor the room type (2 queens) that I booked. The room was ok.

Title: Lacking
ReviewText: No fridge, no restaurant, and the A/c was loud.

Title: Pleasant stay!
ReviewText: We had a lovely stay. We had a nice room and we slept very well. The airconditioning made a lot of noise, luckily during our stay we didn't need to use it. We loved the free coffee, so much choice. Near our room there was an icemachine sometimes that gave some disturbance when other people used in the night or early in the morning. But above that we had a nice stay.

Title: #1maama
ReviewText: Not quite what I expected.  But passable.

Title: All was good. Rated food good as only option since we did not use this service.
ReviewText: Staff and hotel were excellent.

Title: Great location, spacious rooms
ReviewText: Should you be looking for accommodations north of Madison near the NYC Library and high end shopping that have large rooms, nice team, and no nonsense, I suggest Courtyard 5th Ave. Great for business travelers and holiday seekers.

Title: 素晴らしい対応
ReviewText: スタッフの方の対応が素晴らしく、ストレス無く過ごせました。ニューヨークは物価が高いので、コーヒーや水が無料なのも有り難いです。

Title: Excellent service!
ReviewText: They were so accommodating to one of our party that needed some extra assistance. Thank you!

Title: Ok
ReviewText: Bed is very hard, and it is very noisy in the hotell. But staff is very good and a very good location for the hotell.

Title: Fabulous First Time in NYC
ReviewText: This was my first visit to NYC, with no prior knowledge I booked to stay at The Courtyard, Fifth Avenue.  I had doubts as I heard lots of people mention hotels closer to Times Square but I definitely made a great choice.  This hotel is close enough to tourist hot spots but away from the hustle and bustle.  I am already looking to book to return in spring 2026.  Staff were welcoming and always happy to help.

Title: Mediocre Stay
ReviewText: Very average room. "Amenities" was a keurig on the mezzanine. When I asked the front desk for a glass or cup since we had none, I was told "the person that does that is on break for the next half hour" which is not something a Marriott normally says; I'm used to them finding a way.

Title: Nice stay
ReviewText: The room was nice. I liked the coffee/drinks on the mezzanine floor. I liked the entertainment on the in room TV.

Title: Family Vacation
ReviewText: The only thing i didn't like is that there's no microwave in the room and I have to go to another floor to use a microwave

Title: My first time staying at a Courtyard Marriott in NYC.
ReviewText: Perfect location.   Great front desk staff!   Rooms are basic, but clean.   Coffee on the mezzanine was great!    But would appreciate a bagel.

Title: Excellent location
ReviewText: The hotel has an excellent location near top places like Grand Central Station and Bryant Park.

Title: Room Downgraded
ReviewText: Room was downgraded without compensation. Highly not recommended.

Title: Great location great value, best staff ever
ReviewText: I really enjoyed my stay, the location was perfect and in walking distance to all that you want to see and more.  The Staff was very accommodating and can give great tips advice on travel to and from, and restaurant recommendations.  I will be back

Title: Stay somewhere else
ReviewText: This hotel is awful, full garbage bags in the hallway, unpleasant smells, no lobby, paper cups in your room.

Title: Great location, unclean room
ReviewText: Dirty used razor in the shower when checking in, location is great though

Title: Comfort & Convenience on Fifth
ReviewText: We could not have asked for a more comfortable and convenient accommodation. When you add the friendliness of the front desk staff it made for a thoroughly enjoyable stay!

Title: Excellent location and excellent service
ReviewText: We loved this location. It was walking distance to everything we wanted to do and see. The room was great.

Title: Could have been so much better
ReviewText: This Marriot needs more amenities and working elevators.

Title: Worst hotel ever
ReviewText: Worse than motel, dingy dark rooms, dirty carpet, cracked sink … list goes on

Title: Broken Elevators
ReviewText: Walking down 23 flights of stairs was NOT what we wanted before we walk around New York City.

Title: Elevator issues
ReviewText: You wouldn’t think one of the two elevators down would be that big of a deal. Turns out it was no matter what floor you were on if you had bags that you didn’t want to carry up and down the stairs. Get ready to wait for literally 45 minutes up to an hour to - get to your room, get to the lobby from your room, or get your bags if you drop them off to be held. You will have more luck leaving super early in the morning or reallyyy late at night. Truly one of the worst experiences I have had at any Marriott I’ve ever stayed at just because of the elevator issues. Staff was kind and the guy doing the bags was trying his best with what he had

Title: Elevator Issues
ReviewText: Only one working elevator. We were on the 24th Floor and had to take the stairs each time. There was a sign that said it would be fixed mid January. The location is perfect, right directly behind Bryant Park. Felt safe and was a quick walk each direction to wherever we wanted to go.

Title: undefined
ReviewText: Do not stay here till elevator is fixed

Title: Terribly hotel
ReviewText: The elevator was an issue as soon as we checked in. Only one was available and it took up to 30 minutes to get to our room or back to the lobby. It would either not stop for us when we were trying to get to the lobby or I Th would stop on every floor for no reason. No staff did anything to address the issue. This was an everyday issue.

Title: Good location. Long elevator waits.
ReviewText: Hotel was clean and staff were friendly. 30 floor hotel with only one working elevator during the holidays was disappointing.

Title: One elevator
ReviewText:
The hotel only has one functioning elevator and it gets completely jammed which leads to long wait times.  It's been that way for many months from what we were told.  

The room on the 18th floor was fairly noisy...but I imagine better than the lower floors.  

No social space in the hotel and the room was clean, but not at the levels of other courtyard hotels we've stayed at.  A guest coin laundry area would have been welcome.  

Location and staff were the highlight for our stay.
Great and wonderful guest services, Hotel was fully clean. Free coffee was great. Great location. Problems with elevator repair bothers because of the waiting time. Sofa cama mattress was awful.

Title: Gold member is mean less
ReviewText: ignored gold membership no benefits

Title: Not what I expected
ReviewText: Unorganized, broken elevators, no breakfast, no valet parking, no seating so you have to stand in the lobby air didn’t work 30 flights of stairs if elevators are broke, very very small lobby not worth 725.00

Title: TERRIBLE ELEVATOR SITUATION. AVOID AT ALL COSTS.
ReviewText:
This is a 30-floor hotel. It has only ONE, slow elevator. It is as dysfunctional as that sounds. Had to wait 5-20 mins EACH TIME you want to go up or down. 

Almost missed our flight on the way out. Asked for the Elevator at 5:15am, it showed up at 5:40am. The STAFF WAS USING IT. 

Staff casually brushes it off, "elevator is down, nothing we can do". It has been going on for months. AVOID AT ALL COSTS.

Title: Only having one working elevator is a huge inconvenience
ReviewText: Having one of your two elevators out of service is a problem.  This really should be something you tell people before they book so they can make an informed decision on whether or not to stay at your location.  My understanding is it has been out of service for months.

Title: Will never go back to this hotel
ReviewText:
Very poor;
No food and coffee machine some times out of milk

Title: Very basic accommodation in a good location
ReviewText: Poor standard of accommodation, lift wasnt working so long waits to get up and down.

Title: Great location but only one elevator
ReviewText: The hotel location is great and convenient to get around in the central part of New York during holiday season. However, one elevator is broken, maybe for a long time. Therefore, it is not convenient for families to get up to the hotel rooms.

Title: Tired decor.
ReviewText: Needs updating.  Tired.  Old.  More coffee stations.  Only one elevator worked. .

Title: Abusive
ReviewText: Don’t stay here until elevator situation is resolved.

Title: What?  Only 1 working elevator
ReviewText: It's hard to believe that at this time of year that only 1 of the 2 elevators was working.  There should have some type of discount for this problem.  Most times it would take 3 trips to get to my floor.  I have stayed at this location before with no problems.  Big disappointment.

Title: Beware elevator delays.
ReviewText: Will be a year until second elevator is fixed.

Title: Disappointing Stay
ReviewText:
Mixed reviews on staff but over-worked concierge was great! 
Only having one working elevator was awful. Had this been disclosed at the time the reservation was made, I would have made other arrangements. I thinks it’s very deceiving not to disclose this issue sooner..

Title: Literal waste of time waiting on an elevator
ReviewText: We stayed at this hotel based on location. With only 1 elevator working we were unable to enjoy our morning activities due to wait times of over 1 hour just to get to our room, which was on the 25th floor. Although unnecessary, we had to walk down the stairs many times after waiting for the elevator, which never stopped on our floor. The manager had no empathy, telling guests to check out and offering 0 compensation for the inconvenience, stating that the elevator issues were listed on the website, NOT easily found and it stated the issues would be resolved by DECEMBER 1st, over 20 days from our stay. NYC is to be enjoyed outside, not waiting for an elevator. We did cut our stay short due to the inconvenience and the suggestion of the manager.

Title: Good location and clean.
ReviewText: The hotel was in a great location and was clean.  Pretty basic.  The only issue was one of two elevators was down for the entire stay resulting in long waits.  This is probably temporary, but a fairly big inconvenience.

Title: Elevator fiasco
ReviewText: One out of two elevators was not working during the time of our stay. We had an infant and a stroller. After waiting 20 minutes for the elevator we gave up and took the stairs down 8 flights in order to make it to our appointment on time. The staff did nothing to help the situation. The elevator was filled up in the lobby then wouldn't stop at our floor because it was already too heavy. Then it filled up on the way down and skipped us again because it was already too heavy. This happened multiple times. Absolutely ridiculous.

Title: Elevators didn’t work, forced to use stairs
ReviewText: Horrible experience with 1 working elevator during the busiest time of the year. Had a disabled family member so couldn’t take the stairs down 21 flights but was forced to wait 30+ minutes for an elevator. Walking and healthy individuals would cram the elevators leaving no space for those that needed to actually ride the elevator. Make sure the elevators are working before you book here.

Title: Absolute No 4 star
ReviewText: Nice location but very poor hotel

Title: Don’t stay at this hotel.
ReviewText: One of the hotel elevators is out of service, and it is a major inconvenience.  The hotel does not alert patrons to this inconvenience ahead of time.  We were a family traveling with a child on crutches/in a boot, paying for two rooms, and we were put on the 20th floor.  So we had no choice but to take the elevator, which would sometimes pass our floor, despite the button being pressed, or the elevator would be packed, and we couldn’t fit.  I disliked this Marriott immensely, and I’ve stayed at many different Marriott’s in NYC.

Title: Not Up to Marriott Standards
ReviewText: The hotel is dingy with only one working elevator. The towels were thin and the bathroom was poorly appointed. There was not even a chair in the room. I have stayed here more than a few times but this is my last time.

Title: Not worth the money
ReviewText:
No breakfast 
LONG wait for elevator 
adequate room 
good location

Title: Not the best example of marriott
ReviewText: The staff is not very welcoming.   Issues with the property (1 of the elevators was not working for months and was not advised until day before).

Title: Needs repair, dark dingy rooms and broken tile in bath
ReviewText: Don’t stay here. They wanted $1375 for one night in a tiny double room. I used points, it was 70,000. Not at all what you would expect from Marriott. One elevator (out of 2) was broken so wait times were ridiculous. We were in a quick trip to NYC to see the Rockettes and taking a half hour to get an elevator was ridiculous. Stairs weren’t really an option as we were in the 25th floor. I’m usually a Marriott girl but this one missed the mark big time.

Title: Horrible location
ReviewText: The worst Marriott I’ve stayed at to date, and I have over 100 stays this year alone. One functioning elevator, brown water out of the sink and shower, next to zero hot water and no water Bottles available for purchase/provided. This location should probably not even be open.

Title: Macy's parade experience
ReviewText: We had another great experience in NYC walk to Bryant Park, great location to enjoy the parade. The entire staff was very friendly, professional and helpful.

Title: Good convenient location!
ReviewText:
The Hotel was fine, good service and friendly personnel. During our stay they had only one elevator and it was a mess. Not their fault but still pretty inconvenient.
The complementary coffee was really good but in a small area that was very congested all the time.

Title: whats up with the elevator
ReviewText: had to wait for the elevator 10 minutes going up or down.

Title: Worst stay ever
ReviewText: I have already provided everything you need to know. The overall stay was poor. The hotel was not cleaned. Well the elevators didn’t work, the morning food was in disarray, and the staff was overrun.

Title: Horrible property
ReviewText: This is the worst Marriott property that we have used. Room was dirty and furniture had tears in fabric. One elevator was operational so wait was long and very crowded. Fitness area extremely small. Beverage area small and no bottled water offered. Definitely will not visit again.

Title: Friendly staff but broken elevator
ReviewText: We had one elevator working all weekend, this caused a lot of delays all around.  The lobby was also extremely small so it was very congested while we all waited.  The cafe machine was also broken, which was disappointing.  The one positive, was the staff was friendly.

Title: Nov 30 - Dec 2
ReviewText: Probably won’t stay here again

Title: Convenience tempered by maintenance
ReviewText: Courteous and convenient. Elevator maintenance time consuming problem.

Title: Needs lot of work to meet other Marriott standards
ReviewText: Sorry to say that this is not a great experience .  Even my colleagues had the same opinion . Water was muddy, elevators doesn’t work, no room cleaning . Paid premium to stay on a motel setup. Only good thing I was coffee machine but it always had a long wait :(

Title: Not your typical Marriott
ReviewText: If you have stated at the other Marriotts before just know this is nothing like them.

Title: Poor customer service
ReviewText: There was a mix up in the booking rate and front desk staff was rude and unhelpful. I called a few days later and the ‘acting manager’ hung up on me when I requested the manager call me. I called back and requested a manager call and two weeks later still haven’t been contacted. I travel to NYC 10+ times a year for business and stay at Marriott every time and have never experienced this horrible customer service.

Title: Don’t stay here until they fix the elevators…
ReviewText: Only one operational elevator for 30 floors.. I booked on the phone and the representative didn’t mention it.

Title: Not worth it
ReviewText: Very old small rooms, staff was friendly but not informative or helpful. Only ONE elevator for a 30 story hotel was working, it was extremely small and made it take almost 30 minutes to leave our floor. Would not be so upset but was not informed until checking in, it added a lot of hassle getting to room and leaving hotel.

Title: Great location - Not a great experience
ReviewText: Traveling solo for business - Great location, but overall was not a good experience. The room was a bit rundown from the carpeting to the bathroom, the gym was tiny (one treadmill became a morning logjam), elevators were slow and only 1 was working. I would not recommend staying here.

Title: Disappointed
ReviewText:
Disappointed.  One of two elevators was down, yet I was not notified when booking.  Always fun doing 6 flights of stairs a few times a day to avoid the line.  In addition, the coffee machine down most of the stay.  At least put a few coffee pots out. 

We also had a big hair on our sheets when we checked the bed. 

Staff was nice, good location (close to Grand Central and on a quieter street), and the hotel seemed clean outside of the hair.

Title: Disappointed
ReviewText: The elevator was dirty. The mezzanine was not tidy. The microwave was filthy. The coffee machine was out of service for the two days that I was there. Very disappointed with the experience! The room was clean though which I appreciated!

Title: Nice location; but poor customer service/hospitality
ReviewText: Great location to Bryant Park and the New York public Library but poor customer service/ hospitality, outdated room and bathroom. No onsite restaurant, no water provided for the room, one elevator in service for sold out hotel, wasn’t notified the hotel’s elevator service would be limited, or was out of service prior to arrival, lengthy waist times for elevator,   Poor customer experience, wasn’t offered any accommodation or upgrade for being inconvenienced. Poor customer service is always inexcusable.

Title: Overpriced for Amenities
ReviewText:
We decided on a Marriott hotel for our trip primarily for ease, but this location was anything but that. One of the elevators was unfortunately down and it took 20+ minutes to get to and from the 14th floor. This factored into a lot of the decisions we made for logistics on our trip (we couldn’t stop by the hotel to freshen up because it would take an hour to get to/from the room). This was frustrating because we picked this hotel location because of its convenient location. It also took over 30 minutes to drop off our luggage (and pick it up). Additionally, we weren’t able to check into our room until 4:30pm (and check in is supposed to be at 4pm).

The beds and linens in this location were also not on par with other Courtyards that I’ve stayed at. 

Overall, a disappointing experience especially with how much we paid to stay there.

Title: Nope
ReviewText: The staff minus the manager were mostly helpful but the room was claustrophobic and there were no real amenities. There was a restaurant next door but no food or drinks other than coffee on the premises. Decent location but not worth the price tag.

Title: Dont do it!
ReviewText: This hotel is a total mess and definitely should not be a Courtyard!  Maybe, a Fairfield, but definitely not a Courtyard. The property is dated (having last been updated in 2012) and does not offer ANY of the amenities that one has come to expect from a Courtyard.  No common area, no coffee/bar area, no food service of any kind.  The room was dirty and I needed to call housekeeping to come clean up the hair on the bathroom floor when I checked in.  And on top of that one of the elevators is under renovation (hopefully repaired soon), but leaves you waiting for 10+ minutes at busy times!  I have never switched hotels mid stay, but you can guarantee I did this time!  I moved just down the street to the Courtyard Times Sq. It was nicer, newer, had all the features and crazy enough, it was less expensive (even with a last minute booking)!  If you want to feel like you are staying at a old motel on the side of the highway, then the Courtyard New York Manhattan/Fifth Avenue is the right place for you! ) 0 out of 10 stars....would not stay again!

Title: worst experience
ReviewText: This was the worst experience staying at a Marriott property. The front desk was rude, unresponsive, and one felt like I was interrupting them to help a platinum elite member. Overall, the entire facility was dirty, and only one elevator was working, and that elevator was extremely dirty.

Title: Excelente!
ReviewText: Todo excelente, ubicación, servicio!

Title: Please try any other hotel in NYC
ReviewText: I wrote a super long and detailed review in my comments of this feedback. Please copy and paste from there @MarriottCustomerService

Title: New York CIty Marathon
ReviewText: We actually had two rooms, one for me and my fiance, and one for her son, the marathon runner, and a friend.

Title: Pre Marathon stay
ReviewText: A good place to stay , close to food and transportation

Title: Single working elevator during busy marathon weekend
ReviewText: As stated before, I would have stayed elsewhere if I knew there was one single working elevator for all guest and employees during the NYC marathon weekend.  Long waits and full elevators every time you needed to use it.   Paid $1,200 for the 3 nights and being a loyal Marriott elite member, was very disappointed with my stay.

Title: A great place to stay
ReviewText: Frank and the entire staff were very helpful for helping us get around Manhattan. Even though only one elevator was working, the wait wasn’t bad.

Title: Für Städtetrip ein gutes Zimmer
ReviewText: War alles ok. Zimmer war ausreichend für 3 Personen. Service hat super geklappt. Auch die Rezeption war immer freundlich und hat geduldig alles erklärt.  Kaffe und Wasserspender sind gut für den Start in den Tag.  Ein Fahrstuhl ist etwas zu wenig für 30 Etagen. Wir haben öfter die Treppe genommen

Title: Room downgrade without reimboursment
ReviewText:
We stayed 6 nights in the Courtyard 5th avenue, the 1st night we were downgraded to a room with only 1 bed instead of the 2 beds we payed. (we were 3 persons). All the employees were everyday saying they will speak to the manager (which we never met) and in the end the announced they can't reimbourse the difference, because we paid through booking.com. That sounds like some scam, they should have been able to reimburse us by another card or even by cash. Despiite that only 1 lift is working (the second one was in a 10 months maintenance apparently but nothing seems to happen so I guess they are just saving on the money. So the morning you can wait a good 20 min if you're unlucky.
Conclusion I would recommend my friend to book something else...

Title: nightmare in Manhattan
ReviewText: Canceled my reservation while I was flying to New York City. I choose this hotel based on name and location .  The front desk seemed indifferent.  I was traveling with my adult son and the pull out sofa and so bad he ended up sleeping with his mom!   Only one elevator worked and I waited 15 minutes the first morning to go get coffee. Lot’s of employees riding the elevator at prime time. We were promised a room change after 2 days to two beds. We needed to pack all our bags and leave them in the room and staff would move them later that day. The front desk that morning had no knowledge of this situation and told us that would not happen. We left for our day of touring. When we returned we had been moved. Very little communication. My phone number was on the reservation,  this first mess up should have been resolved by a phone call. Changing the room all should have been a phone call to confirm. Poor customer care

Title: Good location
ReviewText: Hotel is in a good location close to subway lines and main tourist attractions but rooms require refurbishment and more amenities.

Title: Good location, needs updating
ReviewText:
Good location, right in 5th avenue, a few blocks from Times Square. Being down to one elevator was annoying but workable. 

The hotel rooms are tiny (even by NYC standards) and in desperate need of an upgrade

Title: Bad service.
ReviewText: No restaurants, bell hops, broken elevator, and not clean

Title: Great staff and location
ReviewText:
Raphael and Frank made our stay wonderful.
The hotel is located 5 minutes from Bryant Park and Grand Central Station

Title: Great location - beware only one working elevator
ReviewText:
This hotel is conveniently located a short walk from Grand Central Terminal. The room was clean.
The biggest downfall, there is only one (1) working elevator while they work on upgrading the other elevator.
There were long wait times and the elevator bypassing pressed floors.

Title: Non working elevators
ReviewText:
Pros-nice front desk staff, clean, great location, room was ready early

Cons-only one working elevator with long waits and by the end of our stay there was NO working elevator-we had to carry our luggage down 24 flights :(

Title: Excellent location
ReviewText: I have explained before! Typed on the first page of this survey!

Title: Terrible state of rooms. Not worth the price.
ReviewText: One of the most unfortunate experiences I have had in a hotel. The rooms (we changed one hoping for a better experience with no relief) had a slept-in/dirty cloth/soiled rug odor. The headboards were caked with grime that I cannot begin to describe. The already bad coffee maker was dirty with mold growing in it. The espresso machine in the lobby was out of order. I don't think a hotel chain affiliated to Marriott should be of this standard (unless I am wrong and update my expectations). The management and oversight department of Marriott should really take care of these things and be on top of it. I understand that this is the economy hotel sub brand but the systemic flaws were unacceptable.

Title: Credit card mix up
ReviewText: As always, we are happy to stay at Marriott properties.  I was, however, disappointed with the check-in process of this stay.  I booked the room for my son using my Marriott credit card for the reservation.  When my son arrived they required the charge to be placed on his credit card rather than the original card.  I tried to rectify the issue with a conversation with the front desk.  They stated they would send me a form allowing the charge to be returned to my card but the email with the form never arrived.  As I was planning on paying for the stay with my Marriott CC so, not only was it an inconvenience to repay my son, I also missed out on collecting the associated BonVoy points associated with the hotel stay.

Title: Broken elevator
ReviewText: I had a bad experience with the elevator however, the staff were nice. The room was great and the view was amazing.

Title: Above and beyond
ReviewText: Far exceeded my expectations.

Title: Relax and have a great time!
ReviewText: I recently traveled to New York after doing a conference in New Jersey. I stay here and it was great. I was in a great area with lots of food places in walking distance and f you're a library person the New York Public Library was right across the street. The hotel was nice and clean and smelled wonderful. There were not a lot of rooms on the floors and once you went into your room it was like you were in your own little world. I really enjoyed myself and I will stay here again if the price is right!

Title: Friendly staff, quick and easy access to work
ReviewText: The staff was friendly, offered up where I could grab breakfast and coffee quickly on my way to the office if I didn't want to stop on the mezzanine level and the room was clean and quiet.

Title: Courtyard New York Manhattan/Fifth Avenue Stay
ReviewText:
Excellent location, accessibility to transportation, central to most Manhattan attractions. 
Rooms were clean. 
Bathroom is dated and could use some upgrades. 
One of the elevators was not working causing some minor delays, but hope they fix it soon.
Overall it met expectations and definitely recommend it for anyone who is visiting Manhattan for leisure for 1st time.

Title: Wonderful stay and amazing customer service!
ReviewText: This was our first time staying in the city and we really lucked out with this hotel. It was close to a lot of restaurants and sightseeing. We also had easy access to taxis and the subway. The staff was so very friendly and helpful! We also enjoyed the fancy coffee maker that we had access to all day in the lobby. Frank, the concierge, was amazing and helped us out with transportation and directions throughout our stay. I can’t recommend this location enough!

Title: Marriott Stay
ReviewText: Mediocre stay with one elevator and no coffee.

Title: Lage top - Hotel müsste nachgebessert werden
ReviewText:
The location to Bryant Park and the metro stations was excellent. The beds were absolutely toppppp!!!!! 
Concierge (Frank) was cheerful and very helpful, always with a smile.

There isn't enough space for clothes, the bathroom is outdated.
