Absolute no-go: every night around 3 a.m. the garbage cans were emptied and all the rooms on the street side were exposed to this noise. The failure of one elevator (of 2 possible) resulted in long waiting times for the elevator. However, this information was only given to us 3 days before the start of the trip. (Information in the room: elevator outage from February to October) !!!!!!! Unimaginable given the size of a hotel like this

Title: Would not stay at this property again
ReviewText: The hotel was old and dated. This was a 30-story hotel and had two elevators, only 1 was working. It took us up to 15 mins to get to the lobby or our room. There is no food and/or beverage, just a coffee machine and the area around it was filthy. Our toilet overflowed in our room and got all over the bathroom. The mattresses are very old and uncomfortable. The air conditioning unit is very loud and can keep someone up. We stay at Marriotts all the time and I booked this hotel thinking it was a safe pick, boy was I wrong. I will never stay at this hotel again.

Title: Bad experience
ReviewText: I had a terrible experience at the Courtyard New York Manhattan/Fifth Avenue. The room was dirty and the bed was uncomfortable. The staff was rude and unhelpful. I would not recommend staying at this hotel.

Title: New York Fashion week
ReviewText: My daughter is a model, and was in a show in New York.  This hotel is in a great location, close to everything.  Will stay again.

Title: Not up to Marriott Standars
ReviewText: Would look for other Marriott properties first before booking a stay here

Title: Broken Elevator
ReviewText: Hotel is well located if you want to stay near Bryant Park but unfortunately one of the elevator was broken and the other one was consistently being used by staff/cleaning team, it took for ever to go up and down as elevator was making stops on each floor.

Title: Marriott Letdown in NYC
ReviewText: Very disappointing stay at this Marriott. Staff that didn’t care. One elevator that could not handle the load. Coffee machine didn’t work and nobody cared. Not the hotel’s fault but construction in front made it worse. The place was just totally negative. Restaurant was the worst. Horrible service. Terrible staff attitude. Poor food.

Title: Great location
ReviewText: Location was perfect for a wedding we attended at Bryant Park. Walking distance to so many attractions. Staff was extremely pleasant and helpful. I wish their was more than 2 floors with ice, and that there were vending options. There were no machines or snack bar at the front desk. They really talked up their coffee bar, but it was simply a gas station latte machine. We stayed in the  "large" King room. Decent size for downtown Manhattan,but don't expect it to be roomy. Hotel could use some updating as well.

Title: Just adequate
ReviewText: Well situated with terrible amenities and an elevator problem

Title: Good location. Clean
ReviewText: Spent 4 nights. Appreciated the location, cleanliness, hospitality of desk staff, and queen sized beds. One of the 2 elevators did not work for 2 of the days. The ice maker on the 25th floor either did not work or could not keep up with demand. If someone is a light sleeper, would avoid floors with ice makers, 5 and 25. Did not expect a view and kept the shade down for privacy. The proximity to public transportation (Grand Central Station and LIRR) was a primary reason for selecting this location. It is walking distance to multiple attractions without being directly in Times Square. There was no refrigerator in the room, but was aware of this prior to booking. Could have used a shelf in the bathroom to store items. Left hygiene bags on a towel on the floor under the sink. Like all hotels in the area, it is expensive. Despite the issues, would consider staying again if elevator and ice machine would be functional.

Title: Only one elevator for 30 floors
ReviewText:
The elevator situation at this location is currently unsafe, frustrating and a horrible experience. Only one elevator operated all weekend. 
No advance notice was provided, no alternative option were available either, and it was managed in such a way that it only aggravated the experience overall.  You cannot operate a hotel with 30 floors and have only one elevator available for all guests, staff, and visitors.  Our average wait to get up or down was 30 minutes, and each trip was crammed in a very dangerous way.  Inexcusable and horrific, and apparently its been going on for many months!  This is a shame because the staff are otherwise great and the location is fantastic

Title: Don’t stay here
ReviewText: Honestly poor experience overall - the elevator didn’t work and it was a nightmare to use. The staff is not friendly or welcoming.

Title: Friendly Staff in a Big City
ReviewText: The Staff was extremely friendly and welcoming. They welcomed us whenever we returned to the hotel and genuinely seemed to care. They were very kind and it helped to create a place of peace and home when in a very different environment from my hometown. I also loved the water available whenever we wanted it.

Title: Bom !
ReviewText: Hotel com excelente localização ! Problemas com elevador …. Tamanho do quarto que fiquei era excelente !

Title: Rooftop closed and elevator not operating
ReviewText: Hotel could have provided advance notice at booking that rooftop was closed and elevator not working.

Title: Hotel stay during the U.S. Open
ReviewText: Elevators did not work the entire time we were there, making it very difficult to get up and down to our room on the top floor. No market to get snacks and very long lines every morning to get coffee at the coffee machine. Rooms were extremely small for the price that you pay for the room.

Title: Sleep on the floor
ReviewText:
I book the reservation with my anniversary card gift.
requested double bed room since we are 3 guest to stay
after our arrival the front desk person was VERY RUDE AND UNPROFESSIONAL with us, MAKE MY SELF TO SLEEP ON THE FLOOR. I not deserved to be treated in a unprofessional matter.

Title: 1 elevator for 30 floors.
ReviewText: One of the two elevators in the hotel was down for the entirety of our stay. The one elevator that was working was constantly packed necessitating people to wait on multiple full elevators or the stairs if you were on a lower floor. You would think they would have restricted the hotel to half capacity.

Title: Wouldn’t stay here again
ReviewText: The hotel only has one working elevator for 27 floors and it takes forever to get up and down especially in the morning when everyone was leaving. No sitting area in the lobby which makes it feel crowded and chaotic. Very small coffee bar but there’s always a line and no room to even wait. General lack of attention to the hotel and rooms. No toilet paper in the ladies room and no extra pillows in the room for the pullout sofa. Phone didn’t work so I had to go down to the lobby at 11:30pm and ask to get a couple pillows sent up to the room. The location is great but unfortunately we wouldn’t stay again unless this property gets updated. Definitely not up to Marriott standard which are our ‘go to’ hotels.

Title: Don’t really recommend
ReviewText: No food or bar. Elevators are broken. Towels are terrible. Don’t stay here until after September when renovations are supposed to be done.

Title: Good location, broken elevator
ReviewText: Good location near Bryant Park and Grand Central. Room was nice and spacious. However, would not recommend until they fix the elevator.

Title: Come only to sleep without any appreciation of your elite status!
ReviewText: This is a less than a motel property!

Title: Very nice hotel
ReviewText: Very nice hotel , with kind people, confortable room and very nice location in New York

Title: Do Not Stay Here
ReviewText: The general location of the hotel is good.  Rooms are small and really dated.  Only one elevator works so expect to wait a long time, sometimes 20 minutes during busy times.  The staff is not helpful.  Other properties are better run.  I stayed at the Courtyard near Hudson Yard on 34th and it was a much better experience.

Title: Courtyard Manhattan/Fifth avenue
ReviewText:
We stayed at the Courtyard Manhattan/Fifth avenue for a quick trip to the city with my daughters. The location was great, very walkable to many locations-Times Square, Broadway Shows, Rockefeller Center, Empire State Building, Bryant Park, and the NYC public Library. Close access to subway stations as well.
The staff were very pleasant and helpful with any suggestions and helping to schedule ride service to the airports. 
The room was clean and had everything we needed.

Title: Great Summer stay every time!
ReviewText: We stay at your Manhattan/ 5th Ave  location every year. Location is great. Staff is always welcoming and the hotel is well maintained.   We will be back again next year!

Title: Really disappointed
ReviewText: Good location. That’s about it. And the staff are polite and welcoming. The actual hotel is tired and serious need of refurbishment. I would have expected much better from a Marriott. There are plenty of better options.

Title: Marriott BP
ReviewText: Exceptional hotel near Bryant Park

Title: Rude staff and no AC the coffee machine did not work
ReviewText: A/c was not working when we got the room and when we wanted to change the room the staff were rude and refused to change the room. I had request to see the manager and then they gave me a new room. The person who was managing the valet and the suitcases was rude to me at least 3 times

Title: Cumplidor
ReviewText: Bien ubicado. Personal de la puerta amistoso. Gran café !

Title: Jolly’s invaded Mahattan, the Bahamas experience
ReviewText: Love everything, we were there to have fun with the kids. We were in close proximity to every. Rode the subway, walked to Time Square many times, Caught the bus to NJ to the water park… 7 days! We expected the cost to be high but we loved it. I am a 30yr veteran in hospitality. I’m usually critical of everything but we/I thoroughly enjoyed my stay.

Title: Excellent location
ReviewText: I've stayed at this hotel several times because of the location.  Down the street from the library, Bryant Park, a few blocks from Times Square, Rockefeller Center and not too far from Central Park.  I will continue to use this hotel when I visit New York.

Title: A very good place, will come back for sure.
ReviewText: The location is perfect for walking to most of the NYC attractions. 2min walk to Bryant Park, 5min walk to Times Square, about 25-30min walk to Central Park and 45-50min walk to WTC. Easy to take Metro, easy to find many restaurants and shops!

Title: Good Stay
ReviewText: Overall good experience except for the elevator maintenance.

Title: Perfectly fine place to sleep but nothing else
ReviewText:
This Marriott is in a great location - 5 minute walk to Grand Central Station and in the center of Manhattan.
If you want a basic hotel room for a good night's sleep this is a fine place. The rooms are antiquated - furniture and especially the air conditioner which is way too noisy even set at 75. The bed was comfortable and bathroom was fine. For NYC at the price range I paid I don't expect much else but I have been in Courtyards that were far nicer. The fitness room ought to be called a fitness closet with weights up to 50# but room for one person or two small people. I wish hotels would be honest about their fitness "rooms" and include dimensions and equipment list. I believe their website said there was an extensive set of free weights which is a gross exaggeration. Otherwise the hotel was fine. Front desk staff was ruthlessly efficient which is good but a little more personality would be nice.

Title: Great location!
ReviewText: This is the perfect location! We were able to walk to a lot of places but also could easily get on the subway at grand central or Bryant park. The hotel room was more spacious than we expected and the hotel staff were friendly and helpful! We will definitely stay here again!

Title: Nice Courtyard and Location!
ReviewText: Very confortable hotel.  Rooms are better than most Courtyards.  Staff was very nice and helpful.  Great location too!

Title: Find another hotel
ReviewText: The location is good. Only one elevator was in service and that one broke down causing a missed appointment.  The staff is generally indifferent.

Title: Estadía muy cómoda
ReviewText: El hotel tiene habitaciones muy cómodas y limpias. Está muy bien ubicado.

Title: My bad experience with Marriott
ReviewText: Would not recommend this hotel location to others.

Title: A great NYC experience
ReviewText: its a great place.to stay at NYC everything is walkable distance. close to subway

Title: Great Stay, walking distance to time square!
ReviewText: Great stay, clean, friendly staff, walking distance to time square and other attractions.

Title: Not a place to get good rest.
ReviewText: I would never stay here again.

Title: Great Location, but need 2 working elevators
ReviewText: You really need to plan ahead if on upper floors and need to get places quickly.  I often waited 20 or 30 minutes and the full elevators would still stop at every floor. Great location, but beware of the time needed to get down in the mornings.

Title: Great Place to Stay
ReviewText: I was able to stay at the perfect place so I could take a continuing education course at Hospital for Special Surgery Rehabilitation Center. I did not stay long but everything was made easy to manage for a quick trip into the city.

Title: Great location and very clean
ReviewText: Great location with Bryan park steps away, very clean rooms and bathroom , staff answers pretty soon to your needs, has a small coffe -tea- water bar , the only elevator working was kind of a down but it seems the other one will be working soon .

Title: Location is perfect……
ReviewText:
This was our first time to NYC.  We met family there and enjoyed 4 days of sightseeing and excellent food while there.  For us, the placement of this hotel was perfect - we were down in Bryant Park at least once, if not twice a day.  Every morning we went there to drink coffee and plan our day while just enjoying the weather and the people.  
Super close to GCS for access to the subway, really close to the Empire State Bldg. and the huge Macy’s!   Diamond District is right down the street and while Central Park was a bit of a hike?  The City is all about walking, so it really wasn’t far at all - and you can always catch a taxi/uber/or train to get back to the hotel.  
The staff were very friendly and helpful, always ready & able to answer our questions or check to see if we needed anything.  
The beds were really comfy - we slept well every night - and we never had any issue with the room being cool enough to either be comfortable during the day or sleep well at night.  
My ‘dining’ review is due to the fact that the Cafe normally open at the hotel was not during our stay.  So we never ate there during our stay.

Title: Welcoming staff and ideal location
ReviewText: The staff (especially Frank, the valet attendant) are exceptionally friendly. The location is perfect—close to Bryant Park and Grand Central. Currently, there's only one elevator, so you might need to wait a few minutes during peak hours. I'd stay here again the next time I'm in the area!

Title: worn out room worn out property
ReviewText:
The property is in desperate need of a makeover.  The furniture was worn out, the bedding was stained, and only 1 working elevator in the building.  
We had made the reservation weeks in advance, and were only notified of the elevator work the night before.

Title: The Worst Courtyard
ReviewText: Avoid this hotel. It is dirty. The staff is indifferent. The rooms are terrible and poorly designed. To add insult to injury, it was expensive.

Title: Courtyard Manhattan 5th
ReviewText: Not my best experience at a Marriott property. It was basically a bed to sleep in for a couple nights. Nothing special about the facility. Elevator being out of service was an inconvenience, but we had a heads up. Toilet in the room didn’t fully flush on first try. Not sure if I’ll book there again if I stay in that area of NYC.

Title: Clean, friendly and the price is right!
ReviewText: We were in town for a few days to explore the city and this hotel was priced for a moderate income to be able to afford and still be within walking distance to explore everything we wanted!  It was very clean and the staff was extremely friendly and helpful!  We also loved that there was a driving company on site to set up travel when needed!

Title: My stay
ReviewText: The hotel was ok  the rooms were very warm and the 1 elevator was annoying! Willie was great he helped me a lot ! The front desk crew was ok  I don’t know the names but some were not great .

Title: I will never recommend this hotel to anyone!
ReviewText: This hotel was probably THE worst hotel I have stayed at.  I really can't believe that this Marriott hotel exists on the heart of Manhattan.  First, there was only one elevator working with no warning when I was booking the hotel.  The room and entire hotel is in major need of remodeling.  There were so many stains and broken things (I have pictures) in our room, the reception told me that no matter what room they switch us to, it will be like that.  This is ridiculous!  The bathtub was disgusting, the carpet was stained and so dirty, the headboards had fingerprints all over them, several things broken in the bathroom and the air conditioning, where do I even start?  I paid so much more to stay at this hotel, thinking its on 5th Ave, it should be good.  Well surprise surprise, it is terrible! The hotel received 15K points in return for the inconvenience, which is more of an insult than a reward.  The way I see this, I will NEVER recommend this hotel to anyone and I will make sure this review and pictures are posted everywhere.

Title: Vacation
ReviewText: Excellent very helpful will stay again

Title: Nur ein Aufzug
ReviewText: Nur ein Aufzug bis Juli, Zimmer nach hinten sehr dunkel

Title: Old wet carpet, gross.
ReviewText: This hotel doesn't conform to any Courtyard Marriot Brand standards.  Its dated, gross, smells, and the staff needs to be sent to customer service training.  It resembles a 1970's Holiday Inn.  You're Marriott status doesn't matter so, if you go there, ask for a room without old carpet where the air conditioner isn't leaking.  There is also no food or beverage service so eat before you enter.  Bring your own water because they don't provide it.  They will tell you to go to the street vendors if you want water.   Don't bother asking for a manager.

Title: NY stay
ReviewText: One elevator not appropriate for 30 floors.

Title: Great stay
ReviewText: it was a good experience. The only 2 problems I had were that as a public school teacher, I am entitled to a 10% discount but when I spoke to them they said it had to be one while booking, also when I told the receptionist I wanted help to become a Bonvoy member she said I had to call and do it.

Title: Needs improvement
ReviewText: This is the first time my family stayed at a Marriott hotel that was sub par in our opinion.  The bathroom did not smell clean at all.  There was a pungent odor of urine and dirty wet rag in the bathroom.  Thank goodness they moved us to another room.  The bathroom situation was a little better.  The shower curtain did not smell clean in this one.  Only one elevator was working and the guests had to cram themselves in to get to their rooms.  The key fob in the elevator was malfunctioning as well. The only consolation is that the front desk staff were very cordial.

Title: Worse Hotel Ever in Manhattan
ReviewText: I would stay in this hotel. There was one elevator that all guests and staff used. It took 10 to 20 minutes extra to go to and from our room every day morning to night. The bed blankets were dirty, the mirror in the bathroom was dirty, and the toilet was constantly running all day. The only thing good about this hotel was the staff. I can’t believe they charged us such a high price knowing since February 2024 that they had only one elevator. I will not recommend this hotel to anyone!

Title: Overpriced.
ReviewText: The hotel is old-fashioned. Best location, very safe, but the hotel and the room is very very basic. High price, poor hotel.

Title: Bon rapport qualité prix
ReviewText: Emplacement idéal et confort suffisant pour 5 jours

Title: Super Hotel
ReviewText: Tolles Hotel, super gelegen in Midtown. Man kann vieles zu Fuss sehen gehen und trotzdem ruhig seitlich gelegen. Sehr nette Mitarbeiter!

Title: Highly NOT Recommended
ReviewText: Wait until renovations are completed and then consider.

Title: Needs a refresh and a Fitness Room that can accommodate more than 2 people.
ReviewText:
The Fitness Center is a joke.  It’s way too small.  It can only accommodate 2 or 3 people at one time.

The bathroom in my room needs an upgrade.

Location is great and the staff is fantastic.

Title: OK For a short stay
ReviewText: We stayed one night in Manhattan at the courtyard by Marriott Fifth Avenue. The room was adequate for our stay, the hotel is conveniently located, the room is clean, but could be cleaner. I did notice some areas where it wasn’t clean. The heater/cooler in the room was constantly running, and very loud made it difficult to sleep, if you are a light sleeper, please take note of this as I had a hard time getting a good night sleep because of the noise.

Title: Stay was Fine
ReviewText: It was fine.  Conveniently located.  Could use face lift.

Title: Très bon séjour
ReviewText: Hôtel très bien situé. Propre. Personnel aimable et très serviable. Les interventions dont nous avons eu besoin dans la chambre ont été gérées très efficacement

Title: Simple et efficace
ReviewText: Une bonne adresse en plein Manhattan!

Title: POOR QUALITY AND STAFF
ReviewText: EVERYTHING THAT COULD BE WRONG MINUS THAT THERE WAS  BED WAS WRONG!!!

Title: Great visit
ReviewText: Great location, staff was great.  Basic hotel but very clean. Would definitely stay there again

Title: Easy access to Manhattan and broader New York
ReviewText: Times square and Empire State and number of other attractions in walking distance and easy access to metro network. Don’t forget to enjoy Byrant Park next to the hotel.

Title: Pleasant Stay in Good Location
ReviewText: Initial thoughts were good, modern looking reception albeit small. Room was very disappointing, everyones hearts sank when we opened the door, small, many,many aircon units just below the window which kept us awake all night, tiny bed. Given we had asked 7 months earlier when we booked it for a high up room it was disappointing. Staff changed us, but seemed a little put out and didn't say 'sorry about that', which is the least I expected having just told them none of us had slept all night due to the noise. Next room was very different, larger beds, plenty of light, a  lot higher up.

Title: Familienausflug
ReviewText:
Das Hotel ist wirklich sehr gut gelegen… 5 Minuten vom Grand Central Terminal. 
2 Minuten bis zum Bryant Park. 
10 Minuten bis zum Rockefeller Center. 
Alles gut zu Fuß erreichbar. 
Hotel war sauber, Handtücher wurden jeden Tag gewechselt, 
Zu Ostern gab es ein kleines Geschenk für unsere Tochter.
the tv yolume was too low even when set at 70.

Title: Nice option close to offices
ReviewText:
The main reason I chose this hotel was how close it is to the office and that it is walking distance to most places I would need/want to go on my business trip.

Pros:
* Distance to office.
* There is a place to get food right by the main entrance and nearby.
* Good price
* Clean room
* Great hotel staff

Cons:
* The only drink options available is a single coffee/hot chocolate machine and filtered water you can fill your bottle with. 
* No in hotel food available

Keep in mind that this is a New York hotel so if this is your first visit like it was mine you may be a little shocked by how small the room is. I stayed in a couple for this visit since I went from business to personal to give me time to explore. The room is a pretty standard size so expect about the same no matter where you stay unless you really want to pay for a larger room.

Title: Staff was very helpful
ReviewText: The gentleman at check in (unfortunately I did not get his name) was very helpful and took the time to look up directions for me, right down to which train I needed and which stop to get off of and then where I needed to walk. The concierge, Pedro, was the nicest man. He was so busy and was still so friendly. At checkout, it was taking a little while for valet to bring my car and he held my luggage and told me to walk around and he'd call me. When my car arrived he called and told me to take my time getting back. He had my luggage in my car for me when I got back. Super sweet guy! My room was clean and my bed was comfortable.

Title: Inoperable thermostat.
ReviewText: Nice hotel. Great staff. Clean room.  Inoperable thermostat.

Title: Smooth Sailing
ReviewText: The hotel was just right for our stay. It was clean, helpful and convenient.

Title: Didn’t have house keeping service one day
ReviewText: Didn’t have house keeping service one day, that’s the first time we had a such bad experience.

Title: parfait
ReviewText: séjour très agréable, literie très confortable, emplacement idéal pour rayonner sur Manhattan

Title: Great location
ReviewText: Nice, clean hotel in a central location of Manhattan. Only has water and coffee in the lobby but a wonderful café right next-door.

Title: Great location
ReviewText:
Great location, close
To everything, but not in a crowded area. Small fitness center, but great staff and clean

Title: Well below Marriott standards
ReviewText: Very poor hotel and therefore poor value. No food on site at all, no available water bottles, not even a glass in the room for tap water, thermostats in the room locked out, and finally the furnishings are downright ancient. The dresser and the desk have in my room has drill holes that on the surface. Ridiculous for $309 per night….owner should be ashamed as should Marriott.

Title: Just what we needed
ReviewText: We had a great stay! Location was perfect and allowed for easy access to the subway, walking distance to Times Square and restaurants. I’d stay here again!

Title: February 2024
ReviewText: My only critique is that the coffee service was slow due to the one cup machine that they have. There was a line every morning. Because the café was being renovated, we didn’t have that option.

Title: Great location, clean, at a great price
ReviewText: Perfect location to get to Broadway, Times Square, 5th Avenue, and Grand Central Station. Close restaurants. Great place to stay and see the city.

Title: Excellent stay
ReviewText: overall excellent experience

Title: Plain Jane Hotel stay
ReviewText: Great location, but lacks the 'wow factor' of a Marriott Hotel.

Title: Outdated hotel
ReviewText: Carpets stained and ripped. In need of a complete makeover. Elevator wait was 15 minutes in the morning. No sheer blinds. Not comfortable at all. Would never stay again. No lobby lounge.

Title: A really great hotel option for everything Mid-town
ReviewText:
We chose to stay here based on the excellent location...near 5th Ave just steps away from the iconic NYC Public Library, and just a short walk to Grand Central, Rockefeller Center/Radio City, Empire State Building, and more.

The check-in process was very easy with no surprises.  There is a really convenient mezzanine self service complimentary coffee bar with hot tea and a automated, touch screen operated coffee machine that can make just about any coffee/latte/espresso/hot chocolate drink you could want! That perk alone saved us at least $100 from what we likely would have spent at Starbucks during the trip!

There are only 7 rooms per floor, so getting to the rooms was a breeze and the elevators worked well.  The rooms were average size for NYC, but the bathrooms were spacious, which was a big help with getting ready for the day with our family.

Beds were comfortable and the AC/Heat worked well with a digital thermostat.  It was cool during our stay so we even opted to open the windows (which only open about 4-5inches..just enough to ventilate excelently).  Our room was on the corner closest to 5th ave on floor 27 and we had a nice view of the city.

The area around the hotel was clean, free from any "sketchy" characters, and quiet in the evenings.  There is a pizza place (Upside Pizza) just across the street that also had some of the best pizza we've had on our many trips to NYC.  

The walk to the Library and Bryant Park is literally steps away (def see the library...it's spectacular and FREE).  Bryant Park is great during the winter, and the Bryant Park Grill is a phenomenal restaurant. Grand Central is about a 5 minute walk, Times Square is maybe a 7min walk.

The Concierge staff/Bellmen are all great...especially Pedro who helped us with the obscene amount of luggage we had and also arranged luxury SUV car service to the airport for our departure.

We will definitely add this hotel to our list of places to stay next time we return to NYC

Title: A really great stay in NYC at an ideally located hotel! Thank you Courtyard 5th Ave
ReviewText: We were visiting family for a solemn event but needed a few days after that to also enjoy the city.  We found the right hotel which allowed us to tick all the items in the bucket list except for snow!  But having Bryant Park and  the rink was close enough!  Thank you to the staff at Courtyard 5th Ave for being so helpful too!

Title: wanting my money
ReviewText: didn't return my money, and very disappointed

Title: Courtyard New York Manhattan/Fifth Avenue
ReviewText: Great location, very nice staff, not many amenities, older looking room, but very comfortable.

Title: Service is okay
ReviewText: I appreciate that when we call for room service early in the morning, a nice gentleman came and clean our room . He was very kind and did an awesome job! Thank you

Title: Gets the job done
ReviewText: Excellent location, friendly staff, clean room. Room was small but larger than other price-comparable hotels I've stayed at in this area. Business center and gym are tiny. Overall a good stay all things considered.

Title: Great location... hotel needs some love.
ReviewText: This hotel is in a great location to get around Manhattan and to get a bite. However, it really needs renovations. There is no "market" or food on-premises. When i asked where I could get a soda, i was directed to CVS 1.5 blocks away. That's annoying when you want grab a bite on your way to meetings or when you arrive late in the evening.

Title: Family Weekend
ReviewText: Great place! Clean Ann friendly.

Title: Nice location, poor customer service
ReviewText: The hotel is well located, the room size, furniture and accessories are descent. However some personnel from front desk are not polite and they forget you are the customer. I was so disappointed about it. I never will back to this hotel

Title: A great find
ReviewText: Affordable excellent NYC option.

Title: NY24
ReviewText: The hotel was business efficient and ideally located but lacked breakfast options.

Title: Our go-to spot for NYC visits
ReviewText: The big plus is location, location, location! Convenient to Broadway theatres, transportation, restaurants, etc. It's not fancy, but it's clean and comfortable. Staff is very pleasant and responsive, too.

Title: Great stay in NYC
ReviewText: Great location. Comfortable stay.

Title: Convenient
ReviewText: The location was excellent! We were close to or within walking distance of all the places we wanted to see. Grand central nearby for travel by train. Plenty of eating establishments nearby. Definitely plenty of shopping!  Our next trip back we are definitely staying here again!

Title: This was the most uncomfortable
ReviewText: I prefer to stay in Marriott but never had issues with beds an mattresses

Title: Worst Hotel in the City
ReviewText: Talk about gross and dirty.  Public hairs in the bathroom from prior guests meant we didn’t even shower the next morning.

Title: Only good thing was Location
ReviewText: Platinum for Life Member. Stayed in 100’s hotels. Way better options in NYC.

Title: Poor Service
ReviewText: I would not recommend this particular Marriott and this is a first for me, I have not had such a not so good experience with other locations. The space throughout the hotel is very small and it feels like you are crowded in the common areas. But the staff that was there on my arrival was not as I expected. As my gift which I normally receive when I check in was 4 bottles of the cheapest water of which I would never drink. The area where the coffee and tea were was an absolute mess and no one was there to clean or clear the garbage on a constant basis. I was very disappointed to say the lease.

Title: Great location and price for small room in the city.
ReviewText: Friendly staff, clean room, nice coffee and tea bar with expresso in the morning. Small room but that's fine since not visiting to sit in the room all day and night. Perfect location for access to Bryant Park, Time Square, and shopping.

Title: Extremely Disappointing Stay at Courtyard Hotel
ReviewText:
I want to express my extreme dissatisfaction with my recent stay at the Courtyard hotel. Frankly, this establishment falls far below the standards I expect from a Marriott property.

Pros:
1. Location: The only positive aspect was its proximity to main attractions.

Cons:
1. Lobby: Unbelievably small, reminiscent of a motel rather than a Marriott hotel in NYC. It was consistently overcrowded, offering nowhere to stand, and was an underwhelming experience overall.
2. Check-in: Despite being a Marriot Bonvoy Platinum Elite member, my 2 pm check-in request was mishandled. The front desk initially claimed my room wasn't ready at 3:40, even though the regular check-in time is 4:00 pm. Only after questioning this did they miraculously find a room.
3. Room: The small, dirty, dark, and outdated room left me uncomfortable. The state of the bathroom was equally disappointing.
4. Valet: I had to search for assistance, adding an unnecessary hassle to the check-in process. When I finally found someone to valet my car, they handed me a paper and casually mentioned that I needed to call 45 minutes in advance to retrieve my vehicle. Following their instructions upon departure, I called the front desk as directed. However, upon arriving in the crowded lobby 45 minutes later, my car was not ready. The valet attendants were engaged in a heated argument, further adding to the delay. Eventually, I had to intervene and inquire about my car, only to be handed the keys and informed that my vehicle was parked across the street. This lack of coordination and the inconvenience of having to walk across the street with my bags, without any offered assistance, reflected a disorganized and unprofessional valet service, contributing significantly to the overall negative experience.

In summary, my stay at this Courtyard hotel was a complete disaster. I paid over $600 a night for a subpar experience, and I would strongly advise against choosing this hotel. My expectations for a Marriott property were sorely unmet, and I have no intention of returning.

Title: Valet parking awful
ReviewText: Please put more bathroom stalls in lobby area. And train valet guy to be better.

Title: Experience
ReviewText: Bad experience and the room was not as I expected

Title: Awesome motel with great location!
ReviewText: Great motel convenient location. Clean, kind and helpful staff.

Title: Last resort
ReviewText: With so many choices this should be a last resort. Location is good. Only 4 blocks from the empire state building

Title: Lackluster Experience
ReviewText:
How this hotel classifies itself as a Courtyard is a head-scratcher. No business center, cafe to get food and a gym that is smaller than most home gyms. 

By far the worst Courtyard I’ve ever stayed in which says a lot since I stayed nearly 3k nights at a Marriott property. 

I will never stay there again.

Title: Perfect location and Great staff!
ReviewText:
This was our first trip to New York and we wanted to walk to everything. This hotel was perfect for that! We walked to Rockefeller, Times Square, Gershwin, The Empire State Building,  Grand Central Station, Central Park, Bryant Park, Macy's and all of the cute little restaurants in between! There was a train just a block over for all of the places we couldn't walk. 
The staff was very friendly and helpful especially when our flight was delayed and we needed to stay an extra night. The concierge helped us to secure a car to the airport. 
I would definitely stay here again and highly recommend anyone else to as well!

Title: Solid Stay
ReviewText: My daughter and I had a quick girls trip- this location is great! I know the city well so it’s an easy block to the F train. Great restaurants in the area and adjacent to Bryant Park. Pleasant staff and the room was adequate for what we needed.

Title: Michalene
ReviewText: Outstanding, would stay there again, and again.

Title: Better suited for business travel
ReviewText: The hotel room was small for 2 adults and 1 child. There is no counter space in the bathroom. And there is no waiting area in the lobby.

Title: I expected Marriott quality, but I've stayed in better rooms at Motel 6
ReviewText: This facility was way overpriced for the quality of the room.  At $850 per night, I expected a nice room.  What I got was a tiny, dated room with tiny dated furniture and zero amenities.  I honestly don't know how this carries the Marriott brand name.  I left feeling like I got mugged at Bryant Park.

Title: Awesome location!
ReviewText: Hotel was very clean and location could not be beat! Loved the valet parking and that you needed your room key to access the elevator.

Title: Stay Somewhere Else!
ReviewText:
Rarely do I leave negative reviews, but if you are traveling to NYC and looking for a hotel/Marriott .... stay anywhere else! This location is the equivalent of a college frat house (think 1990s heavily worn out). I traveled with two young children and my elderly father, we stayed for one night. 
I realize "Courtyard" is on the lower end for the Marriot brands, but I hold the Marriot name to a standard regardless. The room we were placed in was well below this expectation. Cleanliness, outlets not working, carpet littered with thick layer of dust, food crumbs, debris, exposed metal on bedframe that cut my arm (while trying to find working outlet).
I called Marriott Bonvoy regarding this after checkout, and the next day the location manager emailed offering a 28% refund. I replied to him sharing the entirety of our experience and seeking a more appropriate refund. No reply ...

So many Marriot options in NYC - stay somewhere else.

Title: Great location for NYC work.
ReviewText: best location in the city.  quiet room and easy to walk to central station.  staff is helpful too.

Title: Best Bucket list Trip ever!! Walkable to all the sites we wanted to see.
ReviewText:
We planned a bucket list trip for some of the girls in our family-
The main concern was to hit all the sites our 84-year young grandmother wanted to see.  This location provided a great stay and more important it was convenient to all the places and things we wanted to do. We never used the subway once but if we wanted to it was very conveniently located.  Happy to say we checked all the things off our list!!

Title: Great place to stay in Manhattan
ReviewText: We were all pleased with our stay. The hotel is basic, but it has what you need: Great very central location, clean well maintained rooms, a Lavazza coffee machine in the lobby that makes both brewed coffee and some espresso type drinks  and a selection of teas, and a fitness center.  Validated parking 2 blocks away was $40 per 24 hours which is great for Manhattan (this could have been clearer on the website, but I texted the front desk and they told me what to do).  We would be happy to return on our next trip to New York!

Title: Excellent location and value!
ReviewText: Have stayed at this hotel several times. Always clean and comfortable, the staff is excellent, and the location cant be beat!

Title: Great location
ReviewText: Courtyard on 5th worked well for a recent weekend visit that included a trip to Long Island for a baby shower.  I chose the hotel for its excellent location, close to all forms of transportation, and NOT in the fray of Times Square.  Hotel is comfortable and clean, though the lobby and shared areas are a bit run down and the elevators too small for that many floors.  I would stay there again.

Title: Good location and excellent service!
ReviewText: Good location, well maintained hotel, and excellent staff!

Title: Good place to stay.
ReviewText: This hotel is in a convenient location for me and the rooms are clean and quiet.

Title: Nice stay right next to Bryant Park
ReviewText: This was a nice stay at the Courtyard  by Marriot across from Bryant Park.  Within walking distance of everything we needed to get to.

Title: Fair to middling
ReviewText: If you can connect with Sandar Kyaw to act as your advocate, you will be happier during your experience at Marriott Courtyard Bryant Park, NYC. It is a small footprint hotel with no restaurant. Dated interior, cleanliness not so great.  Good location and not highly expensive.

Title: NYC Weekend Getaway
ReviewText:
Took my wife to NYC for a long weekend for her birthday.
It was a great location and we enjoyed our stay - only thing that would have made this "excellent" instead of "good" was the lack of a mini refrigerator and no snack/amenities area at the front desk.

Title: Great service and terrific location
ReviewText: My adult daughter and I spent a long weekend in NYC. This Marriott was very clean, provided a sizable room with two beds, was in a very convenient location (close enough to Times Square but away from the crowds) and most importantly provided exceptional service. I recommend it highly. FYI, I don’t usually do reviews unless the experience is terrific.

Title: Acceptable NYC hotel
ReviewText: While not horrible, my recent stay at this property did not rise to the standards I'v come to expect from the Bonvoy brand.   The property was clean and services available but they failed at a few key details that I'm used to experiencing.  I'm genuinely hoping that my stay was on an "off time" for them and the circumstances were beyond their control.

Title: The Staff make your stay great
ReviewText: Typical small rooms for NY, a little dated decor, no food service, but the staff really make this hotel great. The location is great to get around on food or by train.

Title: Enjoyed my stay
ReviewText: Courteous staff, clean room, quick and efficient check-in and check-out.

Title: Not like other Marriott locations- must be a franchise location
ReviewText: Furniture old and damaged. Carpet is old. Smallest room I have ever been for a Courtyard. Staff "hangs out" in the tiny lobby. Staff doesn't like to use the stairs to go one floor up or down so always on the elevators. No complimentary breakfast...not even bagels on a tray. Recognizing it is NYC it is still overpriced for what you get.

Title: Avis
ReviewText:
L'hôtel est très propre, les chambres sont suffisamment grandes , l'accueil est chaleureux et le petit déjeuner est au top.
Seul point négatif c'est la situation de l'hôtel qui peut effrayer car dans une zone industrielle mais ça peut rassurer si on prend un uber ou taxi environ 10usd pour se rendre à l'arrêt de station de métro le plus proche à 5mn , je conseille le téléphérique roosevelt Island car 3 mn de Manhattan

Title: Book it NOW!!
ReviewText:
We were so happy with this hotel! The staff were so friendly and always welcomed us back from a busy day with a smile and asked how our day was. 
Yummy cocoa! We were 3 females traveling so we were concerned with our safety and we never felt concerned. Keys needed to operate elevators. 
They even arranged a ride for us to the airport!
Clean rooms, bathroom was great. 
Do not hesitate, book it now!!

Title: It was an incredible stay for my family
ReviewText: Exceptional stay, spotless room, friendly and helpful reception. Perfect for families seeking a fantastic experience

Title: Review
ReviewText: Good and would recommend stay at this hotel.

Title: Viaje soñado
ReviewText: Fuimos en viaje de un día y no pudimos elegir un mejor lugar para conocer NY

Title: Jerrod Everett
ReviewText: It was not a good experience. There was no mention of a "Hold fee" and the room was supposed to be a deluxe king bed, but it was. standard one.

Title: One night in New York
ReviewText: General good but below my preferred standard

Title: No bottled water provided
ReviewText: Not only is no bottled water provided non is available for purchase as well.  I was directed to a water machine on the mezzanine level that did not function properly as an alternative solution.  Unacceptable.  It's the hotel's job to provide water to their guests.

Title: Too expensive
ReviewText: The hotel was too expensive for the lack of basic amenities. I would not recommend staying here.

Title: Manhattan
ReviewText:
Very pleasant evening 
I’d stay again

Title: Serviceable at best
ReviewText: This is a clean place to sleep, no amenities, no character virtually no service.

Title: Perfect Location
ReviewText: It was a very pleasant stay, very affordable price, the staff was very friendly and the location is PERFECT. Walking distance to the Grand Central Terminal, Times Square, Rockefeller & the Empire State.

Title: Location is the only thing this hotel has
ReviewText: Rooms are tiny, bed was hard as a rock, fitness facility was the size of a shoe box, staff was cordial, but not too helpful.

Title: Overpriced, loud, and intrusive.
ReviewText: It was nearly impossible to sleep through the night with the amount of door slamming that took place. Primarily from the staff.

Title: Location
ReviewText: You pay for the Good location, there is no restaurant.  It is dated. To me, it will be just a bed to sleep at night.  I wish they have some welcome amenities for elite member.  The employees work with what they have.

Title: Blood stained sheets
ReviewText: Worst experience ever. From not having valet parking at the times needed , Blood stained sheets left on bed from previous occupant to being turned into a maid  at 12:30 am because they don’t have staff.

Title: Great location. Spotless room.
ReviewText: Great location for MSG, shopping, theater. Super friendly, helpful staff. 50% of parking with voucher (!!!). Got early check in.  Room was spotless, quiet and comfortable, but needs updating. Would come again.

Title: AMAZING Trip & Stay
ReviewText: My stay at the Courtyard Marriott Fifth Avenue was lovely; if you want a hotel in a great position when visiting NYC, this hotel is the best in terms of location. The staff is nice and helpful, specially Frank; if you want to know what to do, where to eat, where to go and what to visit, Frank is the person you want to talk to. My room was clean, thanks to Casilda who did a great job cleaning our room during our 7 nights stay, but I wish the rooms were more modern. Nonetheless, the room did the job for the time we were there.  I would totally recommend this hotel for anyone visiting New York city, especially firstimers.

Title: A basic hotel
ReviewText: The location is good as it is close to Time Square but that is about it. No elite benefits (except for the  500 points), no upgrade, no onsite restaurant/bar etc. It is more like a motel with a tiny room.

Title: Not A Typical Courtyard Stay!
ReviewText:
This property is NOT where you go for good customer service and Courtyard's usual amenities.  There was no bar or restaurant service.  Business center consisted of one work station. The lobby area was absolute minimum, consisting of one 3-person couch/sofa positioned less than 8 feet from the entrance.
I would not recommend this property.

Title: Nice hotel, well situated
ReviewText: Nice hotel close to grand central and other attractions. Room clean and bigger than most NYC hotels.  Easy stay.

Title: There's room for improvement
ReviewText: Housekeeping came by at 8:30 in the morning on the first day. We were still asleep and they didn't come back. On the second morning, they never came so I had to ask the front desk for housekeeping. On the last morning, they came again at 8:30am and then ask me at what time was my check out, which is absolutely ridiculous because it's not before 11am, same as everyone. At 10:45am I came back from breakfast and my key no longer worked. Not impressed, very disorganized. Porters were very helpful though.

Title: Well placed locality
ReviewText: Great place, good rooms unfriendly staff

Title: Lavazza coffee station available 24/7 was key
ReviewText: Many good things at this property. Physical location is idea for getting around, but 5th Avenue is very noisy 24/7

Title: Average
ReviewText:
Water pressure is very week

No restaurants

Title: Great Location
ReviewText:
Hotel is in a great location. Close to so many things and within walking distance to Times Square , Grand Central Terminal , New York Library, shopping on fifth and subway. 
They also had my room ready for me a few hours earlier which was great. 
Only thing was I wished when I checked in they would have told me about the coffee machine , and transportation they offer. No one told me until I was leaving.

Title: Good location, simple clean rooms.
ReviewText: Friendly staff from check in/out, room was small but the bathroom was surprisingly well laid out. It is on a very noisy street, if your want more peace, try getting a room higher up.

Title: Sofa looked like a fraternity house
ReviewText: We were in town to get our son off to college, and decided to take the long weekend to see New York.  The location is great.  But if you have more than two people in your party, this is not a good facility.  From what we could tell the largest room they have is a king with a sofa bed.  The sofa bed mattress in our room looked like it came from a fraternity house with urine or vomit stains (couldn't quite tell) and a weird smell.  When I inquired about another room I was told they were sold out and there were no other rooms.  So the risk factor of being stuck with the vomit sofa again isn't a risk I would take.  So for a couple, it's a great location.  For a family - definitely not a good choice.

Title: City visit
ReviewText: This was mine and my two young adult children’s first time in the City.  Great location. A couple things I wish were better explained- elevator access and wifi. I already knew the room would be small - cool view, especially at night.  Not loving the bathroom - considering it’s a four person capacity room, not enough space to hang towels, rags and of course, counter space (I know, small room). Also, saw a bit of mildew/mold in the shower. Water pressure and temperature control was good.  Great staff, friendly, professional, helpful.  Loved the Mezzanine that allowed us to refill our water bottles and get hot drinks and hot water.  Being able to check in a little early was awesome for our plans and we were also able to leave our bags for several hours after check out.

Title: Comfortable convenient and friendly
ReviewText:
Staff were friendly.  The rooms were clean and comfortable.  Good wifi, good selection of hot drinks available any time was a bonus (although not very allergy friendly and no fridge to store alternative milks for example but that wasn't a problem for us).  The gym was compact but had everything for needed for a general work out.  

The room was a good base for our children who needed the city experience paced for them.  We requested a later check out and this really helped the kids to manage a final long day before our flight home late at night.

Baggage storage facilities helpful and the staff were kind and friendly.

Title: Fun fast and simple
ReviewText: Everything at the hotel was great. It was a fast and simple check in and out. That made it even easier to have fun in the city and enjoy my time with my family.

Title: Great value, location, and staff!
ReviewText: This hotel is in a great location in Midtown East, half a block from major streets. Easy access to the subway and buses. The staff were all very nice and helpful. You can tell that they really care about your stay. The hotel was very clean! We felt very safe. The street was relatively quiet, but we could step outside and hail a cab within a minute or two. No need to wait on an Uber. We met nice people everywhere we went!

Title: Solid Manhattan option
ReviewText: This Courtyard by Marriott is well-located with attentive staff. The rooms are small with basic amenities, but it met our needs as a family.

Title: Excellent stay in New York!
ReviewText: My families first time in New York and we stayed downtown next to all of the action.  My family absolutely loved the experience and the hospitality

Title: Great location and value!
ReviewText: Our go-to hotel for NYC visits. Clean, comfortable, well-located, with friendly and helpful staff.

Title: Good Location, Tired Decor
ReviewText: People were really nice. Location was great. Beds were comfortable, but carpeting was old and furniture needed updates. Still not a bad place to stay.

Title: NYC trip
ReviewText: The staff was friendly and the location was great for our planned activities.

Title: Be near it all in the Big Apple!
ReviewText: Hotel room was spacious by NYC standards. Location was convenient for everything and out of the way. I enjoyed our stay and would consider staying again if in the NYC.

Title: Overnight Stay
ReviewText: Highly recommend. Great location for seeing the city

Title: Check the mattress on bed and sofa
ReviewText: I checked  the sofa bed mattress  it Was nasty  next time I will  check both.I  didn't check the bed mattress  because I always sleep on the top sheet. Next time I will.

Title: Excellent room and staff superb location
ReviewText: The hotel was in a great location for sight-seeing.  The room itself was a very good size, the mattresses were incredibly comfortable and the bathroom was aso a good size and well equipped.  When I raised an issue about a broken blind and locking myself out of our room safe, both issues were resolved immediately.  The staff were incredibly helpful and friendly.  I would definitely stay again and recommend to other travellers.

Title: Good stay with close proximity to Times Square
ReviewText: Good for 5th Avenue, Times Square, Empire State Building, Grand Central Terminal….

Title: Satisfactory
ReviewText: It was average because this hotel is dated. Score increased because they did leave snacks in the room & have complimentary coffee.

Title: Almost good
ReviewText: Clean, comfortable, great coffee machine, tv barely works so if u like winding down watching a comedy show it’s not gonna happen

Title: buen servicio
ReviewText: en terminos generales Bien. pero devieron permitirme pagar en efectivo al momento del check in

Title: Old outdated
ReviewText: I have stayed at a courtyard before and have been happy, but this experience will make me think twice in the future before booking.

Title: Don’t bother!
ReviewText: Best part of this place is the location. Otherwise, it’s outdated, loud, in need of serious upkeep and uncomfortable. As a OneKey AND Bonvoy GOLD member, I do not recommend this place unless you’re a tourist who only cares about a convenient location ‼️

Title: Pedro is the best!
ReviewText: We had a great stay here at the 5th Ave property. Pedro at the front door was incredibly helpful. He was kind, warm and went out of his way to make sure we had everything we needed. He is a huge part of what made our trip so positive and our stay so good.

Title: Good value.
ReviewText: This a great place to stay n NYC. The location is perfect. The beds are comfy and the place is clean. Best of all, it’s quiet. The employees are attentive.

Title: Not worth it
ReviewText: Sad to say this is more equivalent to a red roof inn is the closest I can imagine. Staff was nice and coffee machine in lobby was great but that is all. Very awful linens and towels and yet paying a premium price. View of a roof and an office directly about 20ft from the window. Dark room and had to keep shades closed bc of the office. There are many other fun hotels in the area for same price I recommend choosing those.

Title: Good place to stay midtown
ReviewText:
The rooms were a good size, we had two rooms, one with 2 queen beds (a little snug but reasonable) and the other the larger guest room with king and sofa bed. Chose this room for the extra space for the kids to be able to spread out. I forgot to initially ask for a refrigerator, but they were able to provide one on the 2nd day of our week long stay. The bellhop helped us up with our bags and provided a few tips during our stay. 
Prior to arriving I asked if both rooms can be on the same floor and they told me there is no guaranty but they'll try and then when we arrived our rooms were a few doors down on the same floor. Extra star for that. The buildings are close to other buildings and our view was looking in windows of other office buildings.
The elevator was scary sometimes. There are only 2 and sometimes you have to wait a while. Sometimes it would bounce or stop on a floor and the door not open, but then it would continue and be ok.
They have available coffee, tea and hot chocolate 24/7, which is nice. 
The front desk people were good and answered questions on the area.
They validate your parking ticket if you park at the garage they are associated with. The garage attendants were accommodating and did their job. 
The location was great for subway access and walking to many sites; and lots of eateries and coffee shops in walking distance.

Title: A great place to stay near Bryant Park
ReviewText: We enjoyed our stay very much! The hotel was clean and guest services was very accommodating to our needs. We were allowed to get into one of our rooms early when we arrived before check-in time! That was so helpful! The location of this hotel was perfect for our stay. We could walk to so many restaurants and shows and anything we wanted to do farther away, we could just use the subway station that was so close! It was a great trip! I would stay there again!!

Title: Centrally located, but nothing extra
ReviewText: On the positive, the hotel is centrally located, clean, and the staff was nice; and that admittedly is most important.  On the negative, there wasn’t a location to buy drinks or snacks.  Fortunately a CVS was only a few blocks away, so not too big of a deal.

Title: Fantastic location and staff
ReviewText: We were immediately greeted by very friendly staff on checkin.  We were early and the room was not available but they promptly stowed our bags so we could start our vacation.  The room, mezzanine, lobby, etc was very clean!  I almost gave up on Courtyard after bad experiences in MD and VA.  My family will be returning here as our base in the city.

Title: Great location, decent amenities, bathrooms were not great
ReviewText: Staff was amazing, location was fantastic. The hotel is showing its age. The bathroom curtain smelled like pee so strongly and there was mold on the walls.

Title: Stay at marriott 5th avenue
ReviewText: Excellent customer service and staff!

Title: Super happy!
ReviewText: We were all very impressed with the professional and very welcoming/friendly staff. The coffee and tea machine was awesome. We also really appreciated the very clean room. We were a long way from our home (Maui, HI) but we felt comfortable and taken care of. We will be back! Mahalo nui loa!

Title: Terrible stay
ReviewText: Terrible experience, I booked a 2 queen bed room, ended up with 2 full bed the most, tiny beds. Requested extra towels from app that never showered up. No fridge in the room either. Elevator broke two times during stay. Shower gel is irritating that caused discomfort to my friend and my. Will never stay there again

Title: Happy
ReviewText: Everything worked out for two girls traveling to NY. The location was great near Bryant Park. The room rate was good, I would stay there again.

Title: Great location
ReviewText: Our stay was amazing. The staff were extremely friendly and made us feel right at home, specially Willie. Every time he would see us coming he had the elevator waiting and high fives for the kids and small talk with us. This hotel was near to everything we wanted to do and see. 6 block from Empire State Building, 2 blocks for time square and that beautiful library on 5th was pretty neat.

Title: You can find better locations to stay .
ReviewText: Over all an average experience.

Title: ok
ReviewText:
The room rate was misleading..250 a night for Sunday & Monday 450 for Wednesday!

I was there for a meeting but had my 15 yr old daughter with me.

Title: Birthday Celebration
ReviewText: The staff was amazing, I loved it!

Title: Excellent Stay. Great Location, Great Staff.
ReviewText: Our stay was excellent. The hotel is very well located, close to everything. And the staff was excellent too.

Title: Review
ReviewText: This is a clean barebones hotel. Probably a good deal. If you are not planning to stay in your room while in the city.

Title: Hotel Stay
ReviewText: Staff friendly, no drinking water, hard mattress, dirty shower curtain, requested room be cleaned twice in a 5 day stay and room was not cleaned

Title: Will not recommend
ReviewText: Will not recommend it to anyone

Title: Solid hotel choice in a GREAT location
ReviewText:
This was a very good hotel choice in a great location.
Staff were all excellent and the hotel felt secure and clean. 

The room was just a bit ... used, but not bad in any way. Everything was clean and the view from near the top was great.

We would gladly stay there again.

Title: Great location, decent hotel
ReviewText: Location was excellent. But for what we paid, it was a no frills hotel otherwise. Staff was very good

Title: Yes
ReviewText: Good trip good place good people working there, very good in general

Title: Lackluster for a Marriott hotel
ReviewText: The room was small and smelled of mold. Basic bathroom amenities were not provided (i e. Shampoo, lotion). The bathroom smelled of mildew and the shower had mold that had been painted over. Turndown services was incomplete for two out of the six days. I've stayed at many Marriotts and this hotel did not meet my experience.

Title: Great place to stay
ReviewText: Great hotel just far enough away from Times Square that you are away from the noise.  Excellent location beside Bryant Park and across from the library.  The hotel is a bit dated but it is clean and comfortable and the staff are really friendly and helpful.

Title: Location
ReviewText: The location of this hotel is phenomenal almost every where we wanted to go was walking distance..less then a mile .. and grand central station is a few blocks away so easy access to subways and trains to get to anywhere you wanted to go ..

Title: Great place to stay at reasonable rates!
ReviewText: Everything was perfect, and the staff was helpful with directions and suggestions.

Title: Very Well Located
ReviewText: We were close to everything we wanted to see and close to the subway to make visiting other sites convenient.

Title: Tired hotel, in need of renovation
ReviewText: The hotel is outdated and in need of a full renovation.  The rooms are ok but the entire feel is outdated, especially in the bathrooms.  The "gym" can't fit more than 2 people at a time and there is no working air conditioning.  I'd strongly encourage finding another hotel, which is a shame because the location is terrific.

Title: A pleasant  family holiday
ReviewText: Extraordinary good balance price/quality. Located in the heart of the city, easy access to Metro station and other facilities

Title: Super recommended at the heart of Manhattan
ReviewText: This hotel has probably one of the best locations in the city. It’s small, quite intimate and well equipped. The mezzanine coffee bar is a must. Rooms are large in size and beds are super comfortable. The service is simply amazing, super helpful at any time. We really enjoyed our long stay there, super recommended.

Title: Extremely well located and comfortable hotel
ReviewText: Exceeded my expectations, very clean and friendly hotel, located in the heart of manhattan

Title: Hotel located very conveniently, with very good service
ReviewText: Agile check-in. friendly staff. The courtesy coffee and hot water that is located on the mezzanine are very convenient. Comfortable room, with few rooms per floor, which means there is not so much noise on the floors and you can rest satisfactorily

Title: Urlaub
ReviewText:
Gute Lage
Freundlich
Gratis Kaffe rund um die Uhr

Title: Clean and convenient
ReviewText: This hotel is everything you need for a weekend trip to NYC. Close to Times Square and Broadway, Grand Central, shopping, food and drink. Staff is friendly and rooms are just right.

Title: très bon séjour
ReviewText: gentilesse  du personnel, manque une personne parlant le français à la réception

Title: excellent for families
ReviewText: in the heart of Manhattan , at walking distance of many touristic places

Title: Absolutely satisfied
ReviewText: Very good location in midtown Manhattan. Clean hotel with helpful staff.

Title: Great Location!
ReviewText: This hotel has a very good location! It is nice and clean. It offers a good service and in cost is adequate.

Title: Excellent girls weekend
ReviewText: Parking is everything.  I couldn’t have enjoyed my weekend without it.  Hotel is clean, safe, quiet, and free coffee, tea  and water on mezzanine. Great location near NYPL and Bryant Park. Helpful conceirge.

Title: Bon rapport qualité/prix
ReviewText: Personnel cpurtois, hotel très bien situé.

Title: Awesome Stay!
ReviewText: We will be returning to the Courtyard Marriott. Great location, great personnel, great accommodations. Would highly recommend to our family & friends!

Title: Ignorant/racist valet Frank
ReviewText: Let’s just say that due to the poor customer service we’ve received from Frank we will not be returning. We chose this hotel for the location. When I walked up to check in with valet, Frank simply asked Kim or Lee. It was unbelievable to me how rude and ignorant he was. I am not sure if he is a racist or not that is why I’d credited it to ignorance. I did remind him he actually missed one because it’s Nguyen. When I called him out on it, he simply moved on as if that whole incident didn’t just happened. The gentleman at check in was less than friendly. Might be due to the fact that I called out his ignorant coworker? Not sure either way. He could have done better. Positive note would be that our departure valet experience was wonderful due to Pedro being very professional and friendly. Simply put, I will not return to this location.

Title: A basic hotel at a New York price
ReviewText: Set your expectations low and you won’t be disappointed.  Friendly service helps make up for small, noisy rooms and dated decor. But expect to pay for the privilege of sleeping in midtown Manhatten.

Title: Great location
ReviewText: The hotel is in a perfect location, less than a minute from 5th avenue and a few minutes walk from Grand Central Station. Bryant Park nearby is great for eateries and hanging out. Time Square also nearby as are local bus and subway options for exploring manhattan and beyond. Staff friendly and attentive with a well stocked coffee machine and filtered water available 24/7

Title: Great location in Midtown
ReviewText: Great location with excellent staff. Near Times Square, Broadway, Empire State Building, Grand Central station. Walk or take convenient subway to rest of the city.  Only demerit is single coffee machine onsite but multitude of options nearby.

Title: Recommended
ReviewText: Good staffs, neat and comfy room and the food is owesome. Recommended if you are in NYC.

Title: Our go to Courtyard Marriott
ReviewText:
I have stayed at this location for many years.  It is conveniently location near Grand Central Station which we use to visit the city.  Bryant Park is steps away and this location allows us to have easy access to many of the places we visit during our stay.  
The hotel staff is always friendly and we are always able to check in a little earlier instead of waiting around for a room.  The rooms are clean and comfortable.  I will
Continue my to stay at this location.

Title: Do not stay here!
ReviewText:
One of the worst hotel stays we have had in our lives. 
From the wrong number of towels, to the unhealthy cleaning of the rooms, the only saving factor of tis hotel is the location. However, the Cons definitely outweigh the only Pro. 
Housekeeping too busy to get us towels or clean the room. The front desk lady and het uptight patronising rude tone. I could go on. 
Nope, not recommending this hotel to ANYONE.

Title: New York Staycation
ReviewText: Good spot to lay your head close to the action of the city.

Title: NY visit
ReviewText: Good location, comfortable stay.

Title: Really needs some attention and remodel
ReviewText: The hotel was a bit dingy and lacked basic amenities. Not what I expect from a Marriott.

Title: Excellent choice of hotel
ReviewText: This hotel is very central to everything you will need to see and do in New York. Very friendly staff, clean rooms and cleaning service is excellent, comfortable beds and good sized rooms.

Title: Perfect!
ReviewText: It was a incredible vacation in Courtyard.

Title: Excelente ubicación, malo el personal de front desk
ReviewText: Un 7/10, el hotel muy cómodo, en general el personal muy amable EXCEPTO EL RECEPCIONISTA DEL FRONT DESK QUE REALIZÓ MI CHECK-IN que fue grosero y solo se preocupó por cobrar sin explicar nada.

Title: 宿泊費
ReviewText:
やはりNew Yorkですね。
宿泊費が高かったです。

Title: Stay elsewhere
ReviewText: Hotel is in a great location.  However, that is all that is good about the hotel.  Lobby is small and outdated.  Rooms are tiny, and the bathrooms need a remodel.  Book elsewhere.

Title: Family Vacation
ReviewText: Good location, poor amenities, lobby with music very loud in the evenings. Staff need to be more friendly.

Title: Recent Stay
ReviewText:
Horrible coffee. No creamer to offset the taste of the terrible coffee. 
No gluten-free snack options (not everyone wants an apple)

Title: Nice hotel
ReviewText: The hotel was perfect for our needs. Great location, plenty of room and a very attentive and helpful staff. Would definitely stay there again.

Title: Avoid this hotel if you value sleep
ReviewText: This hotel’s soundproofing of windows is so bad, that the noisy sounds of trucks, police, etc from 5th Ave are very loud, making for sleepless nights. In addition, they don’t even have anywhere to get water, let alone snacks. I would avoid it at all costs.

Title: Thank you Marriott on 5th!
ReviewText: Staff was excellent. Would be nice to figure out a way to serve food and wine (not sure logistical how to do that - maybe team up with local restaurants and enable them to deliver at a discount?). The coffee situation isn’t great - tons of people always waiting for the one machine. You should consider getting a few more. But the staff are excellent - helpful and friendly and extremely efficient for check in and check out. I also had an issue in my room and at 9 pm the maintenance person fixed it within about 5 minutes of my calling downstairs which I was super impressed with. The location is also really great.

Title: La ubicación es perfecta
ReviewText: Algunas personas de la recepción brindan un servicio pesimo

Title: Great convenient location
ReviewText: Great location within walking distance to Times Square and numerous eating establishments.  Very near Bryant Park and the Library.  If you are looking for an affordable, secure, convenient location, then this is it. Also, extraordinary friendly and knowledgeable staff.

Title: Parfait !
ReviewText:
Situation géographique parfaite pour sillonner dans Manhattan. Quartier animé mais chambre silencieuse et confortable.
Amabilité du personnel et tarifs très raisonnables pour un un très bon comfort de la chambre.

Title: Pretty Good.
ReviewText: The hotel did have everything but microwaves and refrigerators. You do have to go out to buy your own food so there is also that. No breakfast included BUT very friendly staff, close distance to tourist places, and clean beds and good service. Do recommend!

Title: Great stay
ReviewText: We had a great stay. The staff was great and really understanding with us and the kids. It is close to everything which is a plus.

Title: Excellent stay in Manhattan
ReviewText: Had an excellent stay at the Courtyard by Marriott in Manhattan.  In town for a conference and the money I saved staying here paid for the conference.  Loved the coffee machine and complimentary snacks.  Room was large and nice!

Title: Meh
ReviewText: Mediocre hotel in a great location. Horrible mattresses, out dated decor and no front lounge area.

Title: Stay here!
ReviewText: Great location. Safe. Friendly staff! Clean!!

Title: Great location and great service!
ReviewText:
This hotel is very conveniently located just off of 5th Avenue at 40th Street. Easy walk to Times Square attractions and Broadway Theater district. Plenty of restaurant and cafe choices nearby.

The only negative for me was that there wasn't a lobby area where we could sit as a group in between our scheduled activities.

Title: Wonderful Stay
ReviewText: Had an amazing stay, Rooms were amazing and beds were very comfortable.

Title: Do NOT Park Here
ReviewText: The front of the car was damaged severely by the staff. They took our cash tip and did not tell us about it as we got in the car. We only noticed after we stopped for gas. Nobody will take responsibility. The cost and time to fix the car and replace the license plate far outweigh any stay at this hotel.

Title: Super Duper!
ReviewText: Our weekend quick trip was all the more pleasant by staying here. Our room was ready before it should have been.  The check-in process was seamless and the staff was very helpful and kind. Our room was plenty spacious enough and everything was very clean. We enjoyed the complimentary all-day coffee as we breezed in out during the day while walking the city.

Title: Not happy.
ReviewText: Big problem with street noise and not able to switch rooms.

Title: Outstanding Hotel
ReviewText:
Very Professional Staff
Valet Parking convenient
Outstanding Service
Clean Hotel

Title: A gem in the heart of Midtown Manhattan
ReviewText: My family and I spent 2 nights here this past weekend. What a find in the heart of midtown Manhattan.  Right across the street from the New York Public Library on 5th Avenue, its easily walkable to several "must sees" including Grand Central, Times Square and Bryant Park. The hotel itself was immaculate. The rooms were a nice size and our beds were very comfy.  The bell staff (shout out to Willie and Pablo) made it very easy to move our suitcases from the car to the hotel and they were all professional and warmly welcoming.  Their friendliness was the perfect kick-off to our weekend.  I recommend the hotel highly.  It's got all the ingredients for comfortable stay in NYC.

Title: Great Staff
ReviewText: We came to the city on personal business and included some sightseeing for our granddaughter. The location was great, one block from Bryant Park .

Title: Perfect location and friendly staff!
ReviewText: I stayed her with a friend for 4 nights while visiting my daughter at school in NYC. I was within walking distance to her apartment so that was awesome. The staff was super friendly & helpful. I stayed on a higher floor so street noise was minimal. The room was very comfortable and had everything I needed. I would stay here again.

Title: Worth the stay
ReviewText: We had a perfect stay. The staff was the star of perfection despite any amenities or services Marriot does not offer here.

Title: Really nice place to stay
ReviewText: I really enjoyed and appreciated my stay in this hotel the staff is very nice (I think in particular to Nancy R who was lovely with me) and there are good vibes in this place. I will definitely come back in this place if I come back to NYC. Have a great day.

Title: Clean, safe area, easy access to subway and Bryant Park
ReviewText: Love the area, lots of bakeries, shopping, park, and easy to get to subway.

Title: Location location
ReviewText: Great location, great staff! Near NYC public library, Bryant park, 5th Ave, walking distance to Times Square and Broadway. We walked everywhere, even at night. Small hotel, very clean. Great value! Lots of restaurants nearby. Staff was very helpful. We found the place where we will stay from now on!

Title: Room was to cold and uncomfortable
ReviewText: Nice location, but the room was to cold and the bed way to stiff

Title: Nice property
ReviewText: This property was very well-maintained and extremely convenient.  The service was excellent and the beds were comfortable.  But there is no common area to sit and relax.  There's a fancy and well-stocked coffee machine but nowhere to sit and enjoy it.  The rooms have one desk chair.  The "business" area is out in the open and if someone is participating in a video call you have no choice but to listen to them talking loudly into the computer.  It's a few feet from the coffee machine so while you're waiting in line it's just unpleasant.  Despite this shortcoming, if I wanted to be in this part of NYC, I would stay here again.  All of the staff were courteous and professional.

Title: Our NYC stay
ReviewText: The location of the hotel was perfect for all of our needs as we walked to the majority of locations during our stay. The exterior of the hotel was scaffold as they appear to be renovating.  The interior was okay. They provided a coffee,tea etc machine on the 2nd level.  They have one computer in the corner of their "Business Center" also on the Mezzanine.  Their Fitness Center was very sparse.  The staff was friendly enough.  We felt comfortable and safe there. One issue with maid service on the last day but other than that we had a comfortable stay.

Title: Uncomfortable Bed
ReviewText: The only complaint I have is the bed, but that is the single most important part of a hotel experience. It wasn't just a firm mattress, it felt like plywood. I was in town for business and the location was great for midtown meetings.

Title: Fine stay, horrible fitness centre
ReviewText: Location of hotel is good. The sleeping room is fine, not great (bed was very hard...not firm). The fitness centre is the size of a walk-in closet, not ventilated, and therefore unusable if more than 2 people are already there (and you don't mind breathing someone else's air).

Title: Nice place to stay
ReviewText: We enjoyed our overnight stay at this very convenient location. Sophie, the front desk person was lovely along with the valet person. Room was a decent size for NYC. Comfortable beds. Very clean room. As in most city hotels, you hear street noise all night as well the hallway noise. Heating fan very loud.

Title: Good location for a NYC visit
