Lovely hotel in a great location to Southbank and Trafalgar Square all the tourist attractions like London Eye, London Dungeons, Sealife and Shrekworld, aswell as some other restaurants like Strada and Wagamama further along the river path which we discovered after eating onsite!
Staff were very polite and friendly and the room we had was very nice river view double double decent size, with a lovely modern bathroom (no bath) the only negative was the food we had in Gilrays steakhouse for lunch was ok but not worth the money, we also ordered room service and food came from here again and was pricey and ok at best, the breakfast however served in the morning was very good we had Eggs Benedict and a full English.

Title: Good hotel disappointing restaurant.
ReviewText:
The hotel was great. 
We arrived early and asked for early check inn it was not possible but the room became available early. 
The entrance could do with a doorman with more of a presence he was great with bags but was not the best welcome. 
Food was disappointing at Gilroys.
The room and service was fantastic.

Title: Peace final few days before returning home...
ReviewText:
This was a family from Singapore 'escape' after 20 months of covid chaos. We travelled for almost a month in the UK, and the last 3 days again in MCH, with an upgraded suite. It has been an energetic and tiring trip, but the hotel was a relaxed and calm place to be, and a perfect location in London, with Parliament and London Eye in the neighborhood. This being my 2nd stay since 5 years ago...

The staff support was amazing, and in particular, I want to mention Martina at the M Lounge, who clearly put everything into her role and getting all into a joyful mood.. Thank you again Martina...

Title: Excellent choice for the best view and superb service
ReviewText: We have stayed at this hotel three different times and always find it to be first class in every way.  We asked for the same room we had when we were three three years ago and we so appreciated that you were able to do that for us.  The doormen and concierge are especially accommodating.  Thank you for another lovely experience there.

Title: Review of our 5 night stay
ReviewText: Reviewing our stay at the Marriott County Hall London

Title: Great family stay on London's south Bank
ReviewText: Great location, friendly staff lovely hotel only minor issue room bit hot at night

Title: Exceptional
ReviewText:
Everyone at County Hall went out of their way to make our stay exceptional.
The location is ideal for sightseeing and views of the Thames and London skyline beautiful. The Library afternoon tea was scrumptious and not to miss.

Title: Disappointed
ReviewText:
We was rather disappointed by the hotel it wasn’t worth the money we spent the food was burnt and overpriced 
The menu was very limited 
Breakfast wasn’t very nice at all 
The photos done the hotel more Justice than it looks in person 
We also had to pay £55 parking fee which wasn’t mentioned upon booking 
I wouldn’t say the hotel is 5* 
You cannot order room service past 11pm 
Not enough milk for tea / coffee in the room

Title: Recently stayed
ReviewText: The room we had was a family room, it was very spacious, beds felt like we where sleeping on clouds and the bathroom was excellent. The staff were very lovely and the hotel was perfectly clean. I would not however have breakfast or dinner there again. The evening meal was “pub grub” not what I was expecting, especially as it had came recommended by my own brother who often dines there, there was also a mouse running around, we where not so bothered about that but the couple next to us where horrified. The breakfast, I had read another review where the person had mentioned the breakfast as nothing special, so my expectations where already low, but I was not prepared for that level of low, the bacon was, well brittle and the sausage was fine after you cut the thick skin off.

Title: Por fin una solución como corresponde Gracias
ReviewText: Buena atención, dispuestos a solucionar los problemas correctamente ha sido una de mis mejores experiencias

Title: Xmas weekend stay
ReviewText:
Lovely location and hotel 
A little tired, bathroom fittings needed some repairs and maintenance 
Booked a river view which was fabulous but suffered with the noise from the air conditioning vents below which sounded like Heathrow airport. We were on the second floor so advise to ask for the higher floors 
Would stay again

Title: Catalogue of serious errors
ReviewText:
A lovely property that used to have very good service, but after my recent stay, I will not be returning.

Errors included:
1.  Instead of putting a hold on my card, the front desk person charged  for my entire stay + daily incidentals on check in.  The card I handed over to be used for my stay was not

Title: London weekend break
ReviewText: Overall we had an amazing time!

Title: A wonderful stay - a shame that housekeeping still falls short sometimes
ReviewText: The only irritation on this visit was the housekeeping, which fell short on a number of occasions.  Repeated requests for the same thing, a three hour wait for Pepsi, a failure to make up the bedding correctly and other housekeeping related mistakes spoiled this stay a little. Two senior managers were on leave at the same time, and there seemed to be a lack of direction.  Some housekeeping staff spoke little or no English.

Title: Wonderful hotel
ReviewText: We absolutely loved our stay at County hall. The room was modern, clean and beautiful. The staff were welcoming and really helpful.

Title: Beautiful hotel
ReviewText: Beautiful hotel with fantastic hospitality as you would expect from Marriott

Title: housekeeping
ReviewText: housekeeping not consistent. Missing coins...

Title: Excellent Service with a Great View!
ReviewText: First time in London. Hotel was very clean with great staff!!!

Title: Winter vacation
ReviewText: Staff were friendly. Dining options were limited.

Title: Great place to stay.  Fun!
ReviewText: We enjoyed ourselves.  With COVID, there were restrictions but all in all great.  Our Porter Maiko was so great to us that it made our trip even more enjoyable.

Title: 5 star hotel, 2 star food.
ReviewText:
I don’t mind spending money. Food prices are extortionate and one star Michelin prices. I’ve genuinely eaten in far better pubs at half the cost. 

A real shame as otherwise a lovely experience and location.

Title: An excellent stay in London
ReviewText:
Our stay was great! We arrived early but were allowed to check in to a double family room with a view of Parliament and Big Ben. Part way through our stay we asked for an upgrade and were moved into an Executive room with a small sitting area and nicer bathroom. 

Location is terrific. Right across the bridge from Westminster, Parliament, and Big Ben. A nice river walk to Tower of London, and Borough Market. 
 
Overall we availed ourselves of all the dining options other than room service. The breakfast at the library was good but they could use to offer one different option every morning. The menu got old after 4 mornings of dining. The food at Gilroy's was also good but pricey. I've never seen a 3.50 charge for mixing a martini. We really loved the M club but they as well could use a little more variety. 

A big thank you to the staff at this hotel. Hands down they were all terrific. They are your greatest asset. The front desk who helped with all our little requests made using At Your Service and the nice upgrade. The M Club staff were truly terrific. They made us feel welcome every night and were friendly and attentive to everyone. The Bellman with a warm welcome back every time we returned. 

Really a great stay. A big thank you from the both of us.

Title: Tv not working
ReviewText: Tv not working and took too long (I had to call twice) for maintenance to come fix it

Title: Lovely hotel
ReviewText: We stayed here for 3 nights, love the hotel, staffs were very helpful and friendly, definitely stay here again, highly recommended.

Title: Excellent Hotel But !!!
ReviewText:
This Hotel is in a fantastic location, the rooms, staff and food were all excellent but at breakfast 2 days running we ordered extra bits and they didn't come and when they went to check they still didn't come, this may sound trivial to some but this is a 5 star Hotel !!!!!! 
And detail matters.

Title: NOT  RECOMMENDED AT ALL.
ReviewText: The worse hotel and staff I’v ever encountered. Service from beginning to end was an absolute let down, my family and I were greeted with snobbery beyond belief, looked down on, they even tried to charge full price for parking even though I’m a blue badge holder, lied to by staff and given the view of a derelict court yard, and an old car banger. They took money from my account without my permission and even ignored my request for a birthday experience in the restaurant, the worse service and hotel we have ever stayed in. NOT RECOMMENDED AT ALL

Title: 2 night stay
ReviewText:
Warm welcome at check in
Shown to dinner as soon as we arrived 
Great service at dinner 
Room excellent 
Breakfast and service on the first morning was great
Breakfast service on last morning was terrible. We were waiting awhile for service, breakfast arrived cold. We left without breakfast as the service was that bad. 
Shame that the service was excellent up to the last day when it failed and nobody seemed to care!!

Title: County Hall
ReviewText:
The room was very cold air-conditioning did not work correctly also the sink in the bathroom was blocked television keep switching its self off sometimes after 30 minutes sometimes after an hour my breakfast was burnt one morning . my sons room was cold as well and air-conditioning made  a terrible noise after a few minutes and then went away and then it would come back again . I have stayed in this hotel before and it was great a few years ago before Covid but  this time very disappointed not sure if I would stay here again.
Some people complain about hotels and about food all the time I am not one of these people in fact this is the first time I have ever written a review.

Title: Clean room but no extras and terrible breakfast
ReviewText: The room was fine but no extras eg hot chocolate, minibar or biscuits. Rubber omelette for breakfast and no toast .

Title: Perfect London Experience
ReviewText: Located at the heart of London you get great service and experience

Title: James Ayres
ReviewText: We choose to stay there with our family members for a family wedding the hotel for hygiene and staff at the door I could not throw a stone as they was absolutely great however the heating did not work in our room 235 and also our telly did not work in 230 when we come to check out we spent over an hour going through the bill lady used her telephone as a calculator which I thought was a lot unprofessional And we had missed two of our taxes that the concierge service have a cross because of this with a massive inconvenience as was running late for a dinner appointment

Title: ICONIC...
ReviewText: This is a wow place to stay in London. The location is superb and the views are not had by anyone else. Very warm welcome by all staff and the rooms are spacious and comfortable.  The leisure club is one of the best in London and the pool is a proper swimming venue. Worth a stay at any time of year.

Title: Beautiful hotel
ReviewText: This was the first time to try this hotel, we really loved it. The main thing was that their staff were amazing, always smiling and made us feel welcome. The location of the hotel is also excellent near London Eye, and everything is close by using the underground. The only downside I would say is that it's a bit expensive..

Title: Extremely long walk to lifts
ReviewText:
There were 48 rooms (all on the same side of the hall) between mine and the lifts.  That is way too far to walk multiple times per day. 

Reception have poor directions to the tube.

Title: great location, and service
ReviewText: the only negative was my dinner, and kitchen reaction on the Tuesday night I arrived....

Title: Our new favorite hotel in London
ReviewText: Loved the huge room. Felt very welcomed and everyone was kind, considerate, helpful and thoughtful. We felt like this was our home with a beautiful room.  The bar area is wonderful for children even and the dining room has an amazing view of The Thames.  I felt like the view helped us enjoy every minute even more. Coffee in the room is nice. Bathroom is spacious. We loved every part of this stay. We have been to London many times and this is our new favorite hotel. The Christmas decor added to the delightful stay. Thank you.

Title: Disappointing food experiences
ReviewText: The room was lovely but we had very poor experiences with the food. Had to call 3 times to get the right order for room service for dinner and it wasn’t 5 star room service. The menu and coming in a takeaway bag felt quite cheap. Then at breakfast we were given cold food, told a staff member and they did nothing so we ate what we could but had to leave most of it. Was very disappointing considering it’s supposed to be a top hotel

Title: Weekend treat for the family
ReviewText:
We stayed a night at the London Marriott County Hall  as part of a weekend treat for our family of three up to London. The hotel is perfectly situated  just over Westminster bridge from the Houses of Parliament (and Westminster tube station where we walked from), overlooking the Thames. It is not far to walk to many of the major London attractions and is in itself situated above  the London Aquarium and  the London Dungeon and next to the London Eye.

Staff in reception were very welcoming and we we offered a complimentary  glass of prosecco. They were accommodating of our early arrival, taking care of our luggage whilst we walked to Covent Garden. All the hotels facilities were explained to us and reception pointed out where our breakfast would be served. 

The family room was on the second floor with picture postcard views of The Thames, Big Ben and the Houses of Parliament as well as dramatic view of the London Eye through two enormous windows. The room was enormous with acres of space around the main double bed and pull out sofa bed for our daughter. The main bed was very comfortable and we all slept well. There was a huge TV which could be swivelled around to face either bed or sofa and lots of wardrobe space for the longer stay visitor.  The bathroom was very luxurious and tastefully decorated - large and clean , tiled in white with a very deep bath  and shower.  There was also actop of the range coffee maker and  luxury teas to be had. 

Breakfast was served in rooms overlooking the Thames. You couldn't have asked for a more perfect setting to eat your breakfast for a London visit. We kept it simple with granola and pancakes for our daughter but there were a variety of freshly cooked breakfasts you could chose from, which looked good.

After a short time digesting our breakfast and watching London go by we spent out last hour at the hotel, swimming in the hotel's beautiful and surprisingly big pool up on the fifth floor.  You can do proper 25m  'lengths' here in a stunning surrounding and its standing depth throughout.  Our daughter loved it! 

I would thoroughly recommend a stay at the County Hall. Perfectly situated for the  first time London visitor and luxurious for people who don't feel the  need to leave the hotel.

Title: Bad breakfast and poor service
ReviewText: Not a 5star at all ! No room service, no water at the fitness, old spa, no taxi service, long waiting time for bar service and the worst breakfast I ever had at a Marriott.

Title: London Marriott County Hall
ReviewText: What a fabulous property. Great location, great staff, great restaurants. We were there both for pleasure and business and the business aspect of it was handled superbly with them providing us a meeting room and refreshments. Could not of asked for a better experience.

Title: 10th wedding anniversary
ReviewText:
Pretty Hotel,
A little underwhelmed with a few things considering how much we paid,
Great location,
Probably wouldn't visit again.

Title: Nice location, felt a little under staffed
ReviewText:
As per title great location, good amenities but felt a little under staffed. 

The bar area needed hoovering and service was pretty slow.

Title: Family break
ReviewText: This is a lovely hotel with attentive staff. The location is great.

Title: Great location and room size
ReviewText: Loved the location, close to waterloo station so helpful for travel around london and surroundings.   Room was also extra large so good if you are traveling with 3 people sharing a room.

Title: 85thousand points for this soulless visit
ReviewText:
Clearly this hotel is not ready to be open post COVID unlike the numerous Marriott hotels I have stayed at worldwide in recent weeks.
Breakfast was served in the room cold, tasteless handed over in a brown paper bag with wooden disposable cutlery!!! No vodka in the club lounge, barely any edible snacks in the club lounge, room cold, windy and very very noisy due to faulty window which would not close, never mind lock. Room door lock faulty, room door closer faulty, my god this was an absolute shambles of a stay.I felt sorry for the staff who were clearly embarrassed by the state of things, but why would Marriott care when they have a captive audience in probably the best hotel location in all of London. I never complain and check my previous  countless reviews which all give fantastic reports and I am a very positive person but honestly I am struggling to say anything nice about this hotel, EXCEPT in the downstairs bar the staff are fantastic and really do serve great cocktails.

Title: Fabulous stay - great location and service
ReviewText: From the moment we arrived we were made to feel welcome.  Made use of the 25 meter pool which was amazing

Title: Amazing unique hotel
ReviewText:
Brilliant position alongside the Thames and amazing view. Building has an amazing history. Facilities are first class and staff faultless.
 Had a small issue with the air on not turning off and waking up freezing cold.

Title: London County Hall - practically perfect in everyway!
ReviewText: We stayed for 8 nights in an executive suite at the London County Hall. Our views were spectacular, the rooms were spacious and allowed our son to spend a couple nights with us. The hotel has been lovingly restored and the appointments are perfect. It was very clean and the staff was responsive if we needed anything. We appreciated the great team in the M lounge for cocktails and light bites in the evening, when we had walked too far to go out. The breakfast in the library each morning was lovely and there were always interesting people to meet. We would definitely stay there again. The location cannot be beat!

Title: Wonderful Stay
ReviewText: Excellent Location, Rooms, Staff.

Title: Great location but not valued as guest
ReviewText:
Riverside room was only partial mini side view. No efforts made for my Mum's Birthday week-end.
Breakfast not buffet. Cold option and hotel option. Excuse is pandemic yet restrictions lifted UK 19 July and staff do not wear masks. Staff not friendly. Good location but Disappointing stay

Title: Fantastic location & hotel poor staff
ReviewText:
What a location in the hub of the city, short walks to transport and to the prime tourist spots
But the staff need to appreciate the cost of a stay at the hotel, we were forced out of the hotel bar when we didn’t want to go paying £30 for two drinks. Found it to be very rude and not customer orientated: I could have spent another couple of hundred pounds in the bar but they wanted to go to bed! 
Was a real shame as we loved the location

Title: My review
ReviewText: great location - disappointing level of service and breakfast leaves a lot to be desired

Title: A great base for London exploring
ReviewText: We stayed one night to do a bit of London sightseeing. The hotel was perfectly placed for the London Eye, with a spectacular view out onto the river from the restaurant. The pool was a hidden gem - definitely worth a look.

Title: Happy
ReviewText: Great hotel and location. Only observation, the restaurant looked a bit tired (needs an update) food was perfect though.

Title: Almost there for a platinum member
ReviewText: This hotel is in the prime location but h the service is very inconsistent. I am rating this as a platinum member so the expectation is for it to be consistent wherever I am around the world.  First things first to be confronted about something which is suppose to be included and asked to pay. Awkward

Title: Great hotel and staff
ReviewText:
Excellent hotel and staff, but could do with some improvements to make it perfect. Breakfast in the library was a wonderful way to start the day. And so was starting the evening in the M Club lounge where Florentina, Sandra, and Ibron really looked after you. Service at breakfast and the M Club were excellent and so were the Bell staff and the staff at reception. 
On the negative side, the gin bar had a lot of potential but lacked ambiance. Could have done with some music. The cocktails and service missed the mark. The sofas had straight backs which were not comfortable. 
The quality of the food at Gillray's was good but due to the layout of the restaurant and the noise, it felt more like dining in a railway station. Staff there preparing for breakfast made a lot of noise so it was not a fine dining experience. We gave them the benefit of the doubt that we were still in COVID and things were not yet quite back to normal.
Would we stay here again - of course. But we would expect things to have improved the next time.

Title: Central location.
ReviewText: Stayed two nights for business. Chose this hotel because of central location and onsite parking. Booked a parliament facing room but was more river Thames / London eye with parliament barely visible. Bed sheets had marks on them which gave doubt about cleanliness but the rest of room was well presented. Room service was served in boxes because of Covid but was supposed to be ordered via app but it didn’t work. Breakfast which was included was good.

Title: Excellent location
ReviewText: Loved this hotel. Room service was extortionate as was juice at breakfast aside from that everything else was top class. Spacious, comfortable rooms. Friendly staff. Lovely pool and facilities

Title: Work
ReviewText: Perfect we all loved the time we spent can’t wait to do it again

Title: Great stylish hotel, expensive breakfast.
ReviewText: Loved the hotel, the artwork, the generous sized rooms, the cleanliness. The front desk staff were really friendly and welcoming. The restaurant breakfast is too expensive.

Title: Overall a pleasant stay.
ReviewText: Rooms at the rear of hotel noisy with tannoy announcements throughout the night. Bar area disappointing. Breakfast service lacking but very friendly staff. Overall a pleasant stay. Good location.

Title: Excellent hotel that needs to work out a few details
ReviewText: Except for the lack of step-free access and the gym needing urgent maintenance, this hotel has the best location and provides the best value for money in central London.

Title: Great location and friendly staff.
ReviewText: Make sure to take rooms with view of the Thames River. Perfect for a family trip - nice large rooms.

Title: Weekend occasion
ReviewText: We experienced a great welcome by all the team. Our room was amazing, very modern and clean. We had booked a gin afternoon experience in the bar which was amazing. We also managed to take a swim in the morning, extremely impressive gym facilities. A lovely weekend treat

Title: Lovely family break
ReviewText: We booked the kids staycation offer. The kids were delighted with their slippers and cuddly toys. The room was very spacious. We had access to the lounge-the drinks were nice however there did need to be more snack items especially during the day

Title: London visit good central location
ReviewText: Nice room for a couple however only have 1 easy chair unlike the photo online

Title: Over all it was very good.
ReviewText: It's a nice hotel in a great spot. The staff are very friendly and helpful.

Title: Could be better.
ReviewText: Excellent as 4 star hotel, however it is 5 star hotel. Maybe not enough for 5 stars .

Title: Another stellar experience!
ReviewText: The outstanding staff at this iconic property have made all of our stays absolutely fantastic. Every guest is treated as the most special one, and we have noticed that guests at this stunning hotel treat each other with respect and kindness, which we truly credit to the atmosphere created by the staff, The rooms, restaurants, and common areas are beautifully decorated and The Library is a singularly magical experience. The location is perfect for those who wish to walk about London and also for those wishing to use public transport. As for the views, go see for yourself and you won't ever want to leave.

Title: 20th wedding anniversary
ReviewText: We stayed at the Marriott for our wedding anniversary, right from booking the staff were on hand and made our stay a memorable one, the hotel has fantastic facilities and is spotlessly clean, we had a beautiful view of the Thames and Parliament from our room, the location is perfect with great access to the underground and lovely walks along the south bank. Will definitely stay here again.

Title: Classic and Elegant
ReviewText: We enjoyed our stay at this beautifully restored hotel.  It’s a gem and the location couldn’t be more perfect.  The staff was lovely.  So Friendly and genuine.  Always helpful.  Can’t wait to come back.

Title: Amazing Customer Service but…
ReviewText:
My stay was awesome! They accommodated my requests and the staff were so hospitable. 
The only thing is the room we stayed in had small issues: some light bulbs were not working, the tile grouts in the shower were yellow. 

However, we had a lovely stay there! 

P.S. be ready for some additional £££ as their room service food is pricey. But an alternative is Gillray’s Restaurant. And it is worth it!

Title: Not value for money
ReviewText: Not value for money. Was okay

Title: Excellent experience
ReviewText: Excellent experience with a suite overlooking the River

Title: Room Service?
ReviewText:
We arrived reasonably late in the evening and decided to order room service.
The order arrived some 30-40 minutes later served in a paper bag!
Its not what I'd call an acceptable room service presentation for what I'd deem a fairly up market London hotel.
I have stayed at other Marriott hotels during my travels and have yet to receive room service in a similar fashion to what we received during our stay at this facility.
The quality and presentation of the room service frankly detracted from the other positive aspects of this hotel and needs a lot of attention to bring it back into line with other hotels in the group!

Title: Review
ReviewText: Excellent. Only thing is could have had bag sent up quicker

Title: Zipped twin beds
ReviewText: Not a great night's sleep on a double bed that was actually be two twin beds zipped together. Room was also quite stuffy as the air con appeared not to be functional

Title: Unmet basics for a 5 star hotel
ReviewText: When I arrived, there was no garbage can in the bathroom. One day, the toiletries were not replaced after turnover. There was no plug in the bathroom so that I could use the hairdryer or curl my hair. Nothing was available in the minibar. The spa did not have masseuse available on the weekday. These are basics that should be available at any five-star hotel.

Title: Birthday weekend
ReviewText: Overall a pleasant stay, great location. Shame the bar closes at 11pm and there is still no room service after 10pm or any mini bar. Always prefer a buffet breakfast with lots of choice, but covid is now the normal excuse

Title: Amazing hotel !
ReviewText: This hotel goes above and beyond for you! Honestly nothing is too much for them ! If you’re contemplating going here, just do it ! We went to celebrate a 21st birthday and it was 100% worth the money. Great location and super easy to navigate.

Title: Amazing experience during my stay at the Hotel
ReviewText: From  start to finish the service provided was amazing.  The hotel is luxurious and will definitely be returning and recommending to my family, friends and business partners etc

Title: Lovely stay
ReviewText: Had a lovely time with you

Title: Outstanding location on the Thames
ReviewText: Beautiful room with excellent view of the Parliament, Big Ben and West minster bridge and Abbey

Title: Not 5 star quality
ReviewText: The pool area and gym were nice and were the reason we decided to stay at the Hotel. However I was not very impressed with the standard for the room, King size deluxe, and the standard of the decor, tv, sockets, broken lamp, bed not comfy and poor pillow quality. What really soured the experience was the room service. A service I look forward to the most whilst staying in a high standard of hotel. A £40 breakfast served in a paper bag, with wooden cutlery, which I didn’t even attempt to begin eating with and instead binned it. My wife’s toast was wrapped in a plastic bag and was soggy by the time I came to the door. Very unimpressed. Would not recommend to anyone and will certainly not be back.

Title: Exceptional customer service and a wonderful experience all round!
ReviewText:
From the moment we arrived we were greeted with a warm welcome by Barry and the check in staff where friendly and made it a pleasant experience.

During the few nights we stayed the food was great, bar service was fast and equally as welcoming and overall the customer service was exceptional.

The room itself was also very clean, modern and well thought out with extra little touches and good use of space. 

We'll certainly be back soon!

Title: Fabulous hotel!!!
ReviewText: Amazing building, great location and spectacular views.

Title: Breakfast was not as good as the rest of the service .hot food came out very quickly after the cereal . Quality and service was only average
ReviewText: Very good location we had an excellent room

Title: Excellent everything
ReviewText: Central location, beautiful place and simply excellent service and accommodations. As someone who usually stays in Four Seasons hotels and has experienced many JW Marriotts across the globe, this is by far the best Marriott experience I’ve ever had—and one of the best hotels I’ve stayed at in genera. Best bang for the buck in London, bar none.

Title: Bait and switch
ReviewText:
Paid for Thames view king room
Got a river walk view and two beds pushed together
Shocking

Title: Good location and Friendly Staff
ReviewText: The Hotel was clean and front of house staff were attentive.  I did not eat in the restaurant as the menu was limited and not that appealing for a Vegetarian but OK if you wanted a steak!. Also I thought the application of a service charge for each drink ordered at the bar a little excessive.  The pool was very good and well organised

Title: Weekend trip
ReviewText:
Absolutely fabulous hotel 
In a great location with extremely helpful and friendly staff.

Title: Parliament Big Ben/London Eye Suit
ReviewText: The hotel felt dated. The prices that are charged should be seen the the decor but is not. It needs an upgrade and a suit with no bath tub? What’s that all about? The grey walls I. The room felt dark and drab. The bed felt like there was sand on the sheets. I understand a tiled floor in the bathroom but why in the hallway? Walking from carpet in the room to a tiled floor IN the room just felt wrong. The room is to expensive for that level of quality.

Title: Almost perfect
ReviewText: The location is excellent. The inside makes you feel in history. The staff very smily and helpful. Room are good sized. The only major draw back was cleanliness and room service. It was really really bad. We had always to remind them to clean the room and the toilets and room were often not properly cleaned. Sometimes they only. Make up the bed and that's it. This is not the level of service expected from Mariott.

Title: Well situated but a little lacklustre
ReviewText: One of the managers at the restaurant was rude and not at all welcoming.   Despite the food being good, he ruined the beginning of our evening for us.

Title: Great Hotel and location
ReviewText: On this occasion we stayed for a birthday celebration. This hotel is in a fantastic location for all the big London attractions. The hotel is immaculate, the rooms are beautiful. The reception staff are so polite and very helpful. The guys on the door are friendly and we enjoyed a little chat with them each day. There is a lovely steakhouse within the hotel, the steaks were amazing !! We’ve stayed before and will definitely stay again

Title: Fantastic hotel and excellent attentive staff
ReviewText: Lovely hotel, great location, facilities are really good and staff are fantastic. Our room was lovely and housekeeping made a great job of keeping the room clean.

Title: Theatre Trip
ReviewText: Excellent hotel in a superb location.

Title: One of my favorite hotels!
ReviewText: The best staff! Big gym! Wonderful service!!! Beautiful rooms!

Title: Excellent stay
ReviewText: Excellent location nice rooms

Title: Wonderful family trip
ReviewText:
We stayed here early September for a family trip to London. The location was perfect, the room was spacious and had a wonderful view over the Thames and the London eye. The staff were friendly and made the children feel special which was a lovely touch and often overlooked.

We will certainly be returning.

Title: Family weekend
ReviewText: Fantastic location, excellent facilities and staff

Title: Go here as soon as you can!
ReviewText: Just perfect in every single way!

Title: Great location, great building but poor service
ReviewText:
Been past this Marriott a few times, and decided to stay with the family.  The hotel itself is in a brilliant position to get the most from London and it is very well looked after inside. However, the service really let the experience down. The room was not ready at check in time and my requests had been ignored (such as making up the sofa bed in the room) and then ignored when I asked again.

The M Lounge was nice and has a great outlook over parliament which was a nice touch. Due to COVID, they cannot serve breakfast there, so booked in the main restaurant. The hot breakfast was not great as it was not hot and the you are limited to 1 hot drink (no refill!).

Point to note: the bar closes at 11pm. I mean, my local country pub stays open later than that! So couldn’t stop by it on returning to the hotel in the evening to try the gins.

Title: Stay for my wife’s birthday
ReviewText: What can I say apart from magical, hotel room staff 100% The extras in my room including a lovely cake, Sandra at breakfast can’t wait to come back for my daughter’s birthday in November.

Title: London Marriott Hotel County Hall
ReviewText: We highly recommend this hotel for your London stay.  The hotel itself is stunning.  The staff are very attentive.  The food was delicious.  The room exceeded our expectations.  We will definitely stay here again.

Title: Fab location and good rooms
ReviewText: Perfect location for a London sightseeing. River view room was fab and spacious.

Title: Just gets better and better
ReviewText:
My fourth time at this property and I'll be back. As always everything is simply perfect.

Gillray's is a must do restaurant in my opinion, the chilli beef short ribs are sublime!!

Fortunate enough to receive a suite upgrade thanks to my status and it was not a disappointment, views of big ben and the houses of parliament from the balcony every morning with a cup of coffee, incredible.

A welcome glass of champagne wouldn't go amiss, snacks in the exec lounge, wider availability of alcoholic drinks in the lounge but other than that, very hard to recommend improvements.

Title: Excellent customer experience
ReviewText:
Recently visited London with the kids stayed at this hotel was a very lovely enjoyable stay everything was perfect . The staff is very friendly and helpful. 
.

Title: A perfect stay
ReviewText:
A couple of nights at Marriott county hall over the week end with family
We normally stay at Soho house but this time with the activities planned around the hotel, we decided to try Marriott. Well we were not disappointed! What a treat. The staff were amazing particularly Martina ( I hope I got the name right) at the M club. The kids loved it. She look after us like VIP and above. All the staff from front of house to house keeping were so accommodating and nothing was ever too much. 
We booked a few places to eat but decided to stay at the hotel as we were so comfortable. Food can be a touch better but it wasn’t horrible.  Just my taste. 
Overall, I will go back again and again. The team made a great stay !!! 
Thanks you

Title: Fab stay
ReviewText: Family visit, fab location, amazing staff, great concierge

Title: Stunning hotel
ReviewText: Stunning hotel in the perfect location

Title: Trip to London
ReviewText: Stayed here for a family trip to London. Great location and friendly staff. Room was excellent.

Title: Cleanliness has deteriorated
ReviewText: Cleanliness was of a poor standard

Title: beautiful setting. poor room service menu and annoying breakfast menu
ReviewText:
beautiful setting. Children had a difficult time with the food as very limited room service menu and item not available 
breakfast odd serving ONE croissant - yes you could only have one in a bowl
Not the feel of abundance you would want from a five star hotel

Title: Memorable Anniversary Stay
ReviewText: The hotel is lovely and in a perfect location. We booked a room with a view and that view was spectacular. The food was great, but it was the service that made it special, and everyone we dealt with were    lovely and helpful…..I’d like to give a special mention to the team in the Executive Lounge. Thank you everyone

Title: Family stay
ReviewText: We stayed as a family of 5, perfect location for everything

Title: Overall stay
ReviewText: Food quality and taste is below average for breakfast and lunch. The wait is very long especially lunch although we were dining at 2pm.

Title: Lovely Staycation
ReviewText: We stayed for a long weekend recently and thoroughly enjoyed our time at the hotel.  The room was large compared to other London hotels and everywhere was very clean and covid safe.  Shout out to all the staff who were amazing.

Title: Hotel
ReviewText: Position for sightseeing excellent food over priced and not good standard .

Title: Over charged on everything
ReviewText:
Ordered room service and was given separate cardboard boxes small sized meaning you couldn’t t put the meal together  and wooden cutlery. The salad was missing.
We couldn’t eat steak as not able to cut.
We asked for cutlery and it took 35 mins to deliver which we then had cold food so just put outside. Coke and apple juice was ridiculous prices especially when you take out the ice it was not even 1/3 of a glass.

Title: Great hotel
ReviewText: Really recommend this for a London family stay

Title: Family vacation
ReviewText: Expected door to door concierge service. Otherwise exceptional amenities all round. Would highly recommend.

Title: Great location but a bit pricey
ReviewText: Great location but a bit pricey.  Drinks are very expensive. Didn’t eat in the hotel and had room only £21 each for breakfast is extortionate.

Title: Weekend stay
ReviewText: Returning to what has always been a special hotel, so sad it really didn’t live up to our expectations and some staff (bar and housekeeping) weee rude and surly and bar felt like it needed a very good clean

Title: Service could have been more believable
ReviewText: Our recent stay was an average one. My family and I were extremely looking forward to our stay but we were disappointed. My son had an accident in the pool and we were given one plaster after I asked the pool receptionist but no care or attention was given. The deep cuts to both his toes affected him his whole time away. We were asked to fill in a report but no one checked on him afterwards to see if we needed anything. The room service menu is very limited and when we asked for a slice of bread and butter to go with some chips for our daughter we were told no. I’m sure this would have been a 2 minute walk to take a slice from the breakfast area. On our last night the room hadn’t been cleaned or fresh towels hadn’t been provided until we were going out so we had to use damp towels from the previous evening plus the toiletries hadn’t been refreshed. All in all, the hotel has a fabulous view, the rooms are beautifully decorated but unfortunately the service from members of staff would make me think twice about returning.

Title: Fab Hotel and Staff
ReviewText:
This hotel is a must if you plan to stay in Central London. The building is fantastic and located right on the Thames with easy access to the tube stations & river boats which stop right outside. We experienced a warm welcome from the start, the executive double room we had gave us (family of four) plenty of room and was very clean and well appointed. Breakfast was great and the pool lovely. The M lounge was a nice bonus with complimentary snacks and drinks. Staff friendly and engaging through. A real pleasure to stay!! 

Well done all at the London Marriott County Hall, we will be back!

Title: Short family break - excellent
ReviewText: Every member of staff so polite and friendly.  Service first class in all areas.

Title: Mr
ReviewText: Fantastic hotel, fantastic location, only let down was not many alcoholic drinks available if you’re not a Lover of Gin, as for the steakhouse the meat was cooked to how I liked however, the grill didn’t seem to be cleaned that it was cooked on as there was a taste of burnt which took away the flavour of the meat.

Title: Perfect location and friendly staff
ReviewText:
Perfect location in central London , great views from the hotel , rooms were fabulous as were the facilities, staff were professional, polite and friendly .. nothing too much trouble .
Only downfall was the service in the hotel bar, it was understaffed and waiting times to be served drinks at times exceeded 30 mins 
But overall the hotel was fabulous and definitely stay again

Title: staff
ReviewText: lovely staff . great service . would return , loved the m lounge .

Title: Fabulous hotel
ReviewText: We had a marvellous stay at the Marriott, an excellent location for Southbank and all the attractions. Special mention for Pauline on reception who my 7 year old daughter adored and said was ‘so kind and helpful’ - as were all the staff. Thank you!

Title: A wonderful weekend!
ReviewText: We spent a weekend at the Marriott County Hall celebrating a 60th birthday. The hotel was in the perfect location, and had a London bus tour stop right outside which was extremely convenient for travelling around the city. We stayed in a balcony suite and the view over the river, parliament and the London eye was spectacular, there aren’t many places in London with such a view for the price!! I couldn’t fault the staff at all, they were all extremely friendly and attentive.

Title: London trip
ReviewText: Comfy hotel with lovely staff and grand food

Title: Great stay and service
ReviewText: The valet was very helpful in letting me plug in my EV car to an outside plug for charging. It would be good to see proper EV charging in future, but I appreciate the help to solve this now

Title: Magical!
ReviewText: An AMAZING 3 weeks!  Thank you very much!

Title: Simply the best
ReviewText: The location is unbeatable. The accommodations state-of-the-art. The service is spectacular. The food delicious. You simply cannot do better than this in London.

Title: Excellence at its finest
ReviewText: Throughly enjoyed our stay as a family at the Marriott. Staff was faultless and made our stay enjoyable from the start to the end.

Title: City Break with Family
ReviewText:
An excellent base from which to explore London as it is very conveniently located. The children loved the pool. It was very clean, warm and the gym area and showers were all in very good state. Machines and lockers were all working and the changing areas were cleaned regularly. 
The room itself was a good size, with two double beds. 
The biggest draw back was the food. The room service was quite frankly terrible. We ordered the vegan burgers and for £90 we were robbed! 
The bar was well stocked with gin but for the duration of our stay they could not offer any bar snacks as they were 'out' of olives and nuts. This seems nothing short of bizarre for a top hotel in the centre of London. 
The front of house team were friendly but unable to help me book the London Eye which again I found bizarre given their location directly under the Eye itself! Quite inexcusable. 
The breakfast is currently somewhat limited by covid, I assume, as you order off menu and choose one cold and one hot item each. It is disappointing the pastries don't change daily and we found that the 'one pan' meals are a little dry having sat under a heat lamp for too long. I suggest ordering an omelette which is made fresh to order. I also find it a little odd to only be allowed one filter coffee at breakfast and not to be offered a top-up. The service was fast though and the views over the river were fantastic. 
Lastly, the in-room TV entertainment is limited to terrestrial TV, everything else you have to stream through your phone which can be a bit patchy and was not easy when we wanted a movie night in the room as the movie stream kept glitching. I think they should offer a movie channel.
All things considered, I would not hesitate to book again but would not order the room service next time!

Title: First Class!
ReviewText:
We had an amazing stay at the County Hall Marriott. Everything was wonderful, the room, the view, the balcony, the service, cleanliness. We have certainly found our perfect hotel for future visits to London and will recommend to family and friends.
Special shout out to Andrea who w as so helpful and nothing was too much trouble!
The only negative I have (which didn't affect our stay at all) would be the limited room service evening menu, I feel there could have been more options available.

Title: Warn out and tired leisure
ReviewText: The hotel is tired. The staff appeared tied as well. The rooms do not stand up to the same picture advertised on there site. Very disappointing

Title: Great hotel, great location, poor restaurant
ReviewText: Great location and very well presented hotel, ave stayed at Marriott hotel many times and this is up there. Unfortunately the food beverage side let’s this place down, poor service, missed meals, sauces arriving after meal on evening. Breakfast initially cup of tea and soft drinks arrived then 50mins for food after being told computer error same as evening before excuse. Just wasn’t right people coming in after you order and being served and leaving before you. Several complaints were made over the 2 days from guests, knocking money off is the solution they offer. We would of preferred quality family time and dine together.

Title: Great stay, brill rooms and breakfast, amazing location, pool v basic
ReviewText: Loved our 3 night family stay. Fantastic room, comfy abs spacious two double bed family room for 4 with two little ones. Excellent check in process, welcoming staff. Balloons and fizz a nice touch. Breakfast was very good too. Pool is very basic but location makes up for that.

Title: A wonderful stay - shame about the bedding mistake
ReviewText:
I have explained in great detail that a serious error was made in relation to a specific request about bedding!  This was corrected before our second night, but it was something of a shame when everything else was faultless!  
I cannot speak highly enough of our suite (we could live in it!) or the staff who were so helpful - Laura, Anna, Stelian and George and Gareth in particular.  Gareth is an incredible young man and went way beyond the call of duty, even organising a special treat before we left. Laura had arranged a cake for my companion's birthday. A full sized chocolate cake with stawberries and blueberries! A fridge was in the suite (at my request) together with jugs of fresh milk, bottles of water and cans of Pepsi. Full marks to all those staff named in this review.

Title: Best location for family trip
ReviewText: County Hall is in the most amazing location on the door step of the London Eye and the Aquarium.  In the evenings there was a great choice of nearby restaurants on South Bank.  Staff were friendly and helpful.  Our room had two double beds so was well suited for a family of three. Clean and comfortable.

Title: Sub standard
ReviewText: As before, room service choice, quality and price, sub standard.

Title: Nice Stay
ReviewText: Nice family stay, personal opinion is the front entrance looks very tacky for a high star hotel with the Big Ben and guards.

Title: Luxurious and well- situated
ReviewText: Very luxurious stay from glass of bubbles on arrival to amazing views of Thames from rooms.  Very convenient for all activities.

Title: Nice hotel, great location
ReviewText: This is a nice hotel in a beautiful, historic building - in a great location in central London.  It is next to the London Eye, opposite Parliament and just a short walk from the West End.  We enjoyed our short stay here and would come again.

Title: Perfect location perfect service
ReviewText: We had a wonderful stay, the view was amazing directly looking out at the London eye. Service was excellent would definitely go back when next visiting London

Title: Great to be back with Marriott
ReviewText: Titanium Elite member, frequent traveler until Covid. Great to be enjoying the Marriott service again!!

Title: Location
ReviewText:
Stay was overall what you would expect from a decent hotel
But way overpriced

Title: Beautiful Hotel
ReviewText: Such a beautiful hotel with amazing views of the London eye! The Staff were extremely friendly and welcoming. It was my birthday and they sent balloons and Prosecco to our room which was such a lovely touch and made my day extra special! Our room was clean and the bed was very comfortable. Would highly recommend.

Title: Weekend away
ReviewText: This is one of best hotels I’ve stayed in, brilliant location, excellent food, lovely views, and great staff. Highly recommend

Title: Great Hotel
ReviewText:
We stayed in a 2 bedroom suite and the view was amazing, the room was newly renovated, clean and comfortable without any ambient noise from the busy esplanade below. 
Staff were super friendly and helpful. Amazing location for families, easy to get to all the top spots. Only disappointed was breakfast and the limited dining options. Would stay here again.

Title: Fabulous hotel
ReviewText:
What a beautiful hotel!
I stayed here with my partner for the weekend and we honestly couldn’t fault a thing.
The staff are so friendly and helpful, the location is absolutely perfect and the river view rooms are just beautiful.
We will definitely be returning, thank you for such a lovely stay.

Title: Fabulous Birthday Treat
ReviewText: The staff at County Hall were amazing from start to finish and really made my wife’s birthday treat so special. Many thanks to the front desk, concierge and especially the breakfast team .. super nice and can’t thank them enough

Title: County Hall
ReviewText: Nice hotel but stay away from Gillrays restaurant, staff are distracted forgetting orders and can’t make even pancakes from fresh

Title: Incredible stay with my daughter
ReviewText: Incredible experience! Thank you.

Title: Fantastic Location for London Break
ReviewText:
Just returned from a short break in London.  Hotel was fantastic - great location and very clean.

Staff were very friendly and helpful - going the extra mile to make our stay a very memorable one.

Have stayed in many city hotels but this is one of the best around!

Title: Excellent hotel-great location.
ReviewText: We recently stayed as a family at this hotel and had a great time. Walking outside was very convenient with views of the Thames. The staff were all lovely from beginning to end. The teenagers loved the pool and extensive gym.  Would definitely stay here again.

Title: Great visit
ReviewText:
Great week in London 
Especially recommend the balcony suite and the Executive club lounge

Title: Lovely
ReviewText: Lovely stay in Marriott county hall. We were there this time last year and wanted to come back.

Title: Enjoyable Experience
ReviewText: Congratulations to the Marriott Training Programme of its staff.   Without exception in all areas they confirmed that their mission was to ensure that the needs of guests were their first priority.   This coupled with extremely comfortable beds, the restaurant facilities and of course the hotels superb location contributed to a most enjoyable experience.

Title: Best place to stay in london
ReviewText: the location, staff and room were top class.

Title: Great location, hotel and Marriott benefits
ReviewText: Chose this hotel as a family celebration. All staff made you feel very welcome and good upgrade as Titanium member

Title: Classy, comfortable - a great five star hotel
ReviewText: The hotel was just what we were looking for - a building with history, style and luxury, really good location, delicious breakfast and every single member of staff made us feel so welcome (the glass of wine as we checked in was a nice touch too!). Thanks for a great stay, we’ll be back.

Title: Sensational stay
ReviewText: We had the best time ever at the London Marriott County Hall. By far this is the best Marriott Hotel we’ve stayed at - the staff were absolutely phenomenal!

Title: Honeymoon
ReviewText: Excellent service from the staff. Always helpful and on hand for any query’s. They set up our hotel room with a ‘just married’ sign, balloons and rose petals as it was our honeymoon. Cant recommend them enough
The hotel was so pleasant and very well situated. The staff were fantastic and not a single person was anything less that a fine example of hospitality in London

Title: Family Break
ReviewText: Excellent location and great facilities.

Title: Stayed for 5 nights
ReviewText:
My husband had to a undergo major surgery at St Thomas' Hospital. The hotel is directly opposite the hospital so it was in the perfect location for my needs.
As I was at the hotel for six days, I got to know a number of staff. They were, without exception, charming, helpful and genuinely concerned about my husband. They made a very stressful situation bearable. 
I extend my heartfelt thanks.

Title: A Wonderful Stay
ReviewText: We had a wonderful overnight stay at the Marriott County Hall. Check in was friendly, quick and easy. Our room was on the third floor. It is a large hotel so it was quite a walk to the room but well worth it for the view of the river and the London Eye. The room was spacious and equipped with everything we needed. We used the pool and sauna which was a nice relaxing bonus after a busy day taking in London. We had a meal from the set menu at Gillray's and watched the final. The staff were attentive with such a big event going on.  Overall it is a beautifully designed hotel in a great location. A real touch of luxury to our stay in London.

Title: Service let the whole place down
ReviewText: First room I got smelled funny. They parked my friends car in the car pack and decided to take almost 30 mins to bring the car to the front which lead to us missing dinner reservation. No apology from the concierge. Checked out the hotel and reviewed receipt which showed parking charges on my bill even though my car wasn’t parked there. I called them up and they advised it would be refunded (£55) checked my account and they refunded £5. Called again and they advised this will have to be investigated. What a joke if a hotel service will never be staying here again

Title: stay at the suite
ReviewText: they did not get it right

Title: First Class Service
ReviewText:
Beautiful well positioned hotel that was spotlessly clean. 

Staff were very helpful and professional. 

Would highly recommend.

Title: Review for London Marriott County Hall
ReviewText: We had an extremely relaxed stay due to its excellent location and great service.

Title: Altogether a very pleasant stay
ReviewText: Hotel was everything we expected.  Staff were particularly helpful, friendly and efficient without being overbearing or intrusive. Lovely overall impression.

Title: Beautiful hotel
ReviewText: Beautiful hotel, the staff are amazing. The beds so comfortable. Amazing facilities. Cannot fault.

Title: Best place to stay in London
ReviewText: My husband and I enjoyed our stay here, the service was great, food was amazing and we loved our room. It was spacious, cozy and very clean. We will definitely be back.

Title: Great riverside choice that you pay for
ReviewText: A dramatic Grade-II listed property, County Hall is situated on the banks of the River Thames and in 2022 will celebrate 100 years since being built.  This 5-star Marriott takes prime position within a shared use building that was previously the 64-year home of the London local government.  Many features have remained and with prime balcony views of Big Ben & Parliament, this property is as iconic as the fireworks that burst into life in front of it every new year in the shadow of the London Eye. Refurbished to a fantastic standard using the secondary brand palette just a few years ago, bedrooms are stylish, well appointed with many having a fabulous view of the river.  It has a well positioned M-Lounge that looks out to Londons most famous clock tower as well as a 25m swimming pool, well equipped and spacious gym & a fabulous afternoon tea in a classic library setting. A short walk brings guests to the South Bank where theatre, restaurants and entertainment is readily available as well as being in close walking proximity to Covent Garden or the tube network.  Overall this Cat-8 is typically one of the more expensive properties in London and provides guests a solid Marriott experience in a fantastic location.

Title: Family stay
ReviewText: A nice hotel in a fabulous location

Title: Lovely stay
ReviewText: Really lovely stay and overall good service. Thank you for the memories!

Title: Ok stay but not worth the cost
ReviewText: We decided to stay here as we were having afternoon tea in the library. The best thing about the hotel is the location. We paid a lot of money to stay but we’re unfortunately disappointed. The beds were not changed daily, we were advised it was once every 3 days unless the bedding was dirty etc. Our bedding was visibly dirty with stains and the staff did not change the bedding, we had to contact reception for this to happen. When we arrived we also had to request missing items from reception that should have been in the room already; not the best service given what we paid for.

Title: Excellent location but average breakfast disappointing
ReviewText: Location, rooms, pool, check in excellent...the policy of on hot/one cold food and drink at breakfast was disappointing esp with kids

Title: Under staffed, resulting in poor service level
ReviewText: I was disappointed with the level of service I received, mainly because I felt the hotel was under staffed and therefore it was not what I expected and certainly did not meet the standard and value I expected.

Title: Family staying
ReviewText:
Good value for money.
We would highly recommend this place!

Title: Great Staff great Location.. good service in Covid times
ReviewText: My wife and I stayed for a staycation visit to London. Front desk went the extra ile to make our stay nice. Rooms are very nice although we had to change first one because it had a bath not shower and second one had been smoked in which was a shame. We eventually had a great room with view over thames. M CLub staff were also excellent although the choice of wines was a bit limited .. Breakfast was OK but think because of Covid restrictions choice was limited. Great place to base for walks along thames.. and great staff that we met

Title: Brilliant stay
ReviewText: Brilliant weekend spent at this hotel.  Perfect location for us, and the parking is an added bonus!

Title: Hotel stay
ReviewText: My stay was outstanding. We got to check in early and even got a room upgrade to a room with a view of the London eye. I can’t wait to come back and stay with my daughter

Title: 40th Birthday Celebration
ReviewText: We visited as a group of friends celebrating a milestone birthday. This is an amazing hotel with beautifully appointed rooms and public spaces. The views of the Thames and Westminster were stunning. But the true highlight is the staff: from Graham the doorman to the staff in the executive lounge on the 12th of June everyone we met was friendly and helpful and nothing was too much trouble. The endless complimentary bubbles were much appreciated. We are already planning to return for our next celebration.

Title: Excellent 5* Hotel
ReviewText: Excellent location for this Marriott hotel. Definitely 5* for the upkeep of the property and the exceptional service from staff in all areas.  Would have no hesitation in recommending and my daughter and I will look forward to returning on our next London break.

Title: Food range and quality is the only let down
ReviewText: A wonderful stay and wonderful staff with great facilities but the food range is too narrow and unless one wants to eat steak there is very little else. Nothing Asian, very few healthy options. We ordered takeaway and that worked well

Title: John Butler
ReviewText:
Very good.
Excellent service.
Helpful staff.
Good food.
Bathroom sliding doors did not close properly. Not good for teenage daughter.

Title: Great London Hotel
ReviewText: Perfect location with very professional and accommodating staff. Great room views and right in the middle of all the great sights of London. The gym was exceptionally great and also included a spectacular lap pool. All around it was a very pleasant stay, and me and my wife will be returning in the future.

Title: Nice hotel
ReviewText: Good hotel, good facilities, good gym and good size pool. Everything good.

Title: Not a category 8 Hotel
ReviewText:
Corridors had not be cleaned after maintenance work had been carried out on floor 2. No draft beer available. 
Sanitising units not refilled in a suitable timescale considering the current situation.

No one on the entrance to welcome and deal with luggage when we arrived.

Overall I feel the experience of the hotel was not what I expected for a catergory 8.

Title: Our Doorman
ReviewText: Can I just say that your Doorman Graham was fantastic he really made our six year olds weekend.  What a great was to start our stay at the hotel, so helpful and friendly.

Title: Brilliant.
ReviewText: Faultless. Great staff, super clean, well organised, centre of everything.

Title: Don’t go if you celebrating  a special occasion
ReviewText: Don’t go if celebrating a special occasion they don’t care

Title: Amazing staff
ReviewText: The staff are so wonderful! They make the whole experience so worthwhile. Hotel is clean and the room was lovely, as expected.
Member of staff called Andre made our stay 100

Title: Amazing hotel, but it needs a bit more love.
ReviewText: The location of this hotel is spectacular, however the hotel needs more cleaning and the staff needs training, specially during breakfast as we got the wrong order multiple times. Being a long term Marriott customer I was expecting more. Although I must say that despite the issues we had with our room, the staff was very friendly.

Title: Best Marriott in London
ReviewText: Great hotel right near everything you need,London Eye and children's activities are within few minutes walking distance. The hotel is situated on the Thames so ideal for everyone and sightseeing.  Best place to be in london and for solo travellers,groups and children. Definitely worth a visit

Title: Great Experience
ReviewText: Highly recommend this place. Fantastic experience

Title: Weekend Away
ReviewText: Post lockdown stay. The hotel spotless and the staff friendly and professional. Room fantastic with a view of the London Eye. Close to everything you need to see and do. Would recommend and definitely will go back.

Title: Visit to the capital
ReviewText: Lovely break to see the sites and experience some fine dining - allocated a beautiful room with a view of the river. Spotlessly clean and great shower

Title: Perfect
ReviewText: We had the most wonderful stay at Marriott County Hall. Right from check-in the staff were amazing, with a glass of sparkling wine on arrival and decorating the room brilliantly for a birthday we were celebrating, staff also ensured the cake was peanut free and what requirements we had as a party. The location of the hotel was perfect and the room was amazing, with views of the Thames and the London eye, we couldn’t have asked for better. We booked a time at the pool due to covid restrictions but this was easy done. The breakfast in the restaurant was lovely and the service was great. We had the best stay here and would definitely recommend this hotel to others and we will be staying again!

Title: The best hotel stay in the UK
ReviewText: I visited the Marriott County Hall London with my family and had the most amazing stay. The service was impeccable, the staff were all exceptional and nothing was too much trouble. I would 100% recommend this hotel to anyone visiting London and we will definitely be visiting again.

Title: Restored!
ReviewText: I booked this hotel for a two-night stay just for me after restrictions were lifted in the UK because I was desperate for a change of scenery. And I had the best time.  Nothing was too much trouble, the staff were exceptionally professional and friendly and the food was amazing!  The hotel's location makes it easy to explore London and I'd happily go back again

Title: Did not expect from 5 stars hotel
ReviewText: Lovely staff, but dirty towel (with drops of blood) and terrible food on dinner. Pizza looked like it was done week ago. Absolutely uncomfortable cultery (from wood)

Title: Elite Not!
ReviewText: The hotel is in a good location but the customer service is terrible.  The money spent can be used at a cheaper hotel with better amenities and treatment. Breakfast is not worth it, eat elsewhere if you have to have breakfast.

Title: An outstanding hotel on the banks of Westminster.
ReviewText:
Gorgeous hotel, super friendly staff, great central location with amazing rooms and facilities.

Simply outstanding and would thoroughly recommend! 

20 min walk to Covent Garden.

Title: Review
ReviewText: Very difficult to book by phone only through website that wasn't easy because I need family room for two children and car park and the website doesn't let,  hotel seems to be very good but probably due this pandemic times were short on staff so all services were very slow

Title: Love County Hall
ReviewText: Great service ask for Mr Babu Asst Front Desk Manager

Title: Bad experience due to marriot cuts
ReviewText: As the biggest hotel chain you should find a way to maintain quality of service

Title: Business one-off Stay
ReviewText:
Used the hotel for one-off business purposes. Hotel very well organised and clean for social distancing purposes. Felt very safe. 
Although all facilities were closed and menu options limited, room service was very efficient and prompt, food was very good. 
Staff very friendly, responsive and helpful. 
Look forward to returning when hotel eventually returns to full operations.

Title: London
ReviewText: Excellent location and amenities. I had a little problem in the steakhouse with my meal but they took care of it very professionally but overall we had a great time.

Title: Great London experience
ReviewText: One of my best experience in all my travels.  Would love to go back and visit

Title: Great Location!
ReviewText: Loved the location, central to everything we wanted to see and do and most of it within walking distance.

Title: Parents visiting daughter at LSE
ReviewText: I would highly recommend this hotel. Extremely clean and up to date.  The staff went way out of their way to make us feel comfortable.  It was my husband’s birthday during the stay and they went above and beyond in treating him royally.  Love that it was located so close to so many sites.

Title: Awful
ReviewText: This hotel was impossible to work with. They did not reposed to my concerns at all. Avoid Marriott whenever possible they are thieves!

Title: The Center of the Eye
ReviewText:
The County Hall located behind the London Eye.  Puts you in the center of the experienceS in London.  Walk along the Thames in Southwark from Parliament to  the Tower Bridge. Big Ben smiles across the river at you. The london eye and river cruises are just out front of the hotel.  The County Hall is in the center of attention of southwark.  Along the River you can find music and restaurants on the walk. When your tired, peace can be found in her executive lounge.  A full English breakfast will start your day in the Gilroy restaurant.  A friendly staff is waiting to greet you.  
Enjoy

Title: Excellent service staff stay! Perfect location.
ReviewText: This is the place to stay in London in my opinion and I will stay here every time I go back to London if I

Title: Room with a view
ReviewText: Excellent location, lovely room, beautiful view but the heating was poor meaning the coldest hotel I've stayed in. Maintenance man was friendly and helpful with a small electric heater but the room was still below 20 degrees, which is less than I'd expect.

Title: Wonderful place
ReviewText:
We really enjoyed the Marriott County Hall.
The location is perfect and the staff very professional and friendly.

Title: Excellent location and stunning views
ReviewText: Marriott County Hall has a perfect location close to all you want; the cultural joints of Southbank, 15 min walk to Covent Garden and the theatres, always a superquick and kind door man. We never waited for more than a minute for a taxi! Lobby, reception and bar and the Gillray´s impressed. The breakfast could have consisted of a few more items. We loved our spacious room with a stunning view over Big Ben and the Parliament.

Title: Great stay
ReviewText: Central to most historical sights by foot, there’s also a hop-on bus tour right out front that can take you anywhere. Hotel Rooms are quite, clean and you feel safe.  I’m a seasoned traveler and Often interact with Marriott staff across the world, this staff were all engaging, friendly and offered great suggestions to maximize you time in London. They also have a full time cab stationed at the hotel that can take you any where.  I took a peek at the hotel gym and it was very well equipped and staffed.

Title: Great stay
ReviewText: Stayed at the Hotel for three nights. It didn’t disappoint. Very clean & polite staff

Title: Great Location!
ReviewText: The hotel location is great for couples and families and has great views.

Title: Best Marriott In the world for sure
ReviewText: I can’t this property enough for the way they take care of my family and I when I am there.  This is the only property I feel as if I am at home and not a hotel.  I want to give out a shout out to Jemma W., Manuel from Guest Relations, Zane and Gloreita from the Executive Lounge. You guys made my friends 50th Birthday incredibly special.

Title: Great Hotel
ReviewText: Lovely hotel. Great location and very friendly and helpful staff. Not the best restaurant so I’d avoid.

Title: Comfortable and convenient with really good staff.
ReviewText: The Marriot was close to the House of Lords and was first class. With the staff it’s best feature. Breakfast was enhanced with the help of Sandra and Gina. Excellent

Title: Great stay
ReviewText: This hotel was amazing.  Great location for a week in London.  A five minute walk to the Underground and close to Big Ben, Parliament and the London Eye.  We were greeted every time we entered the hotel.  Our room was spacious with two queen beds.  The bathroom was large with nice amenities.  We had access to the Executive Lounge and ate breakfast there every morning.  Nice selection of food.  The hotel has old world charm.

Title: Wonderful stay!
ReviewText: I was treated like a valued guest from the moment I walked into the hotel - my room was ready (and upgraded!) upon my early morning arrival. The location could not have been a better launching point to explore London - so convenient to walk to major sites and very easy Underground access.  The executive lounge staff went out of their way to help me enjoy my stay with friendly service and recommendations.  I was delighted with my guest experience and will make it a point to visit again at this property. Thank you for a wonderful stay!

Title: O melhor atendimento!!!
ReviewText: Tem uma equipe de atendimento maravilhosa, sempre preocupada em melhorar a experiência dos hóspedes

Title: Wonderful hotel, excellent staff, poor policy on service charges
ReviewText: This is an impressive hotel in a superb and convenient setting. Staff are friendly and efficient, however the policy of slapping a service charge on absolutely everything is annoying especially when it relates to a buffet breakfast.

Title: Excellent séjour à Londres Hotel parfait.
ReviewText: Hotel tres bien situé,chambre et salle de bain propre et confortable literie parfaite cafetière Nespresso bien agréable, petit déjeuner très copieux et excellent,personel chaleureux et agréable. Très bon séjour à Londres

Title: Great hotel with some minor issues to fix
ReviewText:
I loved the location of the hotel but didn't really fell great about three things:
-The location of the lifts is too far away form some rooms.
- The food at The Library as well as the in-room dining were underwhelming and pretty expensive for what they gave us.
- The way into the pool feels very convoluted and the whole code system to get into the pool gets old pretty quickly.

Title: Location and view
ReviewText: Excellent location great size room very nice lounge

Title: CUSTOMER SERVICE A+
ReviewText: The people in the front desk and the entrance were amazing!!!!!

Title: Excellent location!
ReviewText: The hotel is well situated in London and provided us a great anchor to build our trip itinerary! The service is good and the club lounge is perfect!

Title: Great Hotel and Location. Very Poor staff attitude at departure
ReviewText: Great location and 2 day break in London. Nice hotel but really let down by the lack of ability to provide service advertised and zero acceptance or apologies from staff. Very unlike Marriott in general but tarnished what was a great stay until this point.

Title: Excellent séjour
ReviewText: Comme à  l'habitude, cet hôtel du groupe Marriott rempli toutes les attentes!

Title: Great location, amenities and service. Everything you could want
ReviewText: Great location.  Convenient to restaurants , markets, pharmacy, shops, London Eye and Buckingham palace. Sleeping rooms stylish and comfortable, updated decor. We stayed in 2 connecting rooms which was super convenient for my family. Hotel restaurant and bar were great! Friendly service, prosecco on check in. Can't beat that. Will definitely stay here again!

Title: NEVER AGAIN
ReviewText: Hotel is tired looking,excluding the rooms, and employees capture this negative appearance. The food especially the Breakfast was in one word "disgusting" If this is BonVoy then goodbye!!!

Title: Weekend away
ReviewText: 10 year anniversary beautifully celebrated

Title: Awesome Experience
ReviewText: The property, staff, service and food were all excellent. Thank you for a memorable stay.

Title: Good overall
ReviewText: Apart from the steakhouse this is a fantastic hotel. Breakfast is good, location is great and overall the staff are amazing. Especially the porters and the breakfast staff

Title: 一次愉快的体验
ReviewText: 酒店地理位置优越，靠近西敏寺和泰晤士河，去大英博物馆也很方便。酒店服务人员态度非常nice，酒廊风景很好，提供的餐饮也比较充足，以后来伦敦一定会再次入住！

Title: Sophisticated
ReviewText: Sophisticated without pretense.

Title: Great stay
ReviewText: Perfect location, great staff and great room, made our stay a very pleasurable experience. Thanks guys! We will be back again. Merry Christmas!!

Title: Xmas break
ReviewText:
We stayed here as a last minute Xmas break.
Really can't fault the hotel from start to finish.
Beautiful hotel excellent staff.
The gin selection at the bar was amazing

Title: Best Marriott in London
ReviewText: An excellent hotel that is very conveniently located. A good value in a rather expensive city.

Title: 美丽的酒店，糟糕的管理
ReviewText: 位置好，环境佳，管理差！非常不满意。每天的卫生打扫不干净彻底，完全达不到五星级酒店标准，时常遗漏毛巾少给，需要打电话跟前台要，拖鞋也是好几天不换。最夸张的是有一天居然在晚上十点多响消防警报，我已经在床上仍不得不下楼，到楼下才被告知是同一栋楼里其他商业引发了火警，没事让我回房间。要想这是晚上十点多啊！当晚果然失眠了！

Title: HONEYMOON STAY!
ReviewText: we loved everything about the hotel! they treated us with such kindness! our honeymoon was so amazing spending our time there in London before our next destination! they surprised us with balloons, champagne, chocolate, ice skating! it was PERFECT!!!! Everything was in walking distance, the views were amazing, the holiday decor we loved!

Title: Lousy Restaurant but great Hotel.
ReviewText: Do not eat at their Steakhouse! Service is absolutely dreadful and the food was horrible! Eat out! The hotel is lovely and it was extremely fun walking to so many sites close by!

Title: Família con niños
ReviewText: Estancia perfecta en el centro de Londres,Un plus,habitación con vistas,personal muy atento y servicial en todo momento.Hizo que el viaje fuera inolvidable.

Title: Grand
ReviewText: Fantastic property in an iconic building. Interior of hotel is a bit out dated but rooms are very modern. Regular rooms tend to be a short on space. Recommend going for an executive room/ suite. Rooms with views on the London eye and river are amazing.

Title: Great Tourist Location
ReviewText:
Overall, the Marriott Hotel County Hall was a great hotel because it was walking distance to many major tourists sites. Location was key. 

The one thing that this hotel lacked was the individual attention to the guest. It was a nice hotel, but the atmosphere did not feel warm and welcoming.

Title: Absolutely lovely
ReviewText: From check in - to check out - everything was perfect.  The hotel staff is amazing and the hotel is very elegant.

Title: Prime Location, Quirky Hotel
ReviewText: Marriott London Hotel County Hall holds a prime location on the Thames river, with superb views from the (added cost) "Parliament-View" rooms.  The rooms themselves were spacious, modern and luxurious.  Food in Gillray's Steakhouse (both dinner and breakfast) was excellent quality and superb service.  The hotel's decor is a pleasant combination of traditional and "transitional".  Room service made multiple errors in a single order, but quickly rectified all errors with rapid service.  M-Lounge is tiny, dreary and overall disappointing.  Evening hors d'oeuvres supplied parsimoniously by "table service" in minuscule bowls, with up-charges for many items.

Title: London trip
ReviewText:
Week with the family in London - great location.
