Check-in snooty and deceiving but otherwise great trip and stay.  Beds hard and cookie crumbs under bed but beautiful view when you get lucky with room placement.  Staff excellent.

Title: Great overall
ReviewText: Room, service, and location were excellent! Location is ideal!

Title: Customer Service is the Worst
ReviewText:
I went to great length prior to our stay to ensure a positive outcome.
This included several calls to Marriott Corporate.
I was denied early check in upon arrival after traveling from the US, even though it was previously requested.
I was denied a room upgrade upon arrival even though it was previously requested.
I called the Manager on Duty, however he could not speak with me and stated he would call me back.
I waited 24 hours.  There was no call back.  I called again.  I was promised a call.  I never received one.
I called Marriott Corporate.  They simply made excuses for the manager and the occurrences.
I find the entire situation despicable given my level with Marriott.
Dr. John

Title: Fabulous Remodeled Hotel in a prime site
ReviewText: The new decor is charming.  Rooms quiet and restful.  Do ask about room location, as there is a single elevator bank for a very large number of rooms.  Mine was very distant to the elevators.  Access to theater and sightseeing was great.

Title: Staying in Mariott hotel county Hall  Nov 1-4 2019
ReviewText: I have stayed in the hotel for many times. The hotel room is impressive in term of board view and cleanliness. I can always enjoy a quiet environment during my stay. However, the front desk staff are unable to deliver a warm feeling to guest . Perhaps they are all new as  the mobility of staff there is too high!

Title: Amazing Hotel
ReviewText:
Where do I begin?? Your hotel, staff, location, cleanliness, space was 5 + stars
This was our first time in London and I was thrilled with everything.  
Location - perfectly situated for walking to all sites and transportation was close to everything we wanted to experience
Staff - AMAZING... everyone was so helpful from recommendations, circling points of interest on a map for us, welcome cocktails, surprise cupcake for my birthday in the room and everyone was so welcoming and friendly 
Cleanliness - Hotel was spotless, room was immaculate and we noted more than once that the staff was constantly cleaning and maintaining the property overall (we left for Paris right after London and I can tell you that this was not case at all)
I can promise that we will definitely be back and staying with you again... I will always recommend your hotel to all. Thank you all for making our family vacation and celebrations a beautiful forever memory...

Title: Fantastic stay
ReviewText: The only thing we regret was the fact that our stay was to short. The Hotel is in a great location, unique views from the venues and from our accomodation. Exceptional food offerings and most important the very kind associates have made our stay. We will return for sure.

Title: Disappointed
ReviewText:
1-Check in delayed by 45 mins room was not ready. 
2-while I waited for room to be ready I decided to have a drink but it took 25 mins to get a drink. 
3- I specifically booked room with view of parliament but It was not what I paid for. 
4- room was noisy I could not get proper sleep

Title: Mitigé
ReviewText:
Quelques peu déçus de la propreté de certaines serviettes et d’une mauvaise expérience au moment du départ. Nous avons être orienté vers la solution d’un taxi au lieu des transports en commun et avons failli rater notre avion.
L’hôtel est idéalement situé dans un bâtiment magnifique. Les chambres et les prestations sont de qualité et l’accueil du personnel était parfait. Dommage pour les 2 bémols sus-cités...

Title: Great Suite, good ambience.
ReviewText: The Westminster Suite is fabulous, big enough to accommodate my needs, hope to come back soon.

Title: Perfect location!
ReviewText: Location is a +!!  Friendly and attentive service!!  Great food!!  Must experience Tea Time there!!  Spacious Bedrooms! Spent 6 nights there and had no complaints...would definitely rebook!!

Title: The big smoke
ReviewText: Fantastic hotel in a prime position in the capital. Couldn’t fault anything

Title: County Hall London
ReviewText: Great location, Exceptional service. will definitely stay there again.

Title: Why not if you’re in town
ReviewText: Bit expensive but great if you have Marriott points.

Title: Very Bad experience
ReviewText:
The staff in reception desk very unskilled. We had problem checkin and checkout procedures. They do not want to take my sister in the room in spite of my e-mail. She had to wait my plane arrival about 4-5 hours in the lobby.
They took all provisions of room charge during checkin but they asked credit card again for checkout. My credit card are blocked because of bank policy and provision are still witing in my bank(after 4 days of my checkout). Because the staff in reception does not know meaning of provision. Anyway we paid charges and took 45 öinutes. My chind was very anxious and cried. I will naot stay any of Marriot again!

Title: Poor
ReviewText: Room not as requested. Queue at check-in and check-out. No space at breakfast. Staff member let themselves into room when we were asleep.

Title: Nice Stay
ReviewText: This review process is to long! Please make this shorter when revised.

Title: 5 days in London
ReviewText: Location is amazing hotel stately check in nice good touch offering a glass of champagne. Bar service was slow and snack food list at bar needs work  hotel is large Walt to room may be long. View from room was worth the walk.  Service not up to Marriot standards

Title: Exceptional Hotel & Service
ReviewText: The hotel itself is absolutely amazing! Everything is exactly what you would picture for  quintessential London. The service was the best we've ever received from a Marriott. Upon check-in we were walked to the Library to make our tea reservation and given a quick tour of the grounds- I cannot remember the young mans name but his service was stellar, he was very knowledgeable about the grounds and seemed to genuinely care about our stay. My favorite part(s) of the stay were the daily chats with Graham the doorman while we waited for our cab. He is exactly what I picture when I think of London and the formality & kindness I've grown to expect. He really is something special and made us feel very special. I'm looking forward to coming back to this outstanding property & team. Thank you so much!

Title: eine tolle Lage und schönes Ambiente
ReviewText: Wir hatten ein schönes großes Familienzimmer mit Teilblick auf das London Eye. Die Lage ist unschlagbar, da man bereits einige Attraktionen direkt vor Ort hat (London Eye, Aquarium, River Cruise, Bus Hop on Hop Off von dieversen Anbietern etc.).

Title: Great location for a London stay
ReviewText: This hotel is wonderfully centrally located and very accessible to several Underground stations. It made it very easy getting around town throughout our stay. It was always very clean and neat in the public areas as well as guest rooms.

Title: Location, location, location
ReviewText: When we arrived at the County Hall we were concerned that we'd feel like we were staying in Disney Land.  There's an amusement park atmosphere to the surrounding area.  But once inside, the London Eye becomes simply a great piece of scenery and all that hubbub recedes. We walked everywhere easily, and what wasn't walkable was one or two tube stops away.  The hotel is well-appointed, though sprawling.   Our room clocked in at .3 miles from the Lobby, adding at least a mile to every day's walking total.  If you qualify for an upgrade, the executive lounge is just fine for a light continental breakfast, but certainly not worth the $30+ upcharge.  The beds are the best, the bathroom and storage not so much.  Superior hotels should have dresser drawers, adequate bathroom storage and most of all, a bathtub.  The location trumps these fairly minor complaints.  We'll come again the next time we visit London.

Title: Eye of london
ReviewText: One of my best trips loved everything 5 star experience

Title: worst
ReviewText: Not worth staying and spending so much on the room tariff at this hotel. This hotel has no good service, no good food, no good house keeping no good room service,..Just has a good location advantage.. apart from the location rest everything is not comfortable in this hotel..

Title: Week Stay
ReviewText: Overall very good. Loved the doormen. Ambiance of the entire establishment. Would stay again

Title: Anniversary weekend
ReviewText: Excellent staff support during our stay. Everyone was very friendly and welcoming. Next visit to London we know where we are staying

Title: People put it over the top
ReviewText: As usual the staff were very helpful and friendly. The Doormen start the experience in a very positive way with their greeting and enjoyable conversation. Never had anything but ease and convenience with all of the staff. Rooms are well sized and the beds are always comfortable. The river view is great, especially when the clock tower is not being repaired. Hillary’s and the bar have great selections and quality with friendly staff. Over all, the hotel staff seem happy that you’re there and they make it apparent.

Title: One of the best stays
ReviewText:
The hotel was very nice and clean. Staffs were super friendly and kind. Everyone was very attentive and seemed happy to be there. 
Doorman Graham was so sweet and caring. His magic was so cute. We were very pleased by the hotel.

Title: Exceptional Stay
ReviewText: The hotel is elegant with class but also a good dose of fun energy. Lovely modern decor, fantastic staff, positive energy throughout. The breakfast was superb, all around a great stay. Perfect place too if you have a short stay and want to squeeze in some tourist fun.

Title: Great location and hotel - terrible wifi unfortunately.
ReviewText: Great staff and location - close to lots of historic London for walking. Also super close to 2 underground stations.

Title: Best place in London to Stay
ReviewText: Location is right at the London Eye, Westminister Abby is a 15 minute walk and the Palace is about 30 minute walk. The Tube (subway) is across the bridge to hotel. Waterloo Station is a 15 minute walk. The hotel is great! The staff is excellent. Only thing is ask for a high numbet room on your floor. There is only 3 lifts (elevators) on one side of the hotel. So if you end up with a low number room a floor it's a look walk to your room.

Title: Excellent location and a nice building , but huge building which might require walking a very long distance to get to the room.
ReviewText: With some improvements this hotel will be great

Title: Nadie nos esperaba
ReviewText:
Bastante decepcionante ya que nadie nos esperaba. No encontraban la reserva con nuestros apellidos así que tuvimos que mostrar el email con la reserva hecha a través de Booking.com. Acaso sólo esperan a los que hacen reserva directa con ellos?
Nadie acompaña a la habitación. 
No estaban las cunas solicitadas en la reserva.
Reservé habitación vista PARLAMENTO y nos dieron con vista al LONDON EYE.
ACLARO que no hice reclamo porque estábamos sin tiempo y con dos niños. En un viaje de placer con la familia no tenía la mínima gana de perder el tiempo. Se supone que el hotel tiene que tener todo previsto con antelación.

Title: What a vacation!
ReviewText: My wife and I needed a breather and the the London Marriott County Hall gave us just that. The staff was friendly, courteous and always willing to help. We especially enjoyed the Concierge Lounge where the food was fantastic and the views were great. I should note this lounge was open every day/all day. If I return to London, I will stay here and not bother to look elsewhere. My gratitude to the entire staff for making this a great vacation!

Title: Nice Hotel, Great Location
ReviewText: Great hotel to stay in for a visit to London. Clean, comfortable and conveniently located.

Title: 25th Wedding Anniversary
ReviewText: Stayed here as part of our Silver Wedding Anniversary celebrations. The perfect base, we had a room with a balcony overlooking the London Eye and River Thames. The staff at the hotel are quite brilliant. Nothing was too much. Perfect, thank you so much.

Title: 絶好のロケーション
ReviewText:
ロケーションはロンドンアイの脇、ビッグベンの対岸と絶好の場所にあり、観光に大変便利なところです。

建物は重厚感、格式感があります。
スタッフの対応も一流で値段に対してふさわしいと思いました。

シャワーの排水が悪いのは建物自体が古いからなのでしょうか。
しかし部屋は綺麗でなんら問題はありませんでした。

Title: Surprise refurbished county jail
ReviewText: Restored beautiful hotel everything great except needs attention to it elevator service

Title: Perfect location
ReviewText: Loved it and were able to walk almost everywhere-

Title: Perfect room with a view
ReviewText: Loved my very short stay. But, I was able to take advantage of the great location. Everything you need and more!

Title: Great location and beautiful rooms
ReviewText: Very easy to walk to attractions and rooms were updated and beautiful

Title: Great Hotel
ReviewText:
Great hotel, great location. Pretty
Decent rooms and facilities although toiletries rather limited as were the service times for the evening staff in gillrays bar (especially given the price). However, great stay!

Title: Loved the London Marriott Hotel County Hall!
ReviewText: I had a quick business trip and the staff and hotel were awesome! Provided early check in, (gave a me a few hours to catch up on some sleep) room upgrade to an unbelievable view of the London Eye and the Thames! Room service was courteous and quick! Food arrived so fast I couldn't believe it and it was delicious! Will definitely stay there again on my next trip! 5 stars from me!

Title: Decepción
ReviewText: Restaurante malo, zonas comunes pequeñas (grande por fuera, pero pequeño por dentro), servicio lento, buena voluntad del staff y gerencia aunque los resultados no acompañan.

Title: Poor Service
ReviewText: Poor service on all ratings. Very disappointed. Please review feedback provided.

Title: Stunning Hotel
ReviewText: This hotel can only be described as stunning and the staff are so friendly - the location could not have been better.  The staff overall bent over backwards to ensure you had a good stay with the exception of the breakfast staff and it is the only thing that lets it down.  The wait for a table / food and quality of the food in the breakfast buffet is nothing short of shocking considering the prices charged and the hotel rating.

Title: Family Options are Limiting/Bonvoy Status Worthless
ReviewText: A family of four can only be accommodated in a 350 sq. ft. "Family Room". Don't be fooled by online description, these are modestly sized, basically outfitted rooms. Inexplicably, 700 sq. ft. suites can only accommodate 3 people, and I was not allowed to buy an upgrade. For some strange reason we could not use the executive lounge even though I have lifetime status.  Otherwise, nice centrally located property with excellent views.  Also, renovation from an old government building was interesting, but the hotel entrance and Pentagon length hallways meant getting into/out of the hotel room from street was a good quarter mile.

Title: Great accomodations, location and staff
ReviewText: The location of this hotel in London is excellent for all of the London sites.  The staff is very friendly and accommodating.  Our room was very nice.  The daily breakfast was outstanding and great to start a long day of walking and sight seeing.  I would highly recommend staying here.

Title: 3 nights in London
ReviewText: Great location for sightseeing.  The hotel is upscale with large rooms.  The staff is friendly and were very helpful about getting us taxis to dinner and shows.

Title: Exceptionnel !
ReviewText: Le séjour a été exceptionnel. Le sens du détail et de la perfection sont au rendez vous !

Title: London Marriott County Hall
ReviewText: Excellent location.  View of the London Eye from room!!  Nice bar.  Great steakhouse at property.  Centrally located within walking distance to many tourist spots.  Indoor pool also a fun plus!

Title: Just Average
ReviewText: Nice hotel that falls short on service. Hotel could be easily upgraded by paying attention to detail Location is fantastic but the hotel is not

Title: Perfect London hotel
ReviewText: County Hall is the perfect London hotel.  Location is fantastic, easy Tube access and the doormen at the hotel do a wonderful job at hailing cabs and taking care of any transportation needs. Breakfast is excellent, rooms are very comfortable with great views (we were in 302), shower is fantastic (not always something you find in London). Staff were very friendly and went above and beyond to make our daughter's college graduation celebration special by storing a cake for us and then delivering it with plates/cutlery when we were ready and even presented our daughter with a gift certificate to the restaurant. Fitness center is large and well equipped. Can't say enough about the County Hall, and it is steps away from a Starbucks! Thanks for a wonderful visit.

Title: Wonderful vacation stay at the County Hall!
ReviewText: The hotel is gorgeous and centrally located.  The suites are modern and have amazing views.  As an elite member, my welcome gifts were amazing and personalized.  They even put together little snack bags for my kids.

Title: Excellent hotel
ReviewText: Great convenient location and food service

Title: Superb Stay
ReviewText: Friendly, excellent level of attention, room was to a high level. Highly recommended.

Title: 非常好
ReviewText: 環境優美服務周到出行方便能很好的休息工作人員態度很好

Title: overpriced small rooms and not recognizing Platinum Elite membership
ReviewText:
Maybe this hotel is already very busy. Either way, I would not recommend this hotel. 

The Good: The location is very good. Directly in the city centre and good access the transaportation. The rooms are clean and relatively recently refurbished. 

The challenges: 
The hotel did not recognise Ambassador status, no suite upgrades where available. 
The lounge service is poorly organised. Quality of breakfast is sub-par for a hotel of this category. 
During "happy hour", the rule is that patrons shall not pick up drinks or food themselves. But that some snacks would be intended to be served by staff. The challenge with this rule occurs when not enough staff is present (which happened three out of three times during my stay). Then guests have to wait somewhere between 10 and 30 minutes for a few bites of snacks while the staff is slowly moving from one table to the next. Not exactly a great experience. (I can understand the intent from the hotel to reduce the consumption of lounge perks by guests, but keeping people waiting due to under-staffing is not exactly making your guests feel welcome (nor appreciated if you happen to be an Ambassador guest). 

The price of the room per night is quite high, compared to the small room size. And the room is apparently too tiny for even a proper desk, so for business travelers who like to work on some emails and their computer, not exactly ideal. 

Overall the tiny room, lack of work space for business travelers, no recognition of Marriott status, these factors lead me to recommend to better stay at another hotel. 

If all you need is a good location with a clean minimalistic room, this hotel is for you. If you care about service and have expectations that hope for some of the above, I recommend you choose a different hotel.

Title: My stay in London
ReviewText: While I've stated the Marriott County Hall for business, this was the first time staying with family. And they enjoyed the hotel just as much as I have in the past. The staff is very friendly, the amenities are first class and the rooms are spacious.

Title: Hotel
ReviewText: very nice hotel great location

Title: Beautiful rooms
ReviewText: Totally recommend. Great location and gorgeous rooms

Title: Great Place to start your holiday
ReviewText: We visited London with our grand daughter who had never been there before.  It's location was a great plus to us.  It was convenient for transportation, and walking.   We had a wonderful time.   The Staff went out of there way to make us feel special.

Title: A Bittersweet Experience of 1 and 5 stars
ReviewText:
Went to London to celebrate our anniversary. Have been a long time Marriott advocate but...
1) Took several attempts to get contact from hotel to arrange transport from the airport. Finally got someone who told me "the price on our website is wrong and it's actually 20 pounds more".
2) Elite check in sign was there but ignored by front desk staff
3) Had asked for early check in; room not ready. Was told they would call when it was ready; no call. 
4) Paid extra for a "Big Ben" view which you could see if you leaned way out. This was offset nicely by the fact that those on the London Eye could see nicely into our room.
5) 1 star for taking over 2 hours to get our luggage to our room
6) 5 stars for including scales in our bathroom; 1 star for having to call twice to get it replaced because it was broken
7) 5 stars for knowing the trip was for our anniversary and providing a bottle of champagne; 1 star for not asking the actual date and delivering it 2 days early
8) 5 stars for the Executive lounge and it's excellent staff
9) 5 stars to the staff member who brought a wheelchair to our room and personally pushed my wife over to the emergency room at the hospital and kept asking about her for the rest of the stay
10) Internet was free to all but had to re-connect at least once per day 
11) Other than Executive Lounge staff, everyone seemed to be "going through the motions"
12) On checkout, wasn't even asked how my stay was (perhaps he anticipated the answer)

Used 300,000 points and paid an extra 420 pounds for a special occasion, and essentially ended up with a standard room that didn't have anything I expected.

Title: Excellent hotel, excellent stay!
ReviewText: I really enjoyed my two weeks in London at the County Hall Marriott.

Title: 2019 Trip
ReviewText: Overall a very nice hotel. Great location but had poor view.

Title: Average
ReviewText: Poor room in a handicap room with no mention at check in. Telephone didn’t work at all. No currency exchange available at front desk.

Title: Excellent
ReviewText: Highly recommended! As a beautiful hotel within a cultural grand building, the furniture and property maintenance are great.  Associates services are warmly welcome comes with a great sense of humor.  Please do keep up and looking forward to stay with this hotel next time.

Title: Nice and Clean Former London County Hall
ReviewText: Nice and Clean Former London County Hall, staffs are friendly and accommodating

Title: Poor and inhonesty service
ReviewText:
I do know know why people at the front desk or manager so unkind or unfair since this morning before and after checking out. Quite unpleasant. I feel some racism as well. I will report to the Marriott customer service.

1. I gave the info right after making my reservation a week or so ago that there will be my kids accompanying. Obviously, it was not reflected on my reservation.

2. I know upgrade is given depending on availability. When checking in, I saw two people checking in at the same time, they were promised of a river view or partial view for upgrade even though they were not gold members or paid extra. Stay using points does not mean that I should receive such a discriminative service. Hotel manager kept on saying no availability, but I doubted from the beginning since I observed their check-in process.

3. After checking in, I came into the room 607 and TV was not working. A technician came and did his best to fix, but nothing was resolved. We ended up wasting our precious time during this afternoon in London. 

4. There was also a sanitation issue with the coffee machine that the water tank had a glimpse of being used leaving water inside. I once had water poison issue that caused serious problem in the past. Such an unclean service is unacceptable. The usb charger hole on the wall dis not work, either. I did report all these problems and was moved to another room, which actually is much better than the former one. I could not help but thinking the hotel tries to give me less favorite room. Manager later on promised to give romantic package which I have not asked of, but it was a joke that I had also to spend some times to get rid of the sheds the artificial roses someone placed on the bed. It did make mess around the bed. 

5. I had to use a computer so that I asked if there is a computer in the hotel. The person at the front desk told me that I should not be able to use because I do not have access to the lounge in which he says there is one. I told him that I do have access and did go see check already and could not see any. But, he kept on insisting that there should be one and I need to go check again. I was kidded even though I said I did go check already. Okay. I went there again just just to check if I was missing it, but I was right. I came back and called again, but this time a woman in the front desk told me again that there should be one with unkind tone. I explained about what I have done. Nothing resolved or understood.

Title: Great Location
ReviewText: Enjoyed the entrance to this magnificent hotel. Excellent location for sightseeing and close to many fine restaurants.

Title: Great hotel
ReviewText: This hotel looked great when I did the research online but exceeded all my expectations! Location is perfect and near the train station and hop off-hop on buses. Well maintained hotel and staff is extremely friendly. Can’t wait to visit this city again.

Title: The Place to Stay While in London.
ReviewText: Perfect English Service, comfortable rooms, fantastic location make this hotel the place to stay in London.

ReviewText: Lovely hotel, friendly helpful staff, my second visit. Would definitely stay again. The hotel has bus and tube stops basically at the front door so is perfect for getting out and about sight seeing etc.

Title: Don't stay anywhere else!
ReviewText: We thoroughly enjoyed every aspect of our stay at this hotel while in London. Not only does the hop on/hop off bus stop right in front, it is highly walkable to many desirable locations; including Westminster Abbey, Trafalgar Square, Piccadilly Circus, and many shopping areas. The "Eye" is next to the hotel. The staff made us feel very welcome and the club snacks were outstanding.

Title: Marriott London Vacation
ReviewText: Beautiful hotel. Great location and food. needs more room amenities such as more 110v outlets and vanity with mirror to blow dry hair b/c you can’t do this in the bathroom.  Room very nice overall but very dirty carpets and inadequate daily cleaning of the room.

Title: London Bridge Did Not Fall Down!
ReviewText: I loved this hotel. From the valet to the front desk staff for a one night stay was white glove service. I am truly a hotel snob and not afraid to admit that to anyone. Other Marriott's can learn from what was delivered during my visit. When I return to London, this will of course be my first choice and I'm hopeful that I will be able to stay longer. The hotel is close to everything, the folks stayed on the phone with us as we were lost while driving to the location. They were patient and graciously directed us to their front door. I love this hotel and all that was experienced, albeit brief. I left knowing that I would return and that I'd get to see more of London. I'm a true believer that London Bridge did not fall down - at least not on my watch! LOL. Thank you to all the staff for delivering the best overnight stay I've had in years!

Title: Very nice hotel, great team, great location
ReviewText: I recommend to stay at the London Marriot Hotel County - I have spent 4 nights with my family (2 young kids) and it has been a great experience (calm, clean, classic/modern decoration). This hotel has a great location close by walk to a lot of spots (Westminster, St James Park, Piccadilly, Covent Garden...). High attention and availability from everyone. Great food both at breakfast and dinner, nice long heated long swimming.

Title: It deserves a stay
ReviewText: Everything was generally nice except the gym and swimming pool. The facilities were simply 'okay', but I thought it could be much better as a 'five-start hotel'. The facilities were kind of old (the shower room was unbelievably poor like a public pool! Come on, this is Marriott!?).

Title: Surprising number of hassles in this very nice property.
ReviewText:
Room 203 has a mechanical whirring sound in the wall between bed and bathroom that cycles every few seconds that will prevent a good nites sleep. 
Depending on your room location, prepare for a very long walk to your room, and an even longer one if you need ice.
At check-in, be sure you get fully registered otherwise your Wi-Fi will not work, your luggage will not be delivered, and you won’t be able to charge restaurant tabs to your room.
The location is perfect for visiting many sites across the river. (Just be prepared for the gauntlet of shell game hustlers on Westminster bridge.)
Well-maintained property that like any old building has its quirks. Nice room amenities.

Title: London April 2019

Title: Perfect Location, Quiet, Professional, Spectacular!
ReviewText: Loved staying here for business—it was centrally located to all the things we needed, had a beautiful view, yet was quiet enough to get a total good night's sleep. The staff was great, responded to all my requests via phone within 5 minutes. Hotel was linked up to the Marriott Bonvoy app and took them a few hours to respond to my request via chat there—also was able to  call directly through the app if I had a more urgent question. Overall, I loved my stay at the Marriott County Hall and wouldn't hesitate to stay there again.

Title: Birthday celebrations
ReviewText:
We just wanted to say how impressed we where with the hotel, staff and service during last weekends stay. The location was fantastic, staff were friendly and the balcony suite was amazing. Our 8 year old daughter was so impressed because she could see the London Eye so close to our room. This is the first hotel room we have stayed in with no disappointments about cleanliness and specification. A young lady at the check in desk called Rita even arranged a surprise birthday cake and card for our daughter and the gentlemen in the Library managing all the afternoon teas found a table for us to sit at, despite it being fully booked. 

We were also impressed with the swimming pool and room service.

A very enjoyable stay with peace and quiet away from the crowds.

Title: Great stay
ReviewText:
We accidentally chose this hotel and it was the perfect choice! We were one block from the underground ! Within walking distance of so much
The staff was the best ! Definitely would return

Title: Fantastic hotel
ReviewText: Fantastic hotel,,, everything from, arrival to departure. Staff are very friendly and welcoming, rooms are very clean, comfortable and calming the hotel is ideally situated for most attractions,,, fantastic place,, can't wait to come back in the future.

Title: Amazing hotel in an amazing location
ReviewText:
Do not hesitate to stay here!  It is a great hotel located in the best possible place - right at the center of everything 
The staff could not be more helpful and friendly and the lounge makes a trip so convenient

Title: You will not be disappointed!
ReviewText: We stayed one night on a return trip from Paris to fly out of Heathrow the following day. We arrived and immediately knew it was going to be a great stay. Our check in was prompt and all employees behind the desk were extremely friendly and accommodating. Even walked us to the elevator! We had reserved a room with a king bed and paid a nominal fee for a roll-away bed for our son. We paid a bit more for a river view room and we were so pleased when we opened the door. The roll-away was already in the room and made up, and it was such a nice surprise to see the newly renovated, very spacious corner room. We are home flippers in the US and were so inspired by the decor and finishes, we snapped photos of everything - from the bathroom with dual pocket doors, classic wall and floor tile, the London map-themed wallpaper, beautiful chrome fixtures and marbled porcelain sink, to the main room's wainscot walls and crown molding, contemporary cluster wall art, solid wood side tables, leather-wrapped lamp, integrated fabric headboard and beautifully selected paint colors...this room was really very dreamy. The single window overlooking the river was enormous and was decorated with a solar blind and heavy insulated curtains. We fully enjoyed our stay! Room service was incredibly fast and although we didn't use the pool, we checked it out and wished we had more time! This is a VERY touristy area because of the close proximity to London Eye (steps from the hotel), Big Ben, Parliament, and Westminster Abbey, but because its location right on the Thames, looking at crowds below did not bother us at all. Take some time to enjoy a cocktail and light bites at one of the cozy lounges on the main floor next to the restaurant and take in the view. The building is absolutely beautiful (an older building that housed some kind of political offices before it was renovated to the hotel). From the circular car entry way to the interior and exterior decorative appointments, this hotel is a gem and we will definitely stay if we visit London again!

Title: Marriott reliability in a great location
ReviewText: As I have come to expect from the Marriott brand, this hotel was clean, grand, well staffed and ideally located.

Title: Excellent stay in prime location
ReviewText:
Hotel is located in one of the core spots in London. Easy to access from two tube stations nearby (Westminster is the closest). Interesting building with active history. 
Efficient reception, nice and clean rooms with the possibility for a view over the Thames river / Big Ben (however, thick outside walls with relatively small window can narrow the sight). 
Nice pool on the top floor, long enough for real exercise.
Very pleasant bar and restaurant with casual country-style English Breakfast.
Afternoon tea in the Library is recommended (remember to book in advance).
Friendly and efficient staff everywhere.

Title: Great
ReviewText: Great location, excellent staff, good size rooms and comfy beds.

Title: Great hotel
ReviewText: The location was perfect for tourism. Staff made me feel very special as a platinum elite. Highly recommend.

Title: Spacious rooms and perfect location!
ReviewText:
Perfectly located beside the London Eye in the Aquarium building across from Big Ben on the Thames. Just a few minutes walk to Westminster and Waterloo underground/train station. A few minutes to great restaurants. 
Rooms are very spacious and recently updated with modern decor in a historic building that was the place of British parliament from 1922 to 1986. The only downside is the very long hallways when you’re at the end, it’s a huge hotel. Rooms are also very quiet considering the foot traffic just outside/below at the Eye and Aquarium. It’s also a bonus to be able to open the windows. 
Front desk staff was great at acknowledging platinum status and upgrading. Nice lounge even though it’s on the small side for a hotel with so many rooms. 
Overall, this is my go to hotel when staying in London and would recommend it for business and leisure (especially families)

Title: Lovely Hotel, South Bank Not My Cup of Tea
ReviewText: Love the hotel with its sophisticated interior design, and lovely classical architecture. Being Platinum Elite I was upgraded to a spacious suite, and can't say enough wonderful things about the room. The Executive Lounge is nice, though constantly crowded, so can be difficult to get a seat, or the servers attention. Complimentary drink choices were adequate, with plenty of other choices at additional charge. The room was very quiet, even though I looked out on the Thames and London Eye. My only issue with the hotel is its location. Having been to London many, many times, I was not thrilled about being in such a touristy locale. Because of this I would likely not stay here again, but that is just my personal preference. Overall the hotel was wonderful, and I would recommend it for someone who has not been to London before, or is looking to be in the middle of the tourist activities.

Title: 3 night stay review
ReviewText:
Overall stay in terms of the room was as expected by a hotel of this rating and market positioning, however the breakfast on offer was truly sub standard and one is better off with going outside for the first meal of the day (this applies to both the G/F restaurant for breakfast as well as the one available at the executive lounge)

Rooms were nice enough but please don’t expect to be amazed by what is on offer 

Location though was stunning and the views from the room are great 

Overall I would say I expected more for the price of the hotel

Title: Excellent séjour en famille
ReviewText:
Un hôtel très bien situé pour visiter Londres et faire du shopping .
 Du personnel de qualité et à l’écoute des clients ,l’hôtel très agréable pour ces journées en famille .
Nous devons revenir sur Londres on reviendra dans cet établissement

Title: Don’t stay anywhere but at the County Hall Marriott!
ReviewText: I think I have covered it all Iin my previous comments, thank you so much to the staff and management at the London county hall Marriott

Title: Worth the Visit!!
ReviewText: Can't find a more "London" location or traditional British hospitality than at the County Hall.

Title: Great Infrastructure! Poor Management
ReviewText: You don’t expect a group such as Marriott, to hold your money such as GBP 100, after clearing the bill upon checkout and after requesting certain items upon housekeeping, needing to remind them twice to get them

Title: D & D New Years Stay
ReviewText: Great stay as always at this beautiful hotel with wonderful staff

Title: Review of Marriott County Hall
ReviewText: Overall, they stay is ok but the discrepancy and no coordination among the front desk and reservation staff is very frustating and inconvenient.

Title: Not what you would exspect
ReviewText: The view of the hotel is beautiful, your room is delightful but the staff at the hotel is not the friendliest. I can’ say they will make you feel welcomed. The location to the city tour buses is right out the door but if you have to cross the bridge a lot you may not want to stay here. They now have gamblers on the bridge which causes a traffic jam and it is very hard to cross over.

Title: Iconic hotel
ReviewText: Just wasn’t great and it was expensive for what we have experienced but it didn’t ruin my time with my daughter for her birthday!

Title: Excellent hotel in a fantastic location
ReviewText: Great hotel with superb staff set in a great location to explore London and enjoy the South Bank.

Title: Location & Rooms are fabulous, staffing & food need some work!
ReviewText:
We've stayed at this hotel once before, found it by accident & find the location to be fabulous and the rooms to be fantastic with excellent views of the River Thames. We've stayed in one of the executive suites each time which was great to be able to spread our legs out a bit after a long day and wind down without being cramped and truly picturesque views of Big Ben and the River Thames. Also, some of the best pillows and bedding that me and my wife have slept on at a hotel - hands down!
Unfortunately though the staffing can truly use a lesson on etiquette & customer service, especially for a hotel of this caliber and cost. Food as well, eaten from both the executive lounge and room service - both were a let down. I have absolutely nothing against paying a premium, but the premium should also be accompanied with service & quality!

Title: Horrible beds friendly staff
ReviewText:
Hard terrible beds, not at all like Marriott beds and sheets we are used to. 
No ramp entering the hotel only stairs in the front so definitely not stroller or wheelchair friendly. 
Beautiful Christmas decor in the lobby. Great attention to guests for Christmas including a stocking that was hung on the door with chocolate and other goodies inside Christmas morning (not sure if all guests got it or just Marriott members). 
Terrible check out as there is no designated Elite member line and therefore you have to wait in the general long line to check in or out if you are an elite member. 
Great staff very friendly and accommodating.

Title: Beautiful property perfect location
ReviewText: My husband, son and I came to London right before Christmas. Everything was wonderful except the Executive lounge. If you do not intend to use the lounge you will have the perfect stay.

Title: Beautiful hotel lobby and bar
ReviewText: Prime location with close proximity to the London Eye, beautiful hotel lobby and restaurants for a holiday weekend. However, rooms are quite small and view from relatively small windows. It also requires a significant effort to put things in order, which is unfortunately not how I remember all my other Marriott Hotel experience. Hope they will do a better job next time.

Title: One of Lindons best
ReviewText: A 5 star hotel from check-in to check-out. Charming decor, tasty nibbles, and wonderful customer service.

Title: London Marriott County Hall stay
ReviewText: Elegant hotel, but not sure worth the price for what you get.

Title: London County Hall Marriott is awesome
ReviewText: Look no further. If visiting London, this is the place to stay.

Title: Disappointed
ReviewText: The outside view is the only good thing of this hotel.

Title: Wonderful building and good views
ReviewText: This is an interesting building with lots of history.  The rooms are quiet and peaceful apart from an unfortunate 8am wake up call from a loud water pump in the corridor

Title: Se tiene que mejorar la limpieza del suelo en las habitaciones
ReviewText: No recomendado para gente con problemas de alergia a los acaros

Title: 伦敦市政厅万豪
ReviewText: 超级方便的地理位置，超级体验，早起窗外看到泰晤士河和大本钟，纪录两个地铁站都很近，去很多景点和牛津街无论是地铁出行还是步行都很方便

Title: Excellent but for the quality/standard of catering
ReviewText: This great hotel is only let down by the food quality and catering in the executive lounge

Title: Perfect business trip
ReviewText: Great amenities and ideal location for central London with an iconic view on the Thames - what more could one ask for?

Title: Tourist
ReviewText: We  really enjoyed this hotel and received extremely good service from everyone.

Title: great location
ReviewText: Everything was great. Room, service, front desk. Highly recomended

Title: 20th Anniversary Joy!
ReviewText:
My wife and I were celebrating our 20th Anniversary.  We spent a few days in Paris and a weekend in London.  I called ahead and asked to be upgraded and feel special.  YOU NAILED IT. The room had desserts and wine with a nice hand written note congratulating us and we were upgraded to see the Eye right outside our window.  My wife loved it and you made our anniversary very special.

THANK YOU!!

Title: London Happenings
ReviewText:
Enjoyed all the elements of the hotel and it’s convenient location.  We would stay there again on our next visit. 
Thank you

Title: Exceptional Stay
ReviewText: Exceptional room, Staff and service! I will stay there again for sure. You can’t beat the location & I could see the London Eye right outside my room window. I couldn’t ask for more for a wonderful stay in London.

Title: Great hotel, great location
ReviewText:
Second stay at hotel.  Hotel has been renovated and in very good condition.

Hotel is close to underground and very close walking to attractions and the "circus"...

Title: My worst Marriott stay ever
ReviewText: Being a loyal Marriott patron for the last 7 years and a platinum elite member who stays in Marriotts worldwide I took my family to the Marriott County Hall for a break in London.  What a mistake!  Uninterested and inattentive staff, an unsympathetic and frankly indifferent management and very poor housekeeping coupled with extortionate bar and restaurant prices and the most uncomfortable beds.

Title: London weekend
ReviewText: Great hotel in a fantastic location with excellent staff.

Title: Pleasantly surprised
ReviewText: Great location, friendly staff, a positive experience.

Title: A wonderful experience
ReviewText: From the moment we arrived, we were made to feel welcome by Graham at the front entrance.  The hotel is a beautiful historic building that feels modernized. Our room was spacious and clean and we loved our view of the London Eye. The entire staff was amazing, Paolo the restaurant manager, Teodora at the front desk, Kelvin in the restaurant for breakfast and Stejn in the bar, to name a few of the people that we enjoyed meeting. The location was perfect for sightseeing and we could easily walk or take the Underground to anywhere we needed to go. We had an excellent trip and our decision to stay at the Marriott County Hall made it even better.

Title: Excellent
ReviewText: Beautiful hotel.  Excellent location. Professional and courteous staff.

Title: Great spot for exploring London!
ReviewText:
The Marriott County Hall was a great place to base our stay to see and tour London. The door staff were exceptional and we did enjoy the executive lounge before or after going out. Great access the the London Underground and close to the theatre district.  Excellent views of the Thames, London Eye and South Bank walking area (Embankment). 
Easy walking to many places via the Hungerford And Westminster Bridges. Great relaxing stay!

Title: Program Director
ReviewText: Awesome experience. Stay, service, room over looking the Thames and The Houses of Parliament.  County Hall is Incredible - would love to stay again.

Title: Great location to see some of the big sites in London
ReviewText:
This was our first time to visit London and the location was ideal!  We walked everywhere! Westminster (take the hour audio tour!!), Buckingham Palace, the theatre district (The Book of Mormon!), and walking along the Thames right out the front door.  We weren’t interested in riding the London Eye but if we were, it was right there.

The hotel has a fabulous bar!!  Do check it out - we did every evening for a night cap.  We were in 506 and it was a TREK  from the elevators. Ask for a room closer to the elevators unless you’re “counting steps”.

Lots of those double deck buses stop right in front of the hotel. They’re great for getting an overview of the city!

Can’t wait to go back!

Title: 位置很好，体验一般
ReviewText: 还需要把房间改进，位置好，就是房间景色没有，早餐还可以丰富一点。礼宾很好。很热情

Title: Some great, some not so
ReviewText: I needed something from an outside store and the hotel went and got it for me. Much appreciative since I was on crutches.  The Concierge Lounge service is awful. When you get there, go up to them and let them know what you need. Then keep going up as you can not expect service in this Lounge.

Title: Beautiful hotel
ReviewText: Definitely recommend this hotel. Staff are very friendly and the facilities were excellent

Title: bad experience
ReviewText: Terrible living experience. Will no longer stay here again.

Title: It was just “OK”
ReviewText: I believe that the staff and hotel were ok. I encourage the hotel to be nicer to guest. Ackinowledge executivebership (never once). Do their best be kinder to everyone

Title: Good location but poor staff and facilities
ReviewText: Excellent location near to popular tourist attractions. Hotel needs a refurbishment across its facilities (gym, sauna, lifts), reception  staff were rude and food options were limited and leave a lot to be desired for 5 star hotel.

Title: Great location and hotel
ReviewText: Very convenient location and the hotel is lovely. The staff were wonderful. Room is a very good size for London. Great view of the Thames and Parliment.

Title: The best hotel around
ReviewText: This is an amazing hotel. from the moment you walk in until the moment you leave, you know you are experiencing quality.

Title: London Marriott
ReviewText: All round excellence and a great location close to all the major attractions in London.

Title: Location Cannot Be Beat
ReviewText: Location, location, location. This hotel has it. Lovely hotel. Quiet rooms. Welcoming Doormen. Service can be hit or miss, but you will be out and about. Again, location cannot be beat. Nice, clean, newly renovated rooms.

Title: 素晴らしいホテルです。
ReviewText: ロケーション的にも便利で、落ち着いた雰囲気のイギリスらしい素晴らしいホテルです。　スタッフの方々もフレンドリーで快適に過ごせました。

Title: convivvencia familiar con esposa,hija, esposo y nietas
ReviewText: excelente y buen  dominio del castellano de los empleados

Title: Great Location to get "in the swing" of London
ReviewText: Accross the river from and with great views of Parliamet and Big Ben.  County Hall historic building which Marriott has refurbished with modern rooms and excellent service with an exceptional staff!

Title: What an awesome time
ReviewText: Awesome hotel. Centrally located with incredible Thames views and many, many attractions.

Title: Hamilton
ReviewText: Great location, awesome service, world class view, felt valued throughout.

Title: A hotel gem in heart of London
ReviewText: First class staff, location and spacious rooms, the London court house is the best place to stay. Located on the Thames with uncluttered views of Parliament and Big Ben you will be staying within a stone's through of the best of London sites

Title: Popular which stretches the service response
ReviewText:
I arrived late, was advised that the "hotel was fully booked" (this was not bourne out by the number of people seen).
I am 73  and was given a room about as far as it could be from the lift.
Food was good but room service food left something to be desired.
Argument with Executive Club reception which could have been handled better by her.
Noise from external source outside the room.

Title: Very enjoyable
ReviewText: Superb staff. The executive lounge is very efficient and comfortable.

Title: Aug trip
ReviewText: Great customer service. Really impressed. Will recommend this hotel to others. Enjoyable stay.

Title: Pleased Beyond My Expectations!
ReviewText: We booked the Marriott County Hall for our first-ever London trip, based largely on location (near everything, including the Waterloo station), and were rewarded with so much more!  The rooms were very large and very nicely furnished with everything we needed.  The staff provided friendly and attentive service suited to a 5-star hotel, and even sent up champagne for our anniversary.  We also loved the beautiful historic County Hall building which had previously been a government building - it felt like an authentic London experience to us.  This was probably one of the best Marriotts I’ve stayed in yet.  I highly recommend!

Title: Fabulous Hotel in London
ReviewText: Me and my husband decided to go to London this year for vacation. I'm a flight attendant with a Major Airline and don't get to enjoy your city. The hotel staff made us feel like royalty and there area where the hotel was, was fantastic. Everything was within our reach. I recommend anyone who stays at this hotel will not regret it.

Title: Simply the best
ReviewText:
To put mildly this is the best Hotel and best Marriott I have ever stayed at! From the moment you enter and are greeted by the doorman to the service from the front desk staff you are made to feel like a star no matter your status level. I am a platinum member and the service I recieved was beyond outstanding. The champagne reception, the welcome gift and the personal service from the front desk manager, Conor, was amazing!
Next to the outstanding service, the hotel is beautiful and the location is amazing. Simply put it you are coming to London then stay here!

Title: Excellent stay!
ReviewText: My daughters and I had a great trip to London. The executive lounge was fantastic!

Title: Great Hotel, fantastic location
ReviewText:
Great check-in experience, arrived early and was given room straight away and staff anticipated needs regarding two separate bookings to ensure that we did not have to change rooms.
Excellent room with great location in the centre of all the activities planned.
Could not fault.

Title: Top Rate London Hotel and Location
ReviewText: This hotel is first rate. Great location, right on the River Themes. Attentive staff. Excellent amenities, as you would expect in a high end hotel - decor, fitness center, staff, food and drink.

Title: Great!
ReviewText: Great hotel!  Would stay there again whenever in London.

Title: London Marriott County Hall Hotel
ReviewText:
The staff and facilities are outstanding.  The location is near many of the main attractions and easily accessible to others via the Underground or train.  Rooms are nice size.

The service of the staff in the Executive Lounge, Front Desk, Bell Men and Concierge are truly exceptional.

Highly recommend this hotel to anyone traveling to central London.

Title: Never Again.
ReviewText: From the time we checked in to the time we left we were disappointed with our stay. Screaming kids running around banging on the thin walls and doors past midnight- no response from staff even after informing them. A loud noise from air conditioner or washer(?) all night long and I’m a light sleeper so did not sleep at all. They offered to put us in another room at 7:30 pm the night before we were leaving but it didn’t make sense to pack all our belongings to move when we were leaving so early. We had arranged a ride to the airport for the morning...did not sleep so decided to leave earlier- asked the concierge if it’s ok to take taxi instead of waiting for the pre-arranged transport and he said yes it’s fine. When my husband called about reimbursement for the transport they said sorry- there is a 24 hour cancellation policy so no refund. That cost us 70 pounds.  No one ever explained that to us when we made the arrangements. There were so many other little things that were not right that when you put the whole experience together you walk away feeling like you spent a whole lot of money to stay at a very poor quality hotel. It looks very impressive from the outside (the river view) but if it’s quality you want I suggest you look elsewhere. If you have a bunch of rowdy energetic kids that like to run around the hotel making a lot of noise - this is the place for you! Nobody will stop them! Enjoy!

Title: Exceptional service and style
ReviewText: I have stayed at many Marriott hotels in the US over the past decade and find most provide a level of comfort, convenience and service above the norm of other major hotel chain operators.  The London Hotel County Hall was among the very best in which I have stayed.  The location is great, the room and public areas were first rate, and the service was exceptionally attentive and courteous.  I appreciated the VIP treatment we received throughout our stay.  Highly recommended!

Title: London 2018
ReviewText: Great location and very close to the South Bank with all the music, restaurants, bars, activities....loved it.

Title: Great Hotel with Great Staff in a Great location
ReviewText: This is a fabulous hotel . Very unique property . In a great location . Rooms are  big and well equipped . What really stands out is a great staff . They are friendly and helpful  and make the experience one of a kind

Title: Excellent Hotel
ReviewText: Stayed there for 3 nights. It was amazing experience.

Title: Slightly Disappointing
ReviewText:
We had looked forward to staying at the County Hall Marriott, as we had stayed there about 12 years ago. I was attending a fifty year reunion at St Thomas's Hospital across the street.
We had a very mixed experience. The reception process was satisfactory, and we were allowed early check in . 
The room we were allotted was at the end of a corridor, approx 300 steps from the elevators. This is a long way for anyone, especially an ageing guest.
The room while pleasant enough had no refrigerator, there were no stirrers or spoon for the beverages, the television kept losing reception/signal and I have no idea why. There was no weather disturbance.
I was surprised in a hotel of this type/cost, that there was no "turn down "service.
There was a bottle of wine exposed and it was not clear if this was gratis or a chargeable item. I assumed the latter. 
Staff were obliging in allowing baggage storage post discharge.

Title: Expérience inoubliable
ReviewText: L'hôtel est juste fantastique, idéalement bien placé et le personnel est charmant.

Title: 前台蛮横！
ReviewText: 如题，不给任何关于会员权益的商量余地，就是那个大胡子前台！什么都说要额外付费！免费升房间没有，你要是愿意加钱就可以！这就是金卡会员待遇？！

Title: A/C
ReviewText: When it's unseasonably warm, I would expect a brand like Marriott to keep their guests comfortable.

Title: Great hotel, great hotel, great staff
ReviewText: Room not available on time - disappointing as it impacted on plans.  To be fair, reception staff were excellent, they did their best to resolve and mitigate against the inconvenience.  Got knocked up at 9am by housekeeping wanting to know if we were leaving and when - they were back again at 11am and walked straight in - hardly made us feel like welcome guests.  Still, love the place and intend returning

Title: Great location
ReviewText: The staff was friendly room was very nice the club lounge food and service was great and our location was great

Title: Best service in London
ReviewText: From the moment we arrived we were treated with fabulous service from every member of the hotel staff. The upgraded balcony suite overlooking the Thames was spectacular. This hotel has the best view in all of London!<

Title: London's Best location
ReviewText: This place is perfect. Excellent location, nice fresh modern rooms, and excellent service.

Title: Great Location but not worth the price
ReviewText: Great hotel location.  Clean rooms, and efficient amenities.  The food is marginal, and other than the doormen and concierge customer service (welcoming warmness feeling) is marginal at best.  The food is way over priced in relation to quality.  Room service is slow and the staff are robotic and slow to attend to a request. The housekeeping basically makes the bed wipes down the bathroom.  They forget to re stock water (several requests) and forgot to restock toiletries (shampoo etc).  The executive lounge is lacking compared to others.  You get this vibe that they are trying to control the amount of food that might be consumed.  The staff is very impersonal and control of who accesses the lounge is not well managed (one morning I heard people telling others there was free food on level 5).  Just so happened the lounge was packed to the walls.  The coffee machine was broken the whole 9 days of my stay.  The lounge ran out of a variety of food offerings every morning.  No water bottles offered.  Just a very poor  executive lounge.  Overall the location of the hotel is fabulous and the doormen and concierge are wonderful.  For the price if location is most important  this hotel is for you, if you are expecting equal service that as a Platinum Elite traveler expects, level  try another hotel.

Title: Excellent hotel with true ambiance
ReviewText: We added three days in London coming off a Cunard crossing from NYC.  My wife chose this Marriott after considering others in London, basing it on location (others have this also), rooms, building appearance and historic feel (again others have this). We found the room, staff and facilities to be well above expectations and a few even above that such a rating is possible.  I would recommend this Marriott property to anyone going to London whether for business or leisure.

Title: 位置绝佳
ReviewText: 酒店位置极好，所有景点都在步行范围内，坐船也非常方便。酒店餐饮也物美价廉，非常推荐第一次到伦敦旅行的人的住宿选择

Title: Lousy view from the room
ReviewText: Despite having one of the best locations in the world, the rooms given to us had the worst view. The so called “Inner Courtyard View” is nothing but a fraud with windows facing the backside of other rooms. Very poor to say the least

Title: 设计、装潢独特完美！床品极棒！
ReviewText: 浅灰色调的地图室内及各种卡类装饰统一、别具一格，特别喜欢。走廊里的小用品装饰图案别具一格，喜欢。只是信号太差！太差！在这个对网相当依赖的时代，是硬伤。

Title: 打开英国的最好体验住宿
ReviewText: 住在传统而典型的英国建筑里，推窗即揽泰晤士河美景，天气晴朗的落日时分，从酒店房间即可饱览映照在晚霞里的国会大厦和Westminster bridge美景，这是英国最好的打开方式。如果你经常在亚洲住万豪，你会发现早餐不够丰富，但味道还不错，早餐风景一流。

Title: 2nd Experience
ReviewText:
Breakfast very poor didn't find any fresh fruit and vegetable (very English breakfast sausages, beans etc) 
Air-condition in Bar was not working we almost were grilled at night when we wanted to have a drinks 
We spotted 2 mouse 1st on 5th floor and one in the Lobby  
One lift was not working for an entire week, waiting for a lift took a while

Title: Excellent hotel
ReviewText: Location location location. Perfect location near London’s attractions and pubs — you have to love this gem

Title: Guest at Marriott
ReviewText: My overall experience was excellent. The staff, services and location is great and central to everywhere.

Title: Historic hotel
ReviewText: Beautiful and convenient hotel with an excellent staff.

Title: London County Hall Marriot Hotel
ReviewText:
If you need to be within easy reach of the Houses of Parliament, Westminster Abbey, Whitehall, or Trafalgar Square, then
this hotel is where to stay.
A little stroll further along the Strand and you are not far from Covent Garden and the bars and restaurants there.

Title: Right location - Great Hosts!
ReviewText: Hard pressed to find a better location to see the sights of London from!  Stately hotel with easy access to the water and subway.

Title: London Family Vacation
ReviewText: Had great stay but experienced problems at check in that were not resolved, ie connecting rooms and Executive Lounge access.

Title: Great location with disappointing service / food
ReviewText: This hotel was overall very disappointing- the service was consistently poor.  They checked us into the wrong room and never corrected the problem.  The nice meal we had booked at Gillrays was terrible - the server only came to our table once in two hours.  We couldn’t order a drink or get a glass of water with our meal -  the busboy ended up provided our drinks and check.   At the reservation, I indicated we were staying in the hotel and celebrating two 70th birthdays.  Not one mention of those birthdays by the waiter or staff the whole visit.

Title: Lovely space
ReviewText: Loved the building, the bar and the room.  Rey clean, very attentive staff and great views!

Title: 很棒
ReviewText: 很好的地理位置，豪华的酒店建筑，很好的服务，内部装修很经典

Title: Great Hotel for Lodging and Location
ReviewText: Great rooms and perfect location for both business and leisure travelers. Rooms are both large and extremely comfortable. Staff is friendly and helpful. We had a wonderful stay in London and it was primarily because of our stay at Marriott.

Title: Fantastic Stay!!
ReviewText: The team at this location was amazing! We ended our honeymoon with a two night stay in London and chose this hotel because I always have great service at Marriott...but this location went above and beyond any expectations I had.

Title: The London experience!
ReviewText:
Location of this hotel is perfect for the vacationers! Center of what’s happening, if you are lucky enough to get a balcony room over looking the River Thames — wow you’ll love what you see!
Staff is amazing and they will go the extra mile to make your stay amazing!!

Title: Accommodating despite the volume
ReviewText: Nice modern rooms, great location for walking to key site.  In terms of amenities and services, you had to know what to ask for in terms of complimentary service otherwise details were not shared.

Title: Excellent location and Friendly staff
ReviewText: My wife and I had a great experience at the Marriott County hall hotel. Our stay, the rooms, the excellent friendly staff made it an amazing time for us. They really go above and beyond and feel very well pampered. HIGHLY RECOMMENDED

Title: Incredible Views - Wonderful Hotel - Excellent Rooms
ReviewText:
Our stay was shortened due to airline problems but we wished we had more time to enjoy the Marriott County Hall.
This is an incredible hotel and everything we experienced was wonderful.

Title: Room service disappointing
ReviewText: Hotel was great with the exception of finding a pack of dirty diapers behind sofa (room 521 I believe)... Do not order room service, especially in the evening. Waited over an hour for it to be delivered, Mac and cheese was cold, sticky toffee pudding was cold. Chocolate mousse was warm

Title: Stay here!
ReviewText:
My wife and two kids (13 and 11) just returned from a 5 night stay at this hotel. I am a Marriott rewards member and was upgraded to a junior suite with a river view at check in. We were on our way back from a medical mission trip in Africa when we arrived at the hotel at 9 AM. We were exhausted from a 16 hour plane excursion and knew our room was guaranteed by 3 PM but asked to be put in ASAP. I wasn’t holding my breath but to our very pleasant surprise, we were put in 30 minutes later. We passed out for a couple hours in the room before touring the city. 

This is a special hotel. There is clearly history in its walls and the classic beauty of the place is a marvel. The location of the hotel also can’t be beat!  It is minutes walk to Westminster Abby and parliament. Some reviewers have commented on the busyness of tourists in the surrounding area. I think it’s ludicrous people would complain about this. The hotel is in very close proximity to everything worth seeing in London which is why there’s a lot of tourist traffic around the hotel. The convenience of the location is a HUGE plus and not a negative. We walked to Trafalgar Square, Piccadilly Circus, Shakespeare theater, Buckingham Palace, and the theater district. The London Eye is on the front porch of the hotel!  Crazy!  
Our room was more than ample for our family of four. Toward the end of the trip, my kids just wanted to stay in our room because it was so nice. We thoroughly enjoyed high tea one afternoon. The service was great all around. I’d highly recommend a stay at this hotel if you’re in the London area and want elegant luxury accommodations in the heart of everything.

Title: Hidden Gem
ReviewText: Very nice hotel...you don’t even know it’s there until you walk by...then you see the road leading back to the beautiful entrance And front court yard

Title: Great stay
ReviewText: Fabulous hotel in a great location for all things London.

Title: Weekend break
ReviewText: Great location :) very friendly and accommodating staff and lovely rooms. Sadly bed not very comfortable and no bath in bathroom, only shower. Also seemed to have paid over the odds for advance purchase room rate, having checked room rates once we had checked in to find they were quite a bit cheaper than we paid! Sunday morning breakfast was a real let down for a ‘five’ star hotel :((

Title: Great hotel with great amenities with great location
ReviewText: Will definitely be back at London Marriott County Hall because of great experience

Title: Great location and staff
ReviewText: Took the entire family to London. I normally travel for work, but this was a holiday trip from the US.  I'd highly recommend the breakfast at the hotel, especially the banana/coconut oatmeal with citrus sauce.  The full breakfast is also wonderful.  The gym is outstanding. It's a full size gym, not your typical hotel gymnasium. It has more than enough equipment for a full workout. The location is close to two tube stations that are well connected. The front doormen were incredibly helpful with advice and were able to get cabs quite easily.  The rooms are nice, especially if you get a room with a river view. Could not find a better locale than this for hitting top tourist sites on foot (ie: Parliament, London Eye, etc). The entrance to the hotel, over the bridge, can get congested.

Title: Great location
ReviewText: Great location and rooms with stunning views. Lobby and other staff provided excellent hospitality. Restaurant could've also matched hotel standards had service times not been horrendous.

Title: ROMANTIC AND AMAZING STAY
ReviewText:
Not only was the hotel personnel friendly and courteous, but the room, services, bar, and cleanliness was amazing.  Looking forward to next year stay!!!!
Thank you

Title: hotel magique
ReviewText:
personnel au top
chambre impecable
repas exeptionnel
hotel situe a proximite de tous les lieux a visiter et du metro

we en amoureux parfaitement reussi...

Title: London
ReviewText: Lovely hotel, very convenient, lots going on around the area

Title: Excellent hotel
ReviewText: We only stayed one night, but it was an excellent experience.  GREAT Location

Title: What a treat
ReviewText:
I would love to go back to this hotel.  The service was fabulous from the doormen, front desk and amazing lounge associates.

The location was perfect for our trip.  We enjoyed being able to walk everywhere.  So many restaurants along the south bank to explore or hang in at the hotel.

 Wish we would have packed a swim suit to enjoy the indoor pool and spa.

Title: Top Hotel in Top Lage
ReviewText: Sehr schöne, große Zimmer in einem stattlichen Gebäude direkt an der Themse und direkt an der Station mehrerer Buslinien, nur ein  paar Schritte zu den wichtigsten Underground-Stationen. Frühstück und Bar qualitativ sehr gut. Personal sehr freundlich und kommunikativ

Title: Inaccurate description of the room
ReviewText: When I was booked the Big Ben executive room I was under the impression that the view was going to be amazing, but when I arrived  I was very disappointed to see Big Ben was undergoing rennovation/ construction. I did not receive any information/email about this. I advise Marriott to temporarily stop letting consumers be fooled by the Big Ben executive view room because they will be very disappointed.

Title: County Hall location London
ReviewText: From check in to check out - Excellent service all around

Title: Excellent Stay Experience
ReviewText: It was a great experience for me to stay in London Marriott County Hall. The location is strategic, walking distance to big ben, eye of London, Westminster church of London & etc. We had a wonderful night in Marriott before we left London.

Title: Great (with some exceptions)
ReviewText:
This hotel is a historical gem having been a central part of English politics, opened by the Queen Mother and had many a famous guest (look it up). The rooms are styled very nicely, I suspect a recent refurbishment. My room gave me an excellent view of the Thames, London Eye and Houses of Parliament.  
The staff are all accommodating.  I found the dining staff to be the most accommodating and kind. 
Breakfast is very good with many options and again served in a room on the ground floor that offers fantastic London views. 
This hotel is an interesting one for tourists and those conducting business - If you are sightseeing all day and walking, you must then walk a bit of a way to get back to your hotel and again once inside your hotel, dependent on where your room is located. Think of Las Vegas hotels. 
I found the in room dining options to be limited and when I received my food, my sticky toffee pudding was harder than bullets. Not what I expect from a hotel of this rating. 
Overall, I may stay here again but I would need to be in a room much closer to the elevator (as there seems to be only one lift) and with some comfortable shoes for all of the walking required.

Title: Excellent hotel with great location
ReviewText: Great stay!  Staff was so accommodating and location was great.

Title: Highly, highly recommend!
ReviewText: This hotel for me, is just perfect. In an excellent and central location, in a beautiful, grand historic building overlooking Westminster Bridge and Jubilee Gardens, and yet with elegant and modern rooms, I could not have been happier. I just loved the juxtaposition of the old and new, the food was great, and the staff absolutely went out of their way to provide great service. I particularly commend Alessandro at the front desk. I would stay there again in a heartbeat!

Title: Exceptional hotel
ReviewText: Conveniently located to most tourist attractions in downtown London  Beautiful hotel common areas and sizeable rooms.

Title: Fantastic Hotel
ReviewText: Love this hotel when I come to London. Rooms are fantastic, dining is great and they have a great gym. This hotel truly deserves its five star rating, unlike many other London hotels that I have frequented.

Title: Magnificent Marriott County Hall
ReviewText: I recently stayed at the newly refurbished Marriott County Hall. My room overlooked the river Thames and Houses of Parliament. From arrival to departure everything was absolutely perfect. Great deals on price if your dates are flexible.

Title: Excellent Stay----- A memorable experience
ReviewText: A memorable experience which I want to gift to my daughter in the city of London

Title: Disappointing
ReviewText:
Stayed here for 5 nights and left the hotel with unpleasant feelings because of so many things that happened during our stay:
-	On the first night, there was no WiFI. Apparently there was a general problem at the hotel. No one informed about it upon check in but only when we were in the room and called front desk to complain.  There was also a problem with the AC. After several talks with several managers we got compensated for the first night
 (discount on the first night and a bottle of wine with an apology letter).
This was the first night but unfortunately things didn’t get any better …

-	We asked for a wake up call for 8am. This call was not done and as a result we woke up late and missed our planned and prepaid tour of the parliament.  This was quite frustrating, not to mention the fact that it had cost us £36 that were lost.

-	I was given the option of late checkout till 7 pm.  My wife went into the executive lounge at 6:20pm.  A few minutes later she was approached by a staff member and was asked to leave and settle my bill immediately or she would have to pay extra.
she told him that we had already paid my bill and that she was given a late checkout till 7pm but he still told her that she had to leave.
she insisted that he recheck and eventually he came back and told her that “he was sorry and his colleague had made a mistake”. Really???
She felt so embarrassed as the whole conversation took place in the middle of a very busy and crowded lounge.  Shefelt like an intruder being caught sneaking into the lounge.
Definitely unprofessional and very disrespectful.

-	Once back in the room, the cleaning lady came to check the room at 6:50pm.
Why was ii necessary to be reminded that we had the room until 7pm? This, again, is unprofessional and mainly annoying.

All thru our stay, we felt that front desk staff tend to reply with a “NO” answer much too often!
We spoke to several on duty managers and  got the feeling that they didn’t actually care.

In summary, I was left with a very unpleasant feeling. This is not the level of service one would expect from a 5 star Marriott hotel.

Title: Nice hotel, excellent staff
ReviewText: The staff of Marriott County Hall London went out of their way to make us feel special. Highly recommended

Title: Fantastic Weekend in London
ReviewText: Had an amazing trip with the help of Marriott. This hotel is in the best location located right next to the London Eye and a 4 minute walk to the underground. The hotel and staff were immaculate. The balcony rooms facing the river were fantastic and the 5th floor lounge was a comfort of home after a long day of walking. I highly suggest this hotel to anyone traveling to London.

Title: Fantastic place to stay
ReviewText: This hotel is right in the center of all the action, the rooms are large the people are so helpful. It might take a GPS to drive to the location, but is well worth it. Andre the doorman is very helpful with just about everything. Great place to stay will return and recommend to all my friends.

Title: Amazing stay at London Marriott Hotel County Hall
ReviewText: Amazing stay in the best location. Friendly staff!

Title: County Hall
ReviewText:
We recently stayed at the County Hall - westminster to celebrate our 30th Wedding anniversary. I had booked a parliament view room, this was not provided but “upgraded” to a garden view suite. The room was in the roof meaning the view was Non existent  for my wife whom is 5’ tall. 

As the M club lounge only provided continental breakfast and my wife wanted cooked food, we used the regular restaurant and paid the difference. The food from Gillrays restaurant was inedible, cardboard would have had more taste. 

I am so clad we used an off site restaurant for our celebration meal.

Title: Great Weekend Stay
ReviewText: We were really well looked after by the very friendly staff at County Hall the rooms especially were up to date, clean and easy to use. A great use of a building that served its Londoner’s so well and nice to know it’s still being used to the same ends today!

Title: Week end to remember
ReviewText: A warm and comfortable central location to visit London. Transport links are abundant but if the weather is fine a joy to just walk and wander around London. We walk over 12 miles on Saturday and experienced the variety of architecture and open spaces in a vibrant city. The Marriott Hotel County Hall offer us everything we could wish for comfort, excellent food and location.

Title: Best location in grand historic building.
ReviewText: This Marriott is housed within an historical building. Located right next to the London Eye, Westminster Abbey, Churchill Museum etc. Location especially for a newcomer to London cannot be beat!!

Title: Amazing visit to London !
ReviewText: We stayed at the Marriott London County Hall. The customer service from the doorman to the maid service was exceptional. To be greeted and made welcome no matter what time of day was great. No request meet with any other response but yes Madam no problem. The young lady named Dewi in the  executive lounge was very kind and informative and an actual joy to see each day. Thanks Marriott for enhancing our visit to London job well done.

Title: Family stay April 2018
ReviewText: Great and super convenient location. Very comfortable hotel/room. Friendly and helpful staff.

Title: Over night stay with family
ReviewText: As always excellent service from Marriott staff, room was quite small as to be expected in London, and booking was made 2hrs before arriving on a busy weekend, but still would rank one of best I’ve stayed at in London, and location was good

Title: Anniversary stay
ReviewText: We had a wonderful experience at Counth Hall! From check in to check out, every staff member was wonderful. Our room overlooked parliament and the Thames, it as beautiful. It was our anniversary as well and the staff made sure to make us feel extra special. We will be staying here for sure during our next trip to London! Thank you Marriott for such a great property!

Title: Great location/ Great People
ReviewText: We had a pleasant stay at London Marriott County Hall. Everything is walking distance. The hop on hop off bus stops right in front of the hotel. We love our room with the view of the river and the London eye. Most of all we love how nice and accommodating the hotel staff were.
