My daughters & I went to London and Paris for a girls trip. Jemma greeted us and checked us in. She upgraded our room since we are elite members and let us check in early. We chatted and laughed like we were old friends. She was absolutely delightful. She also lamented with us as we told her our sad plight of getting all our luggage stolen from our car on Marlybone St on our first day of the trip. She personally escorted us to our incredibly decorated room and had our complementary treats sent up. Nic our concerige was absolutely charming and incredibly helpful! We wanted to see Les Mis and he not only found us good seats but PRODUCERS seats in the 7th row the night of the play!! He helped us with clear directions many times. We were so grateful for his help!

Our day is departure we needed a taxi to get to the airport and the staff had that arranged too!

It was a little noisy since they are doing construction but the incredible customer service l, comfort and amenities made up for it!

Location could not be beat either. In close proximity to Westminster tube station.

At this Marriott even though we were 5000 miles away from home we felt like were already home.

Title: Room for improvement
ReviewText: Please see my TripAdvisor feedback!

Title: Beautiful Renovation
ReviewText: Great hotel with a location that can't be beat and incredible staff!

Title: One of the best!
ReviewText: Really nice stay celebrating my wife's birthday - the staff were very proactive and helpful, the rooms are recently refurbished and look fantastic, we had a great view of Big Ben, the Thames and Houses of Parliament, and the fitness centre is also very nice. The outside of the hotel is also an amazing building, and only the common areas are not as modern but we did not spend a lot of time on them anyway (some of them undergoing refurbishment). I definitely recommend!

Title: Beautiful hotel
ReviewText: Lovely appointed rooms. Great staff. Good restaurant and bar. Amazing views of landmarks. Highly recommend a stay here.

Title: The heart of London.
ReviewText: Great location and amenities. Great services,

Title: Wonderful hotel!!
ReviewText: Loved this hotel! The staff was incredibly helpful and friendly. The rooms were spacious and comfortable. The location was perfect!!

Title: Perfect weekend!
ReviewText:
Breakfast was not 100٪‏ satisfactory
Parking fees too high
Way to various hotel locations not always correctly indicated e.g inside the elevator
An option to sit on terrass Or Even Outsider the Hotel is missing
However! It was a perfect stay for us!

Title: Big Ben and the London Eye from my window 2016
ReviewText: Great location for a film viewing, spot of sightseeing and loads of places to eat.

Title: Signature experience at Marriott London County Hall
ReviewText: Our experience at Marriott London County Hall is one of the most memorable experience in recent times. We couldn't have asked for anything better and Marriott just delivered right on spot. I would love to go back to this property as situation arises. Staying at Central London and not staying at Marriott London County Hall at a decent nightly rate based on the location would be a big miss for any guest who admires the signature experience and great customer service. Our best wishes to the Marriott London County Hall Team and staff for their great service.

Title: Awesome stay
ReviewText: Highly recommend. Excellent staff and location.

Title: Trip of a Lifetime!
ReviewText: Loved, loved, loved the location of the Marriott Hotel County Hall. The hotel employees were very friendly, helpful, and kind to my family. They each made us feel right at home. Our hotel room was spacious, lovely, clean, and most importantly comfortable. The new decor of the hotel is just lovely. We will definitely be back to stay and will recommend this hotel highly to families and business travelers.

Title: MEMORABLE STAY
ReviewText:
LOVE,LOVE, LOVE... Where to begin? Our first encounter was with Andres at the front desk we checked in at midnight and he was extremely helpful, the bellmen were all so welcoming each and every time we walked through the front door always with a smile and a high five for my little one "aka Batman". Nic the concierge was the highlight of our stay, not only was he knowledgeable but so very friendly, patient and a delight to speak with, he was AMAZING!!! Last but not least, the breakfast staff and executive lounge staff always so perky, they made my mornings shine with delight and my evenings calming as I whined down with my chocolate chip cookie ritual. Thank you so much for making our vacation a memorable one. We'll be back soon.

PS. The location was fantastic, the beds were extremely comfortable and the rooms were spacious but the staff was what made our stay unforgettable.

Title: Fantastic Hotel
ReviewText: A lovely hotel, newly refurbished with lovely views of the London Eye and River Thames.

Title: Loved every minute
ReviewText: From the time we arrived to the time we left, the staff was friendly and helpful. They never failed to smile. The view on the top floor is the best view of the eye and Big Ben. I loved the layout of the room and all services provided. I really thank you for making me feel welcome and providing me with the best possible stay. Great job.

Title: Excellent Location
ReviewText:
The hotel location is great, just next to the London Eye and across the bridge from Big Ben. The rooms are great, very spacious and well detailed. I especially liked the pre installed international wall outlets.
I enjoyed the English breakfast offerings and also utilized the lounge. The staff was all quite attentive friendly. My only recommendations for advice or improvement are extremely minor. The bathroom shower is somewhat susceptible to water escaping around the tub onto the floor as the glass wall only covers half the shower. I wish the lounge had offered a local British beer.
Again these were so minor and did not detract from my experience which was a 5 across the board.

Title: County hall
ReviewText: Las habitaciones remodeldas amplias y agradables. No hay contactos en los baños. No hay acceso al Club Lounge para la familia de los socios gold. La zona muy centrica pero al mismo tiempo un poco insegura. Se ven muchos vagos por ahi.

Title: Gorgeous Property and Wonderful Staff
ReviewText: Amazing and convenient location. My room was absolutely gorgeous. The breakfast was excellent each day. The staff is impeccable! I could not have been more pleased with my stay!

Title: Great Hotel!
ReviewText: Great hotel and fantastic location. Wonderful staff. Clean, well appointed room and excellent in-room amenities. Only negative was to have to pay for WiFi.

Title: Building site
ReviewText: If you like building sites you'll love it. And if you don't like swimming you'll like it even more.

Title: Outstandinf Service
ReviewText: The hotel is centrally located for easy access to main parts of London but the staff at the Marriott County Hall is the differentiator. From check in through the duration of our stay, great staff support, anxious to please and a great asset to a wonderful hotel

Title: London genießen
ReviewText: Fantastische Lage,ausgezeichnetes Personal ,stilvolle Zimmer!

Title: My recent stay at London Marriott
ReviewText: Beautiful hotel. Some minor annoyances.

Title: Such a London Treat
ReviewText:
Being an Elite member, this is one of the best Marriott's we have vacationed with. Right at the base of the London Eye & steps away from Big Ben. The staff is attentive with the elegance of high quality rooms.

My only dislikes - no dressers in the rooms which made for a creative way to storage clothes. Also, no electrical plug in the bathroom so you will have to blow dry / straighten hair using a plug inside the closet. It was a bit awkward.

Title: Another delightful stay
ReviewText: My 2 young children and I have stayed once before at the County Hall and didn't think it could be topped, but the attention to detail and brand new designed rooms meant it did just that. Right in the heart of everything, but the hotel remains very personal. Marriott should be proud of the staff that were in hand during the weekend. Can't wait to come back

Title: Opportunity for Improvement
ReviewText: The hotel is remodeling its premises but attention is needed on basic utilities and services such as phone lines, internet, an TV. Elite customers should be recognized. Hotel's location is marvelous.

Title: Best London Marriott
ReviewText: Perfectly situated in the centre of London with great views of the river and parliment

Title: Outstanding stay at the Marriott!
ReviewText: We stayed at this hotel last week and were impressed with every aspect of the services and accommodation that was provided to us. We shall be visiting again shortly and trust our initial impressions will be upheld! You could not be in a better location for central London yet the hotel is quiet ( being set back from the road) and calming. Well done Marriott and all the staff.

Title: What a lovely experiance
ReviewText: I stayed with my wife at the hotel to celebrate our wedding anniversary, and the over all stay was in one word excellent

Title: Holiday
ReviewText: The hotel is being remodeled and we were fortunate to be the first to stay in the room. The detail and design were outstanding and the bathroom was amazing. The wallpaper throughout the room is a map of London and tied in perfectly to the rest of the design. It is centrally located to many tourist attractions and two major tube stations.

Title: A Boutique Hotel in a Large Chain's Logo
ReviewText:
We spent 4 wonderful nights starting on Christmas Eve. We were fortunate to have stayed in the renovated rooms on the fourth floor. We love the wallpaper of the London map and another of umbrellas and bowler hats. The changes were so knew that there was still plastic on the hallway carpet. It was like staying in a boutique hotel.

However, the best part about the hotel was its people - very accommodating, friendly. From the front desk to the concierge lounge to the bar and restaurant, everyone made you feel at home. t We appreciated the Christmas stocking in our room filled with chocolate gold coins, nuts, and oranges. It's the best Marriott I've stayed and one of the best overall.

Title: Tired Old Hotel
ReviewText: The Marriott County Hall is undergoing a long overdue upgrade. However staff appear reluctant to allow guests to enjoy the new facilities. The old rooms are in a very poor state with stained fabrics, broken fittings and very old and uncomfortable mattresses. Another feature of the hotel is the malfunctioning fire alarm. We were evacuated twice in 48 hours (into the December rain) and had the prepare to evacuate alarm go off twice more.

Title: Memory Keepsake for baby/ toddler
ReviewText: A little keepsake would have added / brought happiness to the toddler/ collection to the family for every stay @ Marriott (we usually received a rubber duck / stuffed toy inroom when staying with baby/ toddler @ Marriott), but not this hotel.

Title: Marriott county hall
ReviewText: Nice hotel in busy area. Beautiful architecture

Title: View of London
ReviewText: Picked this hotel due to the location and view to celebrate a 50th birthday. Found bar to be relaxing with very polite and friendly staff. Would not hesitate to recommend to friends and family to stay at this hotel in the future

Title: cath trip
ReviewText: hotel has great location however renovation noise and early morning alarm spoilt the stay

Title: Marriott Stay - Great location.
ReviewText:
We stayed here for just one night at the weekend. The hotel's location overlooking the Thames is excellent.
The room we had had two double beds and was very substantial for the three of us.
We did not sample any food while we were there.
Our night's sleep was extremely comfortable.

Title: Estadia
ReviewText: No me gusto, por el valor que pague esperaba muchísimo más pues he estado en muchos hoteles y estoy muy inconforme

Title: schlechtes Frühstück
ReviewText: Sehr gutes 5* Hotel aber Frühstückbuffet wie 1*.

Title: Disappointing
ReviewText: Had high expectations of this hotel but was extremely disappointed. The room was pokey and the view of the Thames which I paid extra for was obscured on one side by the derrière of a statue. Not the vista I had planned for my wife on our anniversary!

Title: Very Satisfied
ReviewText: Everything was outstanding. The front desk personnel were friendly, the Concierge services staff we interfaced with were both very friendly and quite knowledgeable. We certainly appreciated the advice and help with arranging theater tickets. I also enjoyed the health club. Which I have to say was among the top health club facilities of any hotel or resort I have stayed at in the last 25 years. I believe this facility to be world class and probably rivals the gym I have held membership at for nearly 13 years. The Breakfast service was quite nice. Definitely top tier compared to other Marriot Hotels we have visited.

Title: Great location, friendly staff
ReviewText: Great location, friendly staff

Title: Old hotel undergoing much needed renovations
ReviewText: I would avoid the hotel until the renovations are complete or at the very least get a refurbished room. The lounge is terrible.

Title: Not a value of money
ReviewText: Poor service and club food

Title: Great hotel and location!
ReviewText: This hotel is in the center of everything! The only time we ever needed a taxi was when we were lazy or to get to/from the airport. I would highly recommend this hotel, and splurge a little on the suite overlooking Big Ben.....it is amazing. The staff here is great, and the hotel is basically sound proof. It was crazy, as soon as we walked in off the busy street to the front garden of the hotel it was SILENT...it was great. You cant go wrong with this hotel as it will still have the classic exterior but they are making a more sophisticated interior (10/16/15). They also have internet, spa, pool, room service, and everything else you need.

Title: Excellent location and staff
ReviewText: This is one of the best locations in London. The staff is outstanding and thoughtful. The hotel is undergoing a interior renovation and although I don't like the super modern feel for such a historic site the upgrade is very plush and well appointed.

Title: Overpriced in London
ReviewText:
The hotel is being remodeled, but right now it is overpriced for what you get, except for the location. Beware of the
No Cancellation policy when booking this hotel.

Title: Wonderful location if not long in the tooth
ReviewText: The hotel is in a perfect location but really needs a renovation. I saw evidence that they are working on this but it is not up to par with what I expect from a Marriott property. Bed was lumpy. Hallway outside of room had a very strong mildew smell. Loose tiles in bathroom and stains on countertop. Getting a cab can take up to 20 minutes as hotel entrance is out of the way so plan for it. Recommend eating out versus in restaurant. Will no doubt be wonderful once the renovation is complete but for now I have a hard time recommending it. Other London Marriott properties are in much better shape.

Title: Still has excellent service during the remodel !
ReviewText: Don't let the remodel put you off - the staff was excellent, and very helpful on our first trip to London. Do your due-diligence on your part as well. I heard comments about people not knowing a remodel was in progress. I ALWAYS contact hotels directly to see about issues such as this. The hotel addressed all my concerns without question, and we had a very enjoyable time at this hotel.

Title: Great hotel
ReviewText: Great hotel,great service!!

Title: London county hall stay
ReviewText: Our stay at the London County Hall was the best. It has an excellent location where we could see Big Ben in our room. It was next to the London Eye too. Very close to the transportation bus or tube. This is very important because it was easy to navigate around. The staff were warm and friendly. Assisted us in all our needs and helped us with all the directions to the places we want to go. We would definitely stay there again when we go back to London.

Title: Excellent
ReviewText: The hotel is an excellent central location, short walk to theaters, Parliment, Big Ben and we had a close up view of the "Eye" from our window. The concierge was very helpful and knowledgeable about theatre tickets and the plays. The doorman and staff were helpful in storing our bags on our last day and they always hailed taxis for us as well as arranging transportation to LHR.

Title: Great location
ReviewText: This hotel is winner for its great location.

Title: Fantastic experience!
ReviewText: Service by entire staff was outstanding. Restaurant and bar were topnotch. Only problem was major exterior construction underway, hindering some of the views. Still a great location in the midst of famous London landmarks.

Title: Amazing
ReviewText: Although under remodeling the experience was still amazing. The staff are pleasant and helpful whatever your needs. This is the hotel you want stay at while visiting London.

Title: Disappointed
ReviewText: Great location but hotel under renovation hence did not deliver to the price. Staff not as good as other Marriotts that we have stayed at - largely uninterested and unhelpful

Title: Lovely View, But Mind the Renovations!
ReviewText: Lovely hotel, decor, staff, view, etc., but the renovation noise is a bother, and the scaffolding outside the window ruins what is probably the best view in London: Parliament and Big Ben. Ask for a room with no scaffolding or wait until the renos are done! Or a room discount, as they are pricey.

Title: Best hotrl stay in London
ReviewText: I highly recommend this hotel to travelers that are willing to see the london Eye from their window

Title: Elite member
ReviewText:
Nothing was done for the construction noise?
Could have compensated in some other way...
If there was construction they should have notified in advance and lowered the room
rate instead of full expensive rate.

Title: Fun Family Hotel
ReviewText: Great for families with larger rooms than usual in Europe, and convenient location to London Eye, London Dungeon, and Aquarium as well as Big Ben (across the bridge).

Title: Concierges- are Excellent
ReviewText: Renovations of this resort at time of stay-Early morning noise- The most wonderful part of this resort were the two excellent concierges- they made this trip great- good information- ability to book restaurants and entertainment-Executive lounge was an issue- we had 2 rooms and they would only allow two people in the lounge- my husband is a high level platinum member- was not treated to that level.

Title: London Marriott county hall is wonderful!!
ReviewText: This hotel has a fantastic location - you can walk to so many sites & is great for Americans having things like wash cloths & 110 plugs that aren't in most London hotels!! The first night our room only had a king bed instead of 2 doubles as we had been guaranteed since there were 3 of us - but the next day we were moved to a large room with double beds. Everything else was wonderful. Rooms overall are very large for a London hotel!

Title: 喧騒からの解放
ReviewText: 人混みから一歩門内に入ると静寂が訪れます。スタッフの質も良好。リノベーションが済めば更に良質なホテルになるでしょう。

Title: Beautiful property! Great views!
ReviewText: We had the pleasure of staying at County Hall last week with 2 of our daughters (ages 11 and 13). The hotel is beautiful and located in a wonderful area for tweens. It's right next to the London Eye, and the walk along the river is filled with activity and restaurants. There is also a fantastic playground nearby that the girls loved playing on even though they were a little old for the "recommended playground age." The service was impeccable. Every member of the staff was willing to help with any need and very kind and engaging. Our room had 2 queens and seemed very large even by US standards. The view of Big Ben was really cool, especially at night.

Title: This property is undergoing major renovation and is not a first class Marriott property at this time
ReviewText: Think twice before you spend the money or points on this property. It is in need of and is under going major renovation and most rooms with a "view" only have a view of the construction scaffolding and the workers on the scaffolding. Many rooms have not been renovated and are in need of it and the exec lounge is a temporary facility

Title: Great location but missed the mark on room type reservation.
ReviewText: My reservation was made well in advance and was a 2 double bed room for 4 people. After checking in using the mobile app our room wasn't ready. After waiting several hours the room we were given had 1 King bed and a very uncomfortable fold out sofa. We were told that the view was nice. We couldn't even see out one of the windows because of a large concrete structure blocking the window. There were only two bath towels in the room even after I requested extra towels on my Marriott profile. Every day I had to call for two extra towels. With four people in the room you would think they would figure it out. Also, as a Platinum member I was told that I could have myself and one guest at a time attend the Executive breakfast each day. We did that for four days with me bringing first my wife and then each child separately. Then we were told that I could only bring one guest per day and would have to pay extra for the others. In addition, the rear entrance to the Gillray's lounge was very convenient when coming and going to the pier. However, we were constantly harassed by a security guard from one of the entrances near the construction that we couldn't stand by the rear entrance and take in the view and snap some photos. He said it was private property. I guess that doesn't apply to Marriott guests. I understand the property is undergoing remodeling but that is no excuse for these issues. I must compliment the staff who were very nice and the Concierge service staff was most excellent. I used all of my Marriott rewards points for this vacation and even paid for an extra night. Location was great but I feel disappointed with all the issues that could have been avoided like towels, breakfast and most of all the room. Next time I will look elsewhere.

Title: Don't be surprised by the scaffolding.
ReviewText: Very central to most London tourist attractions

Title: Excellent Hotel
ReviewText: We wanted to visit the main attractions in London and this Hotel is the perfect choice for location. We were able to walk to most attractions easily. The hotel's staff is welcoming, helpful and knowledgeable. The hotel rooms are well kept and clean and the restaurant, including the lounge, offers excellent service and choices.

Title: Loved County Hall
ReviewText: Loved this property! Would recommend to anyone - they are going through a renovation - but it was still lovely.

Title: Vacation in London
ReviewText: The County Hall building is gorgeous and offers many amenities. This hotel is located a few steps away from several underground and overground train stations. The concierge went out of his way to provide us with directions, maps and recomendations. We would love to return to this property in the future.

Title: Westminster stay...
ReviewText: Central location in a building steeped in history. Delightful staff throughout. Lovely food at breakfast and a full gym, pool and spa for some R & R.

Title: Mustafa Family
ReviewText: Hotel is in need of a major renovation of rooms and in general. Bathrooms were not working bedrooms were smelly and chairs were worn out and in general not worth what I paid .

Title: Small rooms for 3 people.
ReviewText: We had to complain to get a better room to acomodate 3 people.

Title: Nice place to stay while in London
ReviewText: Location! Location! Location! This is an excellent Marriott Hotel. Location is everything but so is the upkeep and staff at a Marriott. The Country Hall location is very well kept and the staff is extremely friendly. I would stay here again and recommend it to others on their stay.

Title: Great hotel
ReviewText: Perfect location for sightseeing in London.

Title: Great location in London
ReviewText: We were very pleased with hotel, staff, and executive lounge daily breakfast. Food was great, service very nice, and location in London perfect across river from Big Ben and Westminster Abbey

Title: Perfect for family visit to London
ReviewText:
Our family of four, celebrating our 25th Anniversary, had a wonderful week at the County Hall. We enjoyed a lovely view of the Thames and the Eye. The staff could not have been nicer or more accommodating.
The richly paneled entry and common areas were warm and inviting.
A great stay overall.

Title: Excellent
ReviewText: Was made to feel very welcome from check in to check out. The staff couldn't do enough for me which was great. As an elite Marriott member he room was upgraded and couldn't of been happier. Would recommend this hotel to anyone. Great location right on the river Thames and next to the London eye.

Title: Perfect location if you are vistiing London
ReviewText: This hotel is located next to the Westminster Bridge and the London Eye. The location puts you within walking distance of tourist sites, Waterloo train station, 2 underground (Tube) stations, sits along the Thames. Absolutely great place for a hotel.

Title: Communication Blunders
ReviewText: Hotel on ovations are part of travel. However, this was handled poorly from the start. Terrible communication and they didn't even honor the room discount they had said they were going to give me due to all the renovations. Overall this property I would not recommend anyone.

Title: London County Hall
ReviewText: This is the place to stay when visiting London as a tourist. The hotel is close to everything - Westminster Abbey, London Eye, Big Ben, etc. We also were able to walk to other areas easily - for example we walked to dinner in SoHo. The hotel is undergoing renovation and when that is completed the rooms will be amazing. The staff was pleasant and helpful - full of insider tips to make your visit to attractions a pleasure.

Title: Gran ubicación y esxcelentes instalaciones
ReviewText: Muy buen hotel, excelente ubicación, comida y servicio del personal

Title: London
ReviewText: Property was right in the middle of most tourist attractions and the train/subway station. It is located right in the hustle and bustle on London and you could step inside to a peaceful quiet time.

Title: You should stay here!!!
ReviewText: Great location!!! Nice rooms!

Title: Needs updating
ReviewText: We were very disappointed with the room accommodations especially the bed and linens. Was not worth the points we had to use to stay there. Have had better room accommodations, beds, and linens at Fairfields and Resident Inns. The fourth floor is being renovated and would assume they will be doing all of the rooms. Wait until that's completed.

Title: Great Location! Staff and Lounge needs work!
ReviewText: Overall Good experience, however staff in the executive lounge was difficult to work with.

Title: Great stay!
ReviewText: We stayed at the Marriott County Hall for a brief three day/two night trip to London. It is in a prime location, next to the London Eye. There are plenty of decent restaurants, and you are walking distance from the Tube and many sight-seeing activities. It is a great place to stay!

Title: Great Location!
ReviewText: This hotel is located near to everything you would want to see in London. The staff was especially helpful and accommodating.

Title: Excellent stay at a high quality hotel
ReviewText: First of all the location is excellent. Walking distance to many many points of interest. We had the Big Ben Exectuative room. This provided us the best view in all of London. The County Hall is a beautiful historic building with lots of character. The room was very spacious, which is unusual for European hotels. The room was also quiet which enabled us to get a restful sleep after long days of sightseeing. The staff was outstanding, especially the concierge. We had a great stay

Title: Location, Location!
ReviewText: This is a beautiful hotel located within easy walking distance of Buckingham Palace, Parliament, the London Eye and the Westminster business district. We loved the friendly staff, and the room was immaculate. Our room had a view of the Thames, Big Ben (very beautiful at night!), and the Eye. The breakfast buffet was delicious and featured a wide variety of traditional English breakfast fare!

Title: Pueden mejor
ReviewText: El hotel tiene una ubicación inmejorable, pero tiene muchas áreas de mejora, principalmente el departamento de conserjes. La comida es muy limitada y de regular calidad. Las habitaciones están muy bien y las camas, como en todo Marriott, son espectaculares. La planta física necesita renovarse, principalmente las alfombras que están viejas. El servicio de internet es muy bueno por su velocidad y cobertura.

Title: Excellent Stay
ReviewText: Outstanding accommodations and service

Title: Vacation in London
ReviewText:
excellent hotel with easy access to tube an bus to visit London and surrounding area
Highly recommend the Oyster pass for the tube if visiting for a week or more. Unlimited tube travel

Title: Bcp de deceptions
ReviewText: Piscine froide, 1 peignoir pour 3, impossibilité d annulation suite evenement familial, lit d appoint tres spartiate, personnel non polyglotte

Title: County Hall Marriott
ReviewText: Great hotel, great location and outstanding staff!

Title: markg
ReviewText: An excellent place to stay, the hotel staff was most gracious and very knowledgeable of the area and the accommodations. I highly recommend the Marriott County Hall.

Title: Perfect Place to Experience London
ReviewText: Staying a the Marriott County Hall is like going back to a more regal time in hotel hospitality. The location right on the river across from Big Ben and Westminster Hall is exquisite. Walking into the courtyard from the bustling city street is like coming into an oasis of calm. The doormen greet you properly and the front desk attendants take a personal interest in your needs. It is pricey - but I think it is worth the investment for a very nice London experience. We took the Underground from Heathrow to the Westminster stop and walked across the bridge. Very convenient - even with a few bags of luggage.

Title: overall
ReviewText:
check in was great given our travel time
staff was excellent
location is excellent for vistors to london

Title: recent stay
ReviewText: Beautiful old hotel in a great area of london with great views . Beds in the room were not comfortable and request to remove feather bedding from the room prior to our arrival caused some discomfort. Acces to the pool and spa facilities was not explained and confusing. The staff in the pool and gym area was not interested in helping out. Front desk and Doorman were excellent.

Title: Good, but bar/lounge is not a positive experience.
ReviewText: overall nice due to location and great hotel staff, other than the bar lounge. Should not have to pay extra for internet access.

Title: Treated like Royalty
ReviewText: One of the best hotel experience every.

Title: feedback
ReviewText: I Nice hotel, very vell located, With very Nice staff

Title: London stay
ReviewText: Lovely hotel. Great location

Title: 还不错
ReviewText: 地理位置好，环境不错，但食物一般般...................

Title: Marriott county hall london
ReviewText: Excellent on almost all levels. Felt there needed to be an attendent available n the executive lounge periodically to replenish some supplies.

Title: Great stay
ReviewText: Great hotel. Perfect location in London. Close to many attractions. Great staff. Fantastic view of Thames River, Big Ben and Parliament from the room.

Title: Good view but tired exterior & interior
ReviewText: Highly not recommended. Very Long hallway to rooms. Tired rooms, unwelcome staffs & service.

Title: London Holiday
ReviewText: The hotel was fantastic. It was in an ideal location, very close to everything. The staff could not have been better. Our holiday got off to a great start beginning with the staff!!. We definitely will be back.

Title: First rate hotel - INCREDIBLE staff - phenomenal location
ReviewText: Can't say enough about our stay at Marriott London County Hall. Great location for a vacation visitor (close to Westminster and Waterloo Tube stops); unbelievably professional and courteous staff made our week-long stay a complete success!

Title: Excellent stay
ReviewText: The title says it all. We would stay at County Hall for preference every time. Good location, service, food and beverages.

Title: weekend stay
ReviewText: Nice experience would recommend for a nice quite stay

Title: Great place to stay
ReviewText: Had a wonderful time hotel felt like home

Title: Classy and Convenient
ReviewText: The hotel is located at a very convenient location; easily reachable and quick access to key tourist spots. Well maintained and classy hotel

Title: Amazing views and sport amenities!
ReviewText: I spend 90% of my working time in hotels. Last 2 months I've spent in London across different Marriotts and I have to say that this one has three things really outstanding from the rest of Marriotts: Amazing window view at the Thames river, The London Eye and The Big Ben all at once, great, full size swimming-pool with hot yoga classes available upon booking, big gym with the size of regular fitness centre!

Title: London Marriott Hotel County Hall
ReviewText:
Excellent hotel with a wonderful view of Big Ben and Parliament. The staff made sure our every need was met without being intrusive.
The room was spacious and had ample area for workspace, which is not typical of European hotels. The lounge space was large and offered a wonderful selection of amenities.

Title: stay
ReviewText: very nice hotel although a little dated now, river view was not so much a river view more of the London eye. avoid room 215 if your looking for a romantic view or a view at all

Title: Excellent experience
ReviewText: An awesome and pleasure able stay at this Marriott.

Title: New Years Eve
ReviewText: Great location for visiting London

Title: V good
ReviewText: Not exceptional but solid.

Title: Super Location / Excellent Hotel
ReviewText:
We enjoyed our stay immensely. The hotel is right next to the London Eye and across the Westminster Bridge from Big Ben and the Houses of Parliament. There are two Tube stations withing very closed walking distance (Westminster and Waterloo). As far as location goes, this hotel is in the middle of it all.

The hotel staff were excellent! Everyone was nice and courteous; always willing to help/answer questions.

Our room was particularly nice, with a view of the Thames, London Eye and Big Ben/Houses of Parliament.

I would recommend this hotel to anyone, although it's on the high end as far as price goes.

Title: County Hall
ReviewText: Excellent location, friendly staff, modern rooms but try to request rooms closer to the lift as I was in room 202 which is quite a walk from the lifts.

Title: A night in London
ReviewText:
We decided to stay overnight in London, following a visit to the Opera.

Having worked in London for more than 20 years prior to retirement I chose the Marriott County Hall for our stay.

It proved to be the right decision.

Title: Good stay.....
ReviewText: Good location for trip to see Matilda. children loved it. Rooms and service good.

Title: Magic in London
ReviewText: We were treated to a remarkable experience at the London Marriott County Hall over Thanksgiving weekend. The location and view speak for themselves, but the staff and facility were truly above and beyond and expectations we could imagine. The professionalism, friendliness, and sincere desire to please were evident in every aspect of our stay - from bellman to front desk to bartender to housekeeping. Everyone and everything was designed to amplify our positive experience of London. Our room was a newly renovated balcony room overlooking Big Ben and the Eye...stunning. The room was stylish, well-appointed and more than adequate for our traveling needs (electrical outlets of all variety, post cards, etc). The fitness center was huge with modern equipment and classes. The pool was more suited for lap swimming, but was large and plenty of fun for our six-year old. The breakfast was out of this world; food quality and service was outstanding - fresh food and helpful attitudes all around. The concierge was fantastic, anticipating our unique sightseeing needs and desires. The front desk staff helped us get acquanted with our room and refresh lost room keys, escorting us on more than one occassion to ensure satisfaction. This experience has spoiled us to London --- we will likely never have another experience like this one. If we do, it will only be a result of staying at the Marriott County Hall again.

Title: FABULOUS
ReviewText: For those who enjoy visiting London, I recommend the stay at the Marriott Hotel County Hall as a must. Ask for a view of the Thames.

Title: This was an Outstanding Choice
ReviewText: Don't hesitate to choose this for your London Vacation. Marriott Rewards Members are thought of highly at this hotel. The staff is second to none and there is No Better Area to stay. I can't wait to go back and I will be planning my next stay here soon. Large rooms, great views, great location, the best hotel staff in the world! Special thanks to everyone who made my granddaughter and my vacation the best ever!

Title: Terrible Executive Lounge
ReviewText: Having worked hard to get the Gold/Platinum level to enjoy the benefits of Marriott - we specifically chose this Cat 9 hotel based on its location and its likelihood that it would have a quality lounge. I have stayed at a number of properties around the world where there were sweeping views of the city, good quality drinks and food. The lounge at this Cat 9 hotel was a windowless space that could accommodate about 10 people. "Canapes" were served from 1800-1930 - we got there at 1945 and were shooed away "oh the drinks were stopped at 1930"... in other locations the bar was thriving and did not stop service until late at night, here it was empty at 1930 - when business people would be back from their meetings. It was an embarrassment for a Cat 9 hotel. The rest of the hotel was fitting what I'd expect but the executive lounge was terrible.

Title: Best View in London
ReviewText: If you want to experience quintessential London, a view of Big Ben and Parliament from your hotel room is a wonderful way to do so. The location is also very convenient to the London Tube making it easy to get around London during your stay.

Title: good spot
ReviewText: Friendly staffs, good location either for biz or leisure.

Title: Great western location
ReviewText: Right in the middle of everything at the London eye and Big Ben area.

Title: County hall
ReviewText: People were great. My first room had a lumpy mattress and the cooling system didn't do much cooling. Thank god they gave me a different room after two nights. The second room was bigger, had a nice view of the river and Big Ben and the air seemed to work better.

Title: From Bermuda
ReviewText:
Overall I had an excellent stay as normal at the London Marriott County Hall

Check in was excellent - had to wait for room - not a big problem

Check in agent assisted with bags for storage and provided info on how to travel to Arsenal Club (had to buy Christmas gifts)

Return to hotel - agent was professional

Went to Bar and had excellent service

Room - was clean and big (I like a big room) - decorated with red petals -- travel plans had changed but I had a good time

Dinner - good service and food was excellent -- I love the cauliflower and of course the steak was excellent

Bed was very comfortable

Use of the fitness / exercise room was good and clean

Overall my stay was excellent

Title: It really is that close
ReviewText:
Telephoto lenses can make hotels appear to be closer to attractions than they really are. Not so with this hotel. It is literally across the bridge from Big Ben, Westminster Abbey, and a tube station that can take you anywhere in London.

Loved the hotel restaurant. It was a bit expensive, but it is smack in the middle of London. You pay for convenience. For me, I ate there because I arrived late on the train and it was the only thing guaranteed to be open that late. Overall, I didn't find the steaks as tasty as they are in the USA, but I found that to be the case anywhere I had steak in England. The waiters were very helpful. They are slower than in the USA, but that only bothered me because I was traveling alone - no one to converse with.

The room was great - had everything I could want in a room. The beds were a bit bouncy for my tastes, but I didn't have any issues sleeping.

Oh, bonus - this is one of the Marriotts where if you have the phone app you can check in ahead of time.

Title: Excellent
ReviewText: Great location and history of building...much walking..might consider letting those old leg folks know...Staff was personal and friendly...

Title: Great stay
ReviewText: Best Hotel I've stayed at. The staff were absolutely first class.

Title: Time in London
ReviewText: Location and service made our trip to London very pleasurable

Title: london overnight
ReviewText: great location and hotel that urgently needs to up its game

Title: Anniversary Stay.
ReviewText: We had the most amazing stay at the marriott county hall. It is one of the most comfortable hotels I have stayed at. Fantastic service. Beautiful views from the Big Ben room.

Title: Decor Poor & Renovation Needed
ReviewText: This hotel is in dire need of renovation. It is not what I have come to expect with JW Marriott

Title: Great location, excellent staff.
ReviewText:
We enjoyed our stay here, the staff were excellent and the location is unbeatable. The hotel itself is beginning to show signs of wear. Also we were guaranteed a view of the river but this was very limited and unless you stood up and leaned out you couldn't see much river at all! (Room 212).
Overall it was a good experience and we will come back.

Title: cut me deep
ReviewText: Not recommended, nothing special for the price you cannot get elsewhere for cheaper.

Title: Potential to be excellent, however attention to detail & housekeeping lets it down
ReviewText: This is a 5 star hotel in a fantastic location, however the general upkeep of the hotel & cleanliness of the guest room, makes it in my opinion, unworthy of 5 star status.

Title: Night away with family
ReviewText: A good hotel when staying in London

Title: 2 day visit
ReviewText: Location, location, location. Can't fault its position in London for easy access to major places of interest. Quality of the staff and hotel was refreshing, and will look to visit again.

Title: worst marriott hotel I have stayed in
ReviewText:
The attitude of the staff makes this hotel the worst I have stayed in. I had my family and in consideration of other residents we took room service we stayed in the executive suite the most expensive in the hotel .
The food was delivered to the room and was never right. We on one day asked for someone to come to the room as the food was inappropriate and we got insulted by saying they thought r he food was right and it was just our perception. We had to call the duty manager who looked and realised the food was not right and it was taken back.
If they can treat people living in executive suite like that then how would they treat people living in regular rooms

Title: Irene
ReviewText: Overall really good and will recommend to friends

Title: London
ReviewText: Great location, good food, expensive internet

Title: Nice summer trip!
ReviewText: Good location for a variety of London sites--easy to get to underground. Area on the bridge and street was often crowded and noisy--so it was SO nice to walk into County Hall. Rooms with a river view --beautiful. The afternoon tea was also great with an excellent view of Big Ben.

Title: Luxury hotel with amazing staff
ReviewText: To manage the number of guests arriving and departing, there needs to be some thought at creating a larger space for the staff to work and guests to wait. This is understandably difficult with the nature and age of this hotel and under the circumstances the staff are managing to try their very best, however as a guest the wait can be long. That aside, the location of this hotel places it at the top of the list for staying in London. Walking distance from many of the things tourists want to visit with Big Ben and the river Thames as a back drop. Wow! A very unique hotel, well worth a visit and value for money.

Title: Review
ReviewText:
Point for improvement:
Access to wi-fi should be free once it is a basic requirement
There is only one lift lobby and some room are very far from it (for a hotel so big there should be more than one lift)

Title: Not worth the money
ReviewText: Nice location overlooking the Thames nice looking hotel. Pity staff not that helpful and staff shocked when you complain about the service.

Title: Excellent Location
ReviewText: The location of the hotel is fantastic for being close to attractions / activities, food, and public transportation. Much more quiet than would have anticipated based on the activity surrounding. Great staff and comfortable rooms make this location a winner.

Title: Excellent for holidays
ReviewText: Dora in the Lobby did our holiday very exceptional

Title: Luxury treat
ReviewText: Marriot provided me and my wife with the luxury experience we wanted to celebrate a birthday. The high standards of service and ambiance was lovely, totally recommend it.

Title: Super room
ReviewText: We were given a fantastic room with a great view of the river and the houses of Parliament. The bed was very comfortable and the room quiet. Our breakfast was very enjoyable. The staff were without exception cheerful and on the occasion we needed some assistance very helpful. Marriott County Hall is now our favourite London hotel

Title: Weekend stay
ReviewText: Everything about the Marriott County Hall is excellent. Super service from all the staff and location is ideal.

Title: Marriott
ReviewText: Excellent service with a beautiful room. Very close to great London sights.

Title: Great choice!
ReviewText: I live in the UK and had guests visiting who wished to stay in London for two nights. I selected County Hall hotel because of the location - without every stepping inside the hotel. It was a good decision. The ambience was wonderful and the staff practically perfect! Food was excellent - and the drinks too. Location can't be beat for walking around London - and the view is what you come to London to see! It is a bit of a walk to the tube station - but you are crossing the River Thames and walking toward Big Ben! That can't be a bad thing, can it? I will stay there again for sure.

Title: London Marriott Conty Hall is Excellent!
ReviewText: My family and I recently stayed at the London Marriott County Hall while on a two week trip to England, and we couldn't have been happier with our choice. The location is simply perfect for sightseeing - the best the city has to offer. The staff is outstanding - I don't think I've had better service at any other Marriott property anywhere. The food is fabulous - breakfasts are wonderful and generous and the dinner restaurant/bar has an incredible range of choices (and the food was so good we ended up eating there every night because we knew it would be hard to beat elsewhere). Highly, highly recommend this hotel!

Title: Great hotel, great location, but a few complaints
ReviewText: Clean, comfortable, spacious, beautiful. But ice machine located on only one floor and MILES walk from room, service very slow-getting check, porter to pick up bags, front desk informing room ready.

Title: Excellent service and staff
ReviewText:
Prior to my visit I emailed a very polite member of staff Edison who tendered to every need I requested for a room upgrade and specific details and everything was done to perfect standard. On arrival we met with Edison who showed us around the hotel and up to our room, very friendly member of staff and was a pleasure to meet him!
Phenomenal views of the river thames and big ben from our Big Ben suite and the room is perfect as usual! Fantastic stay at this hotel and will definitely be returning very soon. Decor and the building of the hotel is outstanding and picture perfect whether day or night and perfectly located for London Eye and other local landmarks.
Easily my favourite Marriott hotel!

Title: Would recommend
ReviewText: Excellent central location , professional, friendly & helpful staff, breakfast fantastic

Title: Home away from home
ReviewText: London Marriott County Hall is, far and away, the best hotel experience I've had in many years of business travel to London. I have stayed there on several occasions mostly for work (and on one occasion for leisure) and I find the staff exhibits an uncanny and usual sensitivity to my needs in the light of the particular purpose of my trip to London. I am always made to feel special when I stay there. It is high quality service at a reasonable price - definitely value for money! Whenever I travel to London now, it is my home away from home. I recommend this hotel highly.

Title: Very good location and a nice building
ReviewText:
We enjoyed 4 nights at the hotel. 3 persons in a comfortable large room overlooking the river and London Eye. Kind staff and nice restaurant. The view from the bar is one of the best in London.
Small things and misstakes did occure that one would not expect in a 5-star hotel. However it did not spoil my stay in anyway (just irritated slightly)
